CHAPTER 99 225
such agency or agencies as the Director may approve , a
statistical report showing its underwriting experience by
classes in form approved by the Director . No insurer shall
be required to record or report its experience on a classifica-
tion plan that is inconsistent with the rating plan filed and
used by it . The compilations of such reports by such agency
or agencies shall be made available subject to reasonable
rules promulgated by the Director to insurers and rating
organizations .
(e ) Every rating bureau or other insurer engaged in
making rates or estimates for fire insurance on property in
this State shall inspect every risk specifically rated by it
upon schedule , except on inland marine insurance and insur-
ance on motor vehicles , and make a written survey of the
risk , which shall be filed as a permanent record in the office
of the bureau or insurer . Upon the making of any survey ,
the bureau shall promptly furnish to the owner upon request
a statement of the rate and of the removable defects found ,
with suggestions for improvements and the removing of the
defects . A copy of the survey shall be furnished to the in-
sured or his authorized agent upon request .
Sec . 4- RATE FILINGS : USE OF RATES
Rates may be used when promulgated in accordance with
the following provisions :
(a) Every authorized insurer shall file with the Di-
rector , except as to classes of inland marine insurance for
which class rates or rating plans are not customarily fixed
by rating bureaus , every manual , minimum , class rate , rat-
ing schedule or rating plan and every other rating rule, and
every modification of any of the foregoing which it proposes
to use and such other information concerning rates made or
used by it as may reasonably be required . Every such filing
shall indicate the character and extent of the coverage con-
templated , and shall state the proposed effective date thereof .
A filing shall be open to public inspection after the fil-
ing becomes effective . Where specific inland marine rates
on risks specially rated are made by a rating organization
such rates shall also be filed .
(b) The Director shall review filings as soon as reason-
ably possible after they have been made in order to deter-
mine whether they meet the requirements of this Act .

Finally, it mandates that all insurance contracts and policies must conform to the act's provisions.

Sec . 5- DISAPPROVAL OF FILINGS : REMOVAL

( a ) If within the ten day period or extension thereof ,
as provided in sub-paragraph ( c ) of Section 4, the Director
finds that a filing does not meet the requirements of this
Act , he shall send to the insurer or rating organization which
made such filing written notice of disapproval of such filing
specifying therein in what respects he finds such filing fails
to meet the requirements of this Act , and stating that such
filing shall not become effective .
(b ) If at any time thereafter the Director finds that an
effective filing does not meet the requirements of this Act ,
he may, after a hearing held on not less than twenty ( 20 )
days ' written notice , specifying the matters to be considered
at such hearing , to every insurer and rating organization
which made such filing, issue an order specifying in what
respects he finds that such filing fails to meet the require-
ments of this Act , and stating when , within a reasonable
period thereafter , such filing shall be deemed no longer ef-
fective . Said order shall not affect any contract or policy
made or issued prior to the expiration of the period set
forth in said order . However , if at any time the Director
finds that an unfair discrimination exists in the application
of a filing to an individual risk he may , after a hearing held
on like notice to the insurer affected and to any rating or-
ganization which made such filing issue an order that such
discrimination be removed . Copies of any such order shall
be sent to every such insurer and rating organization .

ified in its application and shall file therewith ( 1 ) a copy of
its constitution , its articles of agreement or association or its
certificate of incorporation , and of its by-laws and rules gov-
erning the conduct of its business , ( 2 ) a list of its members
and subscribers , (3 ) the name and address of a resident of

process affecting such rating organization may be served
and ( 4 ) a statement of its qualification as a rating organiza-

classification.title: An Act relating to rating organizations
classification.summary: This act outlines the requirements and procedures for licensing and regulating rating organizations in Kentucky. It details the qualifications for a rating organization, the process for granting or denying licenses, and the conditions under which licenses can be revoked. It also mandates that rating organizations must provide their services without discrimination to both members and subscribers, and establishes a review process by the Director for any disputes regarding subscriber admission or rules. The act also requires prompt notification to the Director of any changes in membership, constitution, or by-laws.

The Director may make reasonable rules and regulations

Sec . 10- FALSE OR MISLEADING INFORMATION
No person or organization shall knowingly give false or
misleading information to the Director or to any statistical
agency designated by the Director , or to any rating organiza-
tion , or to any insurer which will in any way affect the rates
or premiums chargeable under this Act . A violation of this
section shall subject the one guilty of such violation to the
penalties provided for in Section 11 of this Act .
Sec . 11- PENALTIES
Any person or organization who wilfully violates any
provision of this Act shall, upon conviction , be fined not less
than Twenty -five Dollars ( $25.00 ) nor more than Five Hun-
dred Dollars ( $ 500.00 ) ; such penalties may be in addition to
any other penalty provided by law.
The Director may suspend the license of any rating or-
ganization or insurer which fails to comply with an order of
the Director within the time limited by such order , or any ex-
tension thereof which the Director may grant . The Director
shall not suspend the license of any rating organization or
insurer for failure to comply with an order until the time
prescribed for an appeal therefrom has expired or, if an ap-
peal has been taken , until such order has been affirmed . The
Director may determine when a suspension of license shall
become effective and it shall remain in effect for the period
fixed by him, unless he modifies or rescinds such suspension ,
or until the order upon which such suspension is based is
modified , rescinded or reversed .
No penalty shall be imposed and no license shall be
suspended or revoked except upon a written order of the
Director , stating his findings , made after a hearing held
upon not less than ten days ' ( 10 ) written notice to such per-
son or organization specifying the alleged violation .

REVIEW

232 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
any order or decision of the Director made without a hearing
may , within thirty days ( 30 ) after notice of the order to the
insurer or organization , make written request to the Direc-
tor for a hearing thereon . The Director shall hear such
party or parties within twenty ( 20 ) days after receipt of such
request and shall give not less than ten ( 10 ) days ' written
notice of the time and place of the hearing . Within fifteen
( 15 ) days after such hearing the Director shall affirm , re-
verse or modify his previous action , specifying his reasons
therefor . Pending such hearing and decision thereon the Di-
rector may suspend or postpone the effective date of his pre-
vious action .
(b) Nothing contained in this Act shall require the ob-
servance at any hearing of formal rules of pleading or
evidence .
(c ) Any order or decision of the Director shall be sub-
ject to review by appeal to the Franklin Circuit Court at the
instance of any party in interest, who shall, within twenty
( 20 ) days after such order or decision has become final,
file a petition for review in said Court . The petition shall
state fully the grounds upon which a review is sought , assign
all errors relied on and be verified by the petitioner , who
shall furnish copies of the petition to the respondents at
the time of filing . Summons shall issue , upon the petition ,
directing the adverse party to file answer within fifteen ( 15 )
days after service thereof , and directing the Director to cer-
tify to the Court the complete record of the proceedings
upon which such order or decision is based .
No new or additional evidence may be introduced in the
Circuit Court except as to fraud or misconduct of some per-
son engaged in the administration of this Act and affecting
the order or decision appealed from , but the Court shall
otherwise hear the cause upon the record as certified by the
Director . Such appeal shall not be limited to questions of
law . The Court shall determine whether the appeal shall
operate as a stay of any such order or decision of the Di-
rector . The Court may , in disposing of the issue before it ,
affirm , modify , or reverse the order or decision of the Di-
rector in whole or in part , or remand the action to the Di-
rector for further proceedings in accordance with the direc-
tion of the Court .

of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , and insurers operating
on the Lloyd's Plan , regulated by Sections 302.150 to 302.230 ,
inclusive of the Kentucky Revised Statutes . This Act shall
not apply to assessment or co -operative companies organized
or operating under Chapter 299 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-

Sec . 3- MAKING OF RATES
(a) All rates shall be made in accordance with the fol-
lowing provisions :
1-Due consideration shall be given to past and prospec-
tive loss experience within and outside the State , to catas-
trophe hazards , if any , to a reasonable margin for profit and
contingencies , to dividends , savings or unabsorbed premium
deposits allowed or returned by insurers to their policyhold-
ers , members or subscribers , and to all other relevant factors
within and outside the State ;
2 The systems of expense provisions included in the
rates for use by any insurer or group of insurers may differ
from those of other insurers or groups of insurers to reflect
the requirements of the operating methods of any such in-
surer or group with respect to any kind of insurance , or with
respect to any subdivision or combination thereof for which
subdivision or combination separate expense provisions are
applicable ;
3-Risks may be grouped by classifications for the estab-
lishment of rates and minimum premiums . Classification
rates may be modified to produce rates for individual risks
in accordance with rating plans which establish standards for
measuring variations in hazards or expense provisions , or
both ;
4-Rates shall be reasonable , adequate and not unfairly
discriminatory .
(b ) Except to the extent necessary to meet the provi-
sions of Subdivision 4 of Subsection ( a ) of this Section , uni-
formity among insurers in any matters within the scope of this
Section is neither required nor prohibited .
Sec . 4-FILING OF RATES AND RATING INFORMA-
TION
( a ) Every insurer shall file with the Director every
manual of classifiscations , rules and rates , every rating plan
and every modification of any of the foregoing which it pro-

242 ACTS OF THE GENERAL Assembly
Sec . 10-RATE ADMINISTRATION
(a) Recording and Reporting of Loss and Countrywide
Expense Experience .
The Director shall promulgate reasonable rules and sta-
tistical plans , reasonably adapted to each of the rating sys-
tems on file with him, which may be modified from time to
time and which shall be used thereafter by each insurer in
the recording and reporting of its loss and countrywide ex-
pense experience , in order that the experience of all insurers
may be made available at least biennially in such form and de-
tail as may be necessary to aid him in determining whether
rating systems comply with the standards set forth in Section
3. In promulgating such rules and plans , the Director shall
give due consideration to the rating systems on file with him
and , in order that such rules and plans may be as uniform as
is practicable among the several states , to the rules and to the
form of the plans used for such rating systems in other
States . No insurer shall be required to record or report its
loss experience on a classification basis that is inconsistent
with the rating system filed by it . The Director may desig-
nate one or more rating organizations or other agencies to
assist him in gathering such experience and making compila-
tions thereof , and such compilations shall be made available ,
subject to reasonable rules promulgated by the Director , to
insurers and rating organizations .
(b) Interchange of Rating Plan Data .
Reasonable rules and plans may be promulgated by the
Director for the interchange of loss experience necessary for
the application of rating plans .
(c) Consultation with Other States .
In order to further uniform administration of rating
laws, the Director and every insurer and rating organization
may exchange information and experience data with insur-
ance supervisory officials , insurers and rating organizations
in other states and may consult and cooperate with them with
respect to rate making and the application of rating systems .
(d) Rules and Regulations .
The Director may make reasonable rules and regulations
necessary to effect the purposes of this Act .

No person or organization shall knowingly give false or

agency designated by the Director , to any rating organization ,
or to any insurer , which will in any way affect the rates or
premiums chargeable under this Act . A violation of this Sec-

ties provided in Section 13 of this Act .

ingly charge , demand or receive a premium for any policy
of insurance except in accordance with the applicable filing
on file with the Director . No insurer or employee thereof ,
and no agent shall pay , allow , or give , or offer to pay , allow ,
or give , directly or indirectly , as an inducement to insurance ,
or after insurance has been effected , any rebate , discount ,
abatement , credit or reduction of the premium named in a
policy of insurance , or any special favor or advantage in the
dividends or other benefits to accrue thereon , or any valuable
consideration or inducement whatever , not specified in the
policy of insurance , except to the extent provided for in such
applicable filing . No insured named in a policy of insurance ,
nor any employee of such insured shall knowingly receive or
accept , directly or indirectly , any such rebate , discount , abate-
ment or reduction of premium , or any special favor or advan-
tage or valuable consideration or inducement . Nothing herein
contained shall be construed as prohibiting the payment of
commissions or other compensation to duly licensed agents
and brokers , nor as prohibiting any participating insurer from
distributing to its policyholders dividends, savings or the un-
used or unabsorbed portion of premiums and premium de-
posits .
As used in this Section the word " insurance " includes
suretyship and the word " policy " includes bond .
Sec . 13- PENALTIES
Any person or organization who wilfully violates any
provision of this Act shall, upon conviction , be fined not less
than Twenty -Five Dollars ( $ 25.00 ) nor more than Five Hun-
dred Dollars ( $ 500.00 ) . Such penalty may be in addition to
any other penalty provided by law .

244 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
The Director may suspend the license of any rating
organization or insurer which fails to comply with an order
of the Director within the time limited by such order , or any
extension thereof which the Director may grant . The Director
shall not suspend the license of any rating organization or
insurer for failure to comply with an order until the time
prescribed for an appeal therefrom has expired or , if an ap-
peal has been taken , until such order has been affirmed . The
Director may determine when a suspension of license shall
become effective and it shall remain in effect for the period
fixed by him , unless he modifies or rescinds such suspension ,
or until the order upon which such suspension is based is
modified , rescinded or reversed . No penalty shall be imposed
and no license shall be suspended or revoked except upon a
written order of the Director , stating his findings , made after
a hearing held upon not less than ten days ' written notice to
such person or organization specifying the alleged violation .
Sec . 14 HEARING PROCEDURE AND JUDICIAL
REVIEW
(a ) Any insurer or rating organization aggrieved by
any order or decision of the Director made without a hearing ,
may , within thirty days after notice of the order to the in-
surer or organization , make written request to the Director
for a hearing thereon . The Director shall hear such party
or parties within twenty days after receipt of such request
and shall give not less than ten days ' written notice of the
time and place of the hearing . Within fifteen days after such
hearing the Director shall affirm , reverse or modify his previ-
ous action , specifying his reasons therefor . Pending such
hearing and decision thereon the Director may suspend or
postpone the effective date of his previous action .
(b) Nothing contained in this Act shall require the ob-
servance at any hearing of formal rules of pleading or evi-
dence .
(c ) Any order or decision of the Director shall be sub-
ject to review by appeal to the Franklin Circuit Court at the
instance of any party in interest , who shall, within twenty
days after such order or decision has become final , file a peti-
tion for review in said court . The petition shall state fully
the grounds upon which a review is sought , assign all errors
relied on and be verified by the petitioner, who shall furnish

copies of the petition to the respondents at the time of filing .
Summons shall issue , upon the petition , directing the adverse

plete record of the proceedings upon which such order or de-

No new or additional evidence may be introduced in the
circuit court except as to fraud or misconduct of some person
engaged in the administration of this Act and affecting the
order or decision appealed from , but the court shall otherwise
hear the cause upon the record as certified by the Director .
Such appeal shall not be limited to questions of law . The court
shall determine whether the appeal shall operate as a stay of
any such order or decision of the Director . The court may , in
disposing of the issue before it , affirm , modify , or reverse the
order or decision of the Director in whole or in part , or re-
mand the action to the Director for further proceedings in
accordance with the direction of the Court .
Sec . 15- LAWS AMENDED AND REPEALED
Sub-section ( 3 ) of Section 302.080 of the Kentucky Re-
vised Statutes is amended to read as follows :
" (3) Except as provided in KRS 136.370 , 296.320 ,
302.080 to 302.140 and in this Act no law of this State relat-
ing to insurance shall apply to the exchange of indemnity con-
tracts as regulated by KRS 302.080 to 302.140 ."
Section 342.385 and subsection ( 2 ) of Section 342.345
KRS hereby are repealed and all other laws or parts of laws
in conflict or inconsistent with the provisions of this Act are
hereby repealed .
Sec . 16- CONSTITUTIONALITY
If any Section , Sub - section , Sub -division , paragraph
sentence or clause of this Act is held invalid or unconstitu-
tional, such decision shall not affect the remaining portions
of this Act .
Sec . 17- EFFECTIVE DATE
This Act shall take effect October 1 , 1947 .
Passed and enrolled March 21 , approved March 23 , 1946 .

classification.summary: This act authorizes Fiscal Courts in Kentucky counties to establish a dedicated 'land and building fund'. This fund can be used to acquire land, erect, maintain, improve, and reconstruct public buildings for county use. The Fiscal Court can levy a tax to contribute to this fund, which must be kept separate from the general revenue fund. Monies in the fund can be invested in U.S. Government obligations and the Fiscal Court can accept gifts or devises for the fund.

charged , the District shall nevertheless continue to function

Section 1. Cities of the fourth class in this Common-

ing or widening any creek or creeks running through the cor-

thereof and consequent damage to any street or alley or parts.
of any street or alley therein or the health of its inhabitants ;

city by purchase , the legislative body of such city may adopt
an ordinance declaring the necessity for the acquisition of
such land for any of the above stated purposes and authoriz-

the property .

accordance with Sections 416.010 to 416.080 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes .

CHAPTER 108 269
Clerk of Barren County , one in the office of the County Clerk
of Hart County, and one in the office of the County Clerk of
Edmonson County .
Passed and enrolled March 12 , 1946 , not approved .
STATEMENT BY THE GOVERNOR
RE : HOUSE BILL NO . 388
On March 20 , 1944 , I approved a joint Resolution creating a Com-
mission for the purpose of making an adequate and suitable survey of
the boundary lines between the Counties of Barren , Hart and Edmonson
and determining the true location of such boundary lines .
Section 3 of the Resolution defined the authority of the Commission ,
and plainly empowered it to "determine and report " what it finds to be
the true boundary line . The Commission performed its duty and made
a report showing what it had found and determined to be the true
boundary line .
The Legislature in 1946 received the report and permitted exceptions
to be filed by two of the members of the Commisson .
The House Bill No. 388 recited the facts and proceeded to sustain the
exceptions by naming the owners of certain tracts of land which were
excluded and declared to be in Hart County . This presents a judicial
question as to the effect and construction of the act .
It seems to me that I should let this Act take its course without at-
tempting to decide the question . My decision would not settle the
argument .
If these lands referred to are not in Hart County , the act of the legis-
lature can not effectively declare them in that County . It will be necessary
for the Court to ascertain and locate the true boundary line , and until
that is done the argument will not be settled . Under the Constitution ,
territory cannot be stricken from a Couny without submitting the ques-
tion to a vote . The true boundary exists in fact , and that fact must be
conclusively and finally determined by a court .
Hence , I neither approve nor disapprove House Bill No. 388.
Frankfort , Kentucky
March 28 , 1946
SIMEON WILLIS,
Governor

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 90.120 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is hereby amended and as amended reads as follows :
(1 ) There shall be created and established in cities of
the first class a Civil Service Board to be known as .....
Civil Service Board . Said Board shall consist of five members
of whom four shall be appointed by the mayor and the mayor
shall be the fifth member ex officio . Any Civil Service Board
heretofore established pursuant to KRS 90.110 to 90.240 shall
be enlarged to accord with the provisions of this section . The
mayor, as ex officio member , shall not have the right to vote
except when necessary to break a tie . The appointive mem-
bers shall be selected equally from the two political parties
casting the highest and next highest number of votes at the
last preceding general election . All members shall be ap-
pointed for a four year term . In any city in which there has
been heretofore established a Civil Service Board pursuant
to KRS 90.110 to 90.240 the mayor shall appoint the additional
appointive member herein provided for , for a term commenc-
ing July 1 , 1946 , and other appointments by the mayor of such
a city to said Board shall be made at the conclusion of the term
of the present incumbents and shall be made in such a way as
to have said Board conform as soon as practicable to the equal
division between political parties . Vacancies created by death ,
resignation and otherwise shall be filled for the unexpired
portion of the term only . Any member of the Board appointed
according to the provisions of this section shall be eligible for
reappointment unless said reappointment would offend the
equal political division of said Board .
(2 ) No member of the said Board shall be eligible for
any appointive office of the city while a member of said
Board , or for one year thereafter . If a member of the Board
becomes a candidate for an elective office , he thereby vacates
his position as a member of the Board .
(3 ) Any member of the Board may be removed by the
mayor , for cause , after hearing by the mayor, and after at:
least ten days notice in writing shall have been given to the
member , which notice shall contain a copy of charges pre--

STATEMENT ACCOMPANYING APPROVAL OF
HOUSE BILL NO . 405
TO THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY AND WHOM IT MAY CONCERN :
This bill makes ( a ) several changes in the teachers' retirement law ,
and (b ) makes an appropriation of $ 106,000 for the purpose of making
the changes effective .
(a ) Under the present law, the maximum salary on which teachers'
contributions may be based is $ 2000 , all salaries above $2000 being treated
as if they were only $ 2000 . Retirement allowances are based on the
salary received during the five years preceding retirement . Under this
bill the maximum salary on which contributions may be based is raised
to $2400 , but the old $2000 maximum will enter into the computation of teh
annuity of any teacher retiring within five years of the effective date of
the bill , and prior service credit will continue to be based on the old
maximum .
The existing law contains no provision for out- of - state service
credit . This bill proposes to allow out -of -state service credit for service
in positions in other states comparable to service that would be covered
if rendered in Kentucky . The teacher may obtain credit for as many
years , not exceeding eight , as she has taught in another state since July
1 , 1940 , by paying the contributions she would have paid if serving in
Kentucky , plus three per cent compound interest , and by so doing will
also be given prior service credit for a number of years service prior
to July 1, 1940 , equal to the number of years of subsequent service for
which she has paid , not to exceed eight years . It is provided , however ,
that a member of the retirement system may not leave Kentucky to
teach in another state and then return and apply for credit for such
out -of-state service .
The bill further provides that teachers in the non - educational state
institutions under the Department of Welfare may come into the retire-
ment system and pay contributions under rules and regulations pre-
scribed by the board of trustees of the system . ( House Bill No. 369,
which has been approved , contains a similar provision .)
Under the present law, a teacher's contribution rate is based upon
her age at the time of entering the system, the rate being two per cent
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1946, p. 295

classification.title: An Act relating to the Teachers' Retirement System of the State of Kentucky.
classification.summary: This act amends the Teachers' Retirement System of the State of Kentucky by increasing the maximum salary on which contributions can be based from $2000 to $2400. It also allows for out-of-state service credit for comparable positions, with specific conditions for eligibility and payment. Additionally, it permits teachers in non-educational state institutions under the Department of Welfare to join the retirement system. The act includes an appropriation of $106,000 for each of the fiscal years 1946-47 and 1947-48 to cover the costs of these changes.

CHAPTER 112 279
if the age of entry was less than 30 , three per cent if it was 30 to 39 , and
four per cent if it was 40 or over . This bill makes the contribution rate
depend upon the teacher's age at the beginning of each scholastic year ,
the age classes and rates remaining the same . The effect of this change
would be that a teacher who entered the system at an age less than 30
would pay two per cent until she reached 30 , then would pay three per
cent until she reached 40 , and then would pay four per cent, while under
the present law her rate would stay at two per cent .
Under the present law the rate of retirement allowance changes
at the age of 65 and again at the age of 70 , with the result that a teacher
retiring at 65 receives a substantially larger allowance than one retiring
at the age of 64 , and one retiring at the age of 70 receives a substantially
larger allowance than one retiring at the age of 69. This bill fixes the
age of 70 as a basis for computing allowances , and provides for com-
puting the allowance of a person retiring at an age between 60 and 70
according to an actuarial table using the age of retirement as the factor .
The result of this would be to level off the allowances between 60 and
70 at a gradual increase .
Under the present law the maximum annual allowance is $ 1000 .
This bill raises it to $ 1200 .
Under the present law the minimum annual allowance is $ 100 . This
bill fixes the minimum at $ 100 for 20 years or less of service , plus $ 10
per year of service in excess of 20 years , but not in any event to exceed
$ 300 .
Under the present law , disability allowances are based upon one -half
of one per cent of the annual salary for prior service years , plus three-
quarters of one per cent of the annual salary for subsequent service
years , with a maximum of $ 1000 per year and a minimum of $ 100 per
year . Under this bill , the $ 100 minimum is increased by not less than
$10.00 per credit year at the time of retirement , not to exceed $300 .
(b) The bill appropriates $ 106,000 per year for the next two years
for the purpose of carrying out the provisions of the bill . This is to be in
addition to any other appropriations for teachers ' retirement . The Budget
Bill appropriates $ 850,000 to the Teachers ' Retirement Fund. This is be-
low the amount I recommended , and with the $ 106,000 added , it is still
below the amount recommended .
For these reasons I have approved the bill .
Frankfort, Kentucky
March 27 , 1946
SIMEON WILLIS , Governor .

the States in the promotion of such system , and for other pur-

Wagner -Peyser Act . All duties and powers conferred upon
any other department , agency or officer of this state relating
to the establishment , maintenance , and operation of free public
employment service offices shall be vested in the Employment
Service Commission . The provisions of said Act of Congress ,
as amended , are hereby accepted by this State , in conformity

henceforth enacted by the National Congress relating to the
employment service offices or programs .
§ 4. It shall be the duty of the Labor - Management rep-
resentatives on the Employment Service Commission :
( 1 ) To advise with the Employment Service Director
as to the administration of the employment service system .
3) To aid the Employment Service Director in his rela-
tionship with labor and management individuals and organiza-
tions .
(3 ) To assist in promoting cooperation among all groups
and agencies concerned with employment problems .
(4 ) To promote public understanding of the purposes ,
policies and practices of the employment service .
§ 5. The Employment Service Director shall appoint a
State Advisory Committee and Local Advisory Committees
in all important labor market areas in Kentucky . These com-
mittees shall be composed of representatives of labor and
management in equal numbers and the public . The commit-
tees shall aid the director in an advisory capacity in promot-
ing programs and solving problems relating to the adminis-
tration of the employment service and in assuring impartiality
and freedom from political influence in the solution of such
problems . The members of the Advisory Committee shall
serve without compensation but shall be reimbursed for neces-

CHAPTER 113 283
§ 6. The Employment Service Director , with the advice
and aid of the Advisory Committees shall :
( 1 ) Take all appropriate steps to reduce and prevent
unemployment .
(2) Encourage and assist in the adoption of practicable
methods of vocational training , retraining and vocational
guidance .
(3) Promote the employment of unemployed workers in
every feasible way and to these ends carry on and publish
the results of investigations and research studies .
(4 ) Maintain and publish detailed labor market informa-
tion to assist employers and workers in determining oppor-
tunities for employment and places of unemployment .
(5 ) Maintain a system of intra - state and inter - state
clearance of labor for employers and workers whereby job
opportunities may be offered to unemployed job applicants
throughout Kentucky and the nation .
(6) Establish and promote job counselling for Veterans ,
industrial workers , youths and others .
(7) Establish and promote a free industrial service for
employers and workers through a program of training , occu-
pational and worker analyses , testing , etc.
§ 7. ( 1 ) There is hereby created in the State Treasury
a special Fund to be known as the Employment Service Com-
mission Administration Fund . All monies deposited or paid
into this Fund are appropriated and shall be available for
the administration of the Employment Service offices and
functions in Kentucky .
(2 ) All monies in such Fund shall be expended solely to
defray the cost of the administration of this Act .
( 3 ) The Fund shall consist of all money appropriated
by this State and all money received from the United States
or any agency thereof or from any other source for the pur-
pose of administering the Employment Service program in
Kentucky .
(4) The Employment Service Director may enter into
an agreement with any agency charged with the administra-

with any private non -profit organization , as he may deem
necessary in the administration of the Employment Service
program , and may accept money , services or quarters as a con-

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
ARTICLE I
SECTION 1- DEFINITIONS
The following words and phrases , when used in this
Act, shall , for the purpose of this Act, have the meaning
respectively ascribed to them in this Section , except in
those instances where the context clearly indicates a dif-
ferent meaning :
( a ) " Department " means the Department of Revenue ,
except where otherwise specified herein .
(b ) “ Highway " means every way or place of what-
ever nature when any part of it is open to the use of the
public , as a matter of right , for purposes of vehicular
traffic .
(c) "Judgment " -Any judgment which shall have be-
come final by expiration without appeal of the time within
which an appeal might have been perfected , or by final af-
firmation on appeal , rendered by a court of competent
jurisdiction of any state or of the United States , upon a
cause of action arising out of the ownership , maintenance
or use of any motor vehicle , for damages , including damages
for care and loss of services , because of bodily injury to or
death of any person , or for damages because of injury to
or destruction of property , including the loss of use thereof ,
or upon a cause of action on an agreement of settlement for
such damages .
(d ) "Motor Vehicle " -Every self - propelled vehicle
which is designed for use upon a highway, including trailers
designed for use with such ehicles ( except traction engines ,
road rollers , farm tractors , tractor cranes , power shovels ,
and well drillers ) and every vehicle which is propelled by
electric power obtained from overhead wires but not oper-
ated upon rails .
(e ) " License "-Any operator's license , temporary in-
struction permit or temporary license issued under the laws
of this State pertaining to the licensing of operators .

292 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
The Department of Revenue is hereby authorized to
employ such additional persons , in accordance with the pro-
visions of KRS 42.120 and 42.130 , and incur travel , rental
and other current and capital expenses necessary to accom-
plish the purposes of this Act . There is appropriated out
of the Road Fund to the Department of Revenue for each
of the fiscal years ending June 30 , 1947 and June 30 , 1948 ,
an annual sum of $ 50,000 for the administration of this
Act .
(b ) Any person aggrieved by an order or act of the
Department under the provisions of this Act , may , within
twenty days after notice thereof , bring an action against
the Department in the Circuit Court of the county in which
the accident or violation of law upon which the action of
the Department was based occurred to vacate or set aside
the order or action of the Department on the ground that
it is unlawful or unreasonable . The answer of the Depart-
ment shall be served and filed within twenty days after the
service of the complaint . The action shall then be at issue
and stand ready for trial upon ten days notice to either
party , on the equity side of the docket of the Court . The
answer need not deny verbatim the allegations of the com-
plaint , but a general denial thereof on behalf of the Depart-
ment shall be sufficient . The filing of such action shall not
suspend the order or act of the Department unless a stay
thereof shall be granted by the Court pending final de-
termination of the review. Injunctive relief may also be
granted by the Court in the manner and upon the terms
provided by law.
SECTION 3-DEPARTMENT TO FURNISH OPERAT-
ING RECORD
The Department shall, upon request , furnish any per-
son a certified abstract of the operating record of any per-
son subject to the provisions of this Act , which abstract
shall also fully designate the motor vehicles , if any , reg-
istered in the name of such person , and if there shall be no
record of any conviction of such person of violating any law
relating to the operation of a motor vehicle or of any injury
or damage caused by such person , the Department shall so
certify . Such abstracts shall not be admissible as evidence
in any action for damages or criminal proceedings arising
out of a motor vehicle accident .

ARTICLE III
SECURITY FOLLOWING ACCIDENTS
SECTION 4—REPORT OF ACCIDENT REQUIRED
(a ) The operator of every motor vehicle which is in
any manner involved in an accident within this State , in
which any person is killed or injured or in which damage
to the property of any one person including himself , in ex-
cess of $ 50 is sustained , shall immediately report the mat-
ter in writing to the Department of Revenue . If such opera-
tor be physically incapable of making such report , the own-
er of the motor vehicle involved in such accident , shall , as
soon as he learns of the accident , report the matter in writ-
ing to the Department . The operator or the owner shall
make such other and additional reports relating to such
accident as the Department of Revenue shall require .
( b ) Failure to report an accident as herein provided
or failure to give correctly the information required of
him by the Department of Revenue in connection with such
report shall be a misdemeanor and , in the event of injury or
damage to the person or property of another in such ac-
cident , shall also constitute a ground for suspension or re-
vocation of
1. the license or registration for any motor vehicle , or
of all such licenses and registrations of the person failing
to make such report as herein required , and
2. the non -resident's operating privilege of such
person .
SECTION 5- SECURITY REQUIRED FOLLOWING AC-
CIDENT UNLESS EVIDENCE OF SUSPENSION
FOR FAILURE TO DEPOSIT SECURITY
(a ) The Department of Revenue , within 60 days after
receiving the report of the accident as required under Sec-
tion 4, shall suspend the license of each operator and all
registrations of each owner certified as required to deposit
security under this Section , and , if such operator is a non-
resident , the privilege of operating a motor vehicle within
this State , and , if such owner is a non -resident , the privi-
lege of the use within this State of any motor vehicle own-
ed by him, unless such operator or owner or both shall de-
posit security in the sum determined by the Department

SECTION 9—FORM AND AMOUNT OF SECURITY
The security required under this Article shall be in
such form and in such amount as the Department of Revenue
may require but in no case in excess of the limits specified
in Section 5. The person depositing security shall specify
in writing the person or persons on whose behalf the de-
posit is made and , at any time while such deposit is in the
custody of the Department of Revenue or State Treasurer ,
the person depositing it may , in writing , amend the specifi-
cation of the person or persons on whose behalf the de-
posit is made to include an additional person or persons ;
provided , however , that a single deposit of security shall
be applicable only on behalf of persons required to furnish
security because of the same accident .
The Department may reduce the amount of security
ordered in any case within 6 months after the date of the
accident , if, in its judgment , the amount ordered is excessive .
In case the security originally ordered has been deposited
the excess deposited over the reduced amount ordered shall
be returned to the depositor or his personal representative
forthwith , notwithstanding the provisions of Section 10 .
SECTION 10- CUSTODY , DISPOSITION AND RETURN
OF SECURITY
Security deposited in compliance with the requirements
of this article shall be placed by the Department of Revenue
in the custody of the State Treasurer and shall be ap-
plicable only to the payment of a judgment or judgments
rendered against the person or persons on whose behalf
the deposit was made , for damages arising out of the ac-
cident in question in an action at law , begun not later than
one year after the date of such accident , or within one year

3 of Section 7 , and such deposit or any balance thereof shall

PROOF OF FINANCIAL RESPONSIBILITY FOR

SECTION 12- COURTS TO REPORT NON - PAYMENT
OF JUDGMENTS AND CONVICTIONS
( a ) Whenever any person fails within 60 days to
satisfy any judgment , it shall be the duty of the clerk of the
court , or of the judge of a court which has no clerk , in
which any such judgment is rendered within this State , to
forward to the Department immediately after the expira-
tion of said 60 days , a certified copy of such judgment.
If the defendant named in any certified copy of a judg-
inent reported to the Department is a non - resident , the De-
partment shall transmit a certified copy of the judgment
to the official in charge of the issuance of licenses and
registration certificates of the state of which the defendant
is a resident .
(b) The clerk of the court , or the judge of a court
which has no clerk , in which any conviction for violation of
a motor vehicle law is rendered , or in which a person charged
with violation of a motor vehicle law has pleaded guilty or
forfeited bail , shall forward immediately to the department
a certified copy of the judgment, order or record of other
action of the court . This copy shall be prima facie evidence
of the conviction , plea or other action stated .

CHAPTER 118 299
SECTION 13- SUSPENSION FOR NON-PAYMENT OF
JUDGMENTS
( a ) Upon the receipt of a certified copy of a judg-
ment , the Department shall forthwith suspend ( except as
provided in Section 16 ) the license and registration and the
non -resident's operating privilege of any person against
whom such judgment was rendered .
( b ) If the judgment creditor consents in writing , in
such form as the Department may prescribe , that the judg-
ment debtor be allowed license and registration or non - resi-
dent's operating privilege , the same may be allowed by the
Department, in its discretion , for 6 months from the date
of such consent and thereafter until such consent is revoked
in writing , notwithstanding default in the payment of such
judgment, or of any installments thereof prescribed in
Section 16 , provided the judgment debtor furnishes proof
of financial responsibility .
SECTION 14 SUSPENSION TO CONTINUE UNTIL
JUDGMENTS PAID AND PROOF GIVEN
Such license , registration and non -resident's operating
privilege shall ( except as provided in Section 16 ) remain
so suspended and shall not be renewed , nor shall any such
license or registration be thereafter issued in the name of
such person , including any such person not previously
licensed , unless and until every such judgment is stayed ,
satisfied or discharged , except that a discharge in bank-
ruptcy shall not be deemed a satisfaction of such judg-
inent , and until the said person gives proof of financial re-
sponsibility .
SECTION 15- PAYMENTS SUFFICIENT TO SATISFY
REQUIREMENTS
Judgments in excess of the amounts specified in Sec-
tion 1 ( j ) shall , for the purpose of this Act only, be deem-
ed satisfied when payments in the amount so specified have
been credited thereon ; provided , however , payments made
in settlement of any claims because of bodily injury , death
or property damage arising from a motor vehicle accident
shall be credited in reduction of the respective amounts
so specified .

(f) Every motor vehicle liability policy shall be subject
to the following provisions which need not be contained
therein :
1. The liability of the insurance carrier with respect to
the insurance required by this Act shall become absolute when-
ever injury or damage covered by said motor vehicle liability
policy occurs ; said policy may not be cancelled or annulled
as to such liability by any agreement between the insurance
carrier and the insured after the occurrence of the injury or
damage ; no statement made by the insured or on his behalf
and no violation of said policy shall defeat or void said policy .
2. The satisfaction by the insured of a judgment for
such injury or damage shall not be a condition precedent to
the right or duty of the insurance carrier to make payment
on account of such injury or damage .
3. The insurance carrier shall have the right to settle
any claim covered by the policy , and if such settlement is made
in good faith , the amount thereof shall be deductible from
the limits of liability specified in Subdivision 2 of Subsection
(b) of this Section .
4. The policy , the written application therefor , if any ,
and any rider or endorsement which does not conflict with the
provisions of the Act shall constitute the entire contract be-
tween the parties .
(g ) Any policy which grants the coverage required for
a motor vehicle liability policy may also grant any lawful
coverage in excess of or in addition to the coverage specified
for a motor vehicle liability policy and such excess or addi-
tional coverage shall not be subject to the provisions of this
Act . With respect to a policy which grants such excess or
addtional coverage the term " motor vehicle liability policy "
shall apply only to that part of the coverage which is required
by this Section .
(h) Any motor vehicle liability policy may provide that
the insured shall reimburse the insurance carrier for any pay-

Additional lawful coverage beyond the required minimum is permitted and not subject to these specific provisions.

(i ) Any motor vehicle liability policy may provide for
the prorating of the insurance thereunder with other valid and
collectible insurance .
(j ) The requirements for a motor vehicle liability policy
may be fulfilled by the policies of one or more insurance car-
riers which policies together meet such requirements .
(k ) Any binder issued pending the issuance of a motor
vehicle liability policy shall be deemed to fulfill the require-
ments for such a policy .
SECTION 22-NOTICE OF CANCELLATION OR TERMI-
NATION OF CERTIFIED POLICY
When an insurance carrier has certified a motor vehicle
liability policy under Section 19 or a policy under Section 20 ,
the insurance so certified shall not be cancelled or terminated
until at least ten days after a notice of cancellation or termina-
tion of the insurance so certified shall be filed in the office
of the Department, except that such a policy subsequently
procured and certified shall , on the effective date of its cer-
tification , terminate the insurance previously certified with
respect to any motor vehicle designated in both certificates .
SECTION 23-ACT NOT TO AFFECT OTHER POLICIES
(a ) This Act shall not be held to apply to or affect
policies of automobile insurance against liability which may
now or hereafter be required by any other law of this State ,
and such policies , if they contain an agreement or are endorsed
to conform to the requirements of this Act , may be certified
as proof of financial responsibility under this Act .
(b ) This Act shall not be held to apply to or affect
policies insuring solely the insured named in the policy against
liability resulting from the maintenance or use by persons in
the insured's employ or on his behalf of motor vehicles not
owned by the insured .
SECTION 24-BOND AS PROOF
( a) Proof of financial responsibility may be evidenced
by the bond of a surety company duly authorized to transact
business within this State , or a bond with at least two indi-
vidual sureties each owning real estate within this State ,

pend the license and registration or the non - resident's operat-

SECTION 31-SURRENDER OF LICENSE AND REGIS-
TRATION
Any person whose license or registration shall have been
suspended as herein provided , or whose policy of insurance or
bond , when required under this Act , shall have been cancelled
or terminated , or who shall neglect to furnish other proof
upon request of the Department shall immediately return his
license and registration to the Department . If any person
shall fail to return to the Department the license or registra-
tion as provided herein , the Department shall forthwith direct
and peace officer to secure possession thereof and return the
same to the Department .
SECTION 32 —OTHER VIOLATIONS-PENALTIES
( a ) Any person whose license or registration or non-
resident's operating privilege has been suspended or revoked
under this Act and who , during such suspension or revocation
drives any motor vehicle upon any highway or knowingly per-
mits any motor vehicle owned by such person to be operated
by another upon any highway , except as permitted under this
Act , shall be fined not more than $ 500 or imprisoned not ex-
ceeding six months , or both .
(b) Any person willfully failing to return license or
registration as required in Section 31 shall be fined not more
than $500 or imprisoned not to exceed thirty days , or both .
(c) Any person who shall forge or , without authority ,
sign any evidence of proof of financial responsibility , or who
files or offers for filing any such notice or evidence of proof
knowing or having reason to believe that it is forged or signed
without authority , shall be fined not more than $ 1,000 or im-
prisoned not more than one year , or both .

Nothing in this Act shall be construed as preventing the

clares that it would have passed the remaining parts of this
Act if it had known that such part or parts thereof would be

This Act shall be effective from and after January 1 , 1947 .

classification.title: CHAPTER 119
classification.summary: This act amends and re-enacts Chapter 43 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes, 1944 Edition, to redefine the boundary of the Forty-third District in the City of Louisville. It also addresses the consolidation of voting precincts in Jefferson County due to the installation of voting machines, aiming for an average of 700 votes per precinct. The purpose is to adjust and correct boundary lines for legislative districts 35th, 37th, 39th, 40th, 41st, and 43rd, and to make precinct consolidation more feasible and convenient following city annexations. This consolidation is urgent to prepare for the Primary Election in August 1946.
classification.reasoning: The statute describes the redrawing of legislative district boundaries and the consolidation of voting precincts in Louisville and Jefferson County. The stated reasons for these changes are the installation of voting machines and the need to average 700 votes per precinct, as well as city annexations. While the historical context of Jim Crow laws often involved the manipulation of voting districts and precinct boundaries to disenfranchise Black voters, this specific statute does not contain any explicit or implicit racial indicators. The language focuses solely on administrative and logistical reasons for redistricting and precinct consolidation. Therefore, it is classified as 'no' for being a Jim Crow law, with a high confidence score. The category is 'voting' as it directly pertains to electoral districts and precincts. The document type is 'session_laws' as it is presented as an act passed by the General Assembly. Human review is not needed due to the clear lack of racial indicators.

CHAPTER 120
(H. B. 440 )
AN ACT relating to mines and mining .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. Section 351.010 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) As used in this chapter , unless the context requires
otherwise :
(a) " Chief " means Chief of the Department of Mines
and Minerals .
(b ) " Commercial mine " means any coal mine from
which coal is mined for sale or exchange .
( c) " Department " means the Department of Mines
and Minerals .
( d ) " Drift " means an opening through the strata ,
with opening grades such as to permit coal to be hauled by
mules or mechanical traction power , and capable of being
used for the purpose of ventilation , drainage , ingress , egress
and other purposes in connection with the mining of coal .
(e) "Gaseous mine " means a mine in which the per-
centage of explosive gas exceeds one-half of one percent at
the return of any one split of dusty mine , or three -fourths
of one percent at the return of any one split if a non - dusty
mine .
" Nongaseous mine " means any mine not classified as a
gaseous mine .
(f) "Mine " means a group or system of shafts , slopes ,
drifts or inclined planes connected by one general system
of mine railroads over which coal may be delivered to one
or more points outside the mine , and connected with excava-
tions that penetrate coal strata and are ventilated by one
general air current or divisions thereof .

§ 2. Section 351.090 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The Governor shall appoint for a term of four
years not more than twenty -five district mine inspectors , one
of whom shall be designated as an electrical district mine.
inspector , and not more than eight inspectors of mine
weights .
( 2 ) All district mine inspectors and inspectors of mine
weights shall have a thorough knowledge of first aid and
mine rescue and be able to instruct in first aid and mine
rescue , shall possess thoroughly the knowledge required of
the chief of the department by KRS 351.060 , and shall have
a thorough practical knowledge of mining gained by at least
ten years ' experience in and about coal mines , at least five
of which must have been in and about mines in this state .
(3 ) No person shall be appointed to the office of dis-
trict mine inspector until he has passed a satisfactory ex-
amination before the Board of Examiners . The Chief of
the Department of Mines and Minerals may recommend to
the Governor applicants for district mine inspector who have

(4 ) District mine inspectors and inspectors of mine.
weights shall be of good moral character and temperate
habits and shall not , while holding office , act in any official
capacity in operating any coal mine .
( 5 ) Each district mine inspector and each inspector of
mine weights shall receive a salary of not more than four
thousand two hundred dollars per annum , payable monthly ,
and shall be paid their necessary traveling and other expenses
incurred on account of and when engaged in the discharge
of their official duties . Salaries shall be determined by the
chief of the department and approved by the Governor , and
shall be based on the ability and experience of the inspector .
(6) Each district mine inspector and inspector of mine
weights shall take oath , which shall be certified by the officer
administering it . The oath , in writing , and the certificate
shall be filed in the office of the Secretary of State .
(7) Each district mine inspector and inspector of mine
weights shall give bond with surety approved by the Governor .
§ 3. Section 351.140 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
Each mine inspector shall give his entire time and at-
tention to the duties of his office which shall consist of the
following :
(1 ) Inspecting coal mines and aiding under the direc-
tion of the chief of the department in carrying out and en-
forcing the provisions of the laws relating to the inspection
of coal mines :
(2 ) Training officials and workmen in and about the
mines in first aid and mine rescue methods :
( 3 ) Advising officials and workmen in methods per-
taining to safety in all its phases and in methods pertaining
to the prevention of mine fires and explosions :
(4 ) Taking charge of mine rescue and recovery work
whenever a mine fire , mine explosion or other serious ac-
cident occurs within his district , and the chief is not present ,
and assisting in such work in other districts when so directed
by the chief :

§ 4. Section 351.150 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
Each district mine inspector shall each week report in
writing to the chief of the department the number and con-
dition of all mines inspected by him during the week .
Each mine inspector shall each week deliver to the chief
of the department a written report showing the condition of
cach mine inspected by him and , at the same time , shall de-
liver a copy of such report to the operator or the superin-
tendent of each mine inspected and another copy thereof to
the mine committee at such mine so inspected ; and at the
same time each mine inspector shall post a copy of such in-
spection report on a bulletin board at a prominent place of
the operating company or mine where it may conveniently be
read by any of the mine employees .
Each district mine inspector shall , within fifteen days
after the close of each calendar year , file with the chif a re-
port of his proceedings during the calendar year and give
such information concerning the mines and mining condi-
tions in his district as is appropriate and required by the
chief .
§ 5. Section 351.160 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The chief of the department shall annually make
a report to the Governor of his proceedings during the pre-
ceding calendar year . The annual report shall be prepared
and printed as soon as possible within the four months after
the close of the calendar year . The annual report shall cover
the complete operation of the coal mines in this state during
the preceding calendar year, enumerating all accidents oc-

in the return of any split to not more than one half of one

(3 ) If any accident occurs to the ventilating fan or its
machinery , so that the ventilation of the mine will be serious-
ly interrupted , the mine foreman shall order the men to
withdraw immediately from the mine , and shall not allow
them to return until the ventilation has been restored and
the mine thoroughly examined by him or by an assistant
mine foreman or fire boss , and reported safe .

der his charge where explosive gas is likely to accumulate
and he shall place a danger signal across the entrance to
every place where explosive gas is discovered or where im-
mediate danger is found to exist from any other cause . The
signal shall be sufficient warning for persons not to enter .

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
1. Section 186.060 , subsection ( 1 ) , of the Kentucky Re-
vised Statutes , ( section 2739g -7 , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ,
Baldwin's 1936 Revision ) , is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) Applications for registration of motor vehicles
owned exclusively by county , city or board of education in
the state or by the State or Federal government shall be ac-
companied by a statement from the head of the department

fying that the motor vehicle is exclusively owned and operated

number plate and reporting the same the county clerk shall
be entitled to a fee of fifty cents ($ .50 ) in each instance , to
be paid by the department upon whose authorization such
license was issued .

CHAPTER 124
AN ACT relating to marriage .
(H. B. 454)
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 402.010 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) No marriage shall be contracted between persons
who are nearer of kin to each other by consanquinity , whether
of the whole or half-blood , than second cousins .
( 2 ) Marriages prohibited by subsection ( 1 ) of this sec-
tion are incestuous and void .
Passed March 20 , enrolled March 21 , approved March 26 , 1946 .

on behalf of the governmental agency , with power to charge

the payment of any bonds or obligations outstanding against
the public project , and for the payment of operating expenses ,
and to apply the income and revenue in conformity with this
Act and the order , resolution or ordinance referred to in
Section 2 of this Act .
§ 7. At or before the issuance of bonds the governmental
agency shall , by order , resolution or ordinance , set aside and
pledge the income and revenue of the public project including
rents , royalties , fees and proceeds of sales of property and
from rates and charges for services derived from or rendered
by the public project into a separate and special fund to be
used and applied in payment of the cost thereof and in the
maintenance , operation and depreciation thereof . The order ,
resolution or ordinance shall definitely fix and determine the
amount of revenue necessary to be set apart and applied to
the payment of principal and interest of the bonds , and the
properties of the balance of the income and revenue to be set
aside as a proper and adequate depreciation account , and the
remaining proportion of such balances shall be set aside for
the reasonable and proper operation and maintenance of the
public project . The rents , royalties , fees , rates and charges
for the services or sale of the public project shall be fixed and
revised from time to time so as to be sufficient to provide for

classification.title: Amendments to KRS 186.510 and 186.560 regarding motor vehicle licenses
classification.summary: This statute amends two sections of the Kentucky Revised Statutes concerning motor vehicle licenses. Section 6 amends KRS 186.510 to clarify that a licensee must have their license in their immediate possession while driving and display it upon demand to law enforcement or department officials. It also provides a defense if the license is produced in court. Section 7 amends KRS 186.560 to list offenses for which a motor vehicle operator's license shall be revoked, including manslaughter, driving under the influence, perjury related to motor vehicle laws, felonies committed with a vehicle, and multiple charges of reckless driving.

CHAPTER 128
(H. B. 491 )
AN ACT to provide for the construction and maintenance , in cities of the
third class , of memorials in honor of the soldiers and sailors furnished
by such cities and the counties wherein situated , to the United States
Army, and Navy during the first and present World War ; providing
for the levying and collecting of a tax for the construction and main-
tenance in such cities , and for the acceptance of gifts and donations ,
for such purposes ; ( partially repealing Section 94.110 Kentucky
Statutes ; and for related purposes ; including the creation of a
Memorial Commission in such cities .
Whereas , it appears that in the City of Henderson , Henderson County ,
a third class city of this Commonwealth , and in other cities of the
third class if they desire to do so , the citizens desire to erect an ap-
propriate memorial to the soldiers and sailors furnished by said city
and county to the army and navy of the United States , and who
rendered service in behalf of the nation and the flag in the recent
World War , and that in order to construct and maintain a suitable
memorial in said city an appropriate enabling act must be enacted
permitting the raising of funds therefor by gifts and donations ; and
that a sutable commission must be created to handle such funds ,
and to construct and maintain such memorial ; now , therefore ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
1 . Memorial Commission - Members
Terms-Powers-No Compensation .
- Appointment-
The Mayor of any city of the third class in this Common-
wealth is hereby authorized and empowered to appoint fifteen
( 15 ) persons who shall constitute a memorial commission
hereinafter referred to as " Commission . " Each such member
shall not be less than twenty -five ( 25 ) years of age , and shall
be a bona fide resident of the county wherein such city is situ-
ated . Such appointments shall be subject to the approval of
the city legislative body for such city . Said Commission shall
be a body politic and corporate , and shall be known as-
Memorial Commission ; " and in such corporate name said
Commission may sue and be sued , contract and be contracted
with , adopt a seal , and alter same at pleasure ; acquire real

five thousand dollars ( $5,000.00 ) per annum He shall serve ,
and be removable , at the pleasure of the Commission . The
commission is also empowered to select such other officers or
employes as it may deem necessary to properly carry on the
work of the Commission hereunder , to serve at the pleasure
of the Commission ; and the Commission shall fix the com-
pensation of such additional officers and employes .
3. Further Powers of Commission - Said Commission
shall have full power and authority to carry out the purposes
of this Act , among which powers shall shall be the following
the enumeration of which shall in no wise be construed as re-
stricting the scope of the general powers conferred on it by
this Act, to -wit :
( a) To make all such preliminary investigations , and to
do all such preliminary work as , and in its judgment should
precede the actual construction and equipment of the memo-
rial structures herein named .
(b ) To choose an appropriate site for such memorial
structures , and the plans fur such structures , as herein pro-
vided , and to arrange for and supervise the laying out of the
necessary grounds and the construction of the said structures ,
and the maintenance of said grounds and structures for the
purposes of this Act .
(c ) To exact from any of its officers or employes , where
provision is not otherwise made herein therefor , such surety
or idemnity bonds for the appropriate performance of their
respective duties as the Commission may deem proper .

5. What shall Constitute the Memorial .-The said Com-

morial art , to commemorate the valorous and patriotic deeds
and service of the soldiers and sailors of the city and county
wherein such Commission shall act , who were engaged in the
army and navy of the United States during World War No. 1 ,
and present world conflict known as World War No. 2. Said
memorial shall be permanent in character , and shall take the
form of a building , or buildings , with halls and auditoriums
ample for both large and small assemblages , conventions ,
large theatrical , musical , basketball games and other sports
and entertainments , and with suitable offices , rooms and
equipment to properly maintain and operate such building ,

memorial art , and all grounds necessary or appropriate for
such purposes , all of which grounds , buildings statuary and
works of art , shall for the purposes of this Act , be embraced

ing to provide for the construction , equipment , and mainte-
nance of such memorial , it shall be the duty of the Commission
to make , and to cause to be made , such investigation as may

7. Report and Approval of Plans .-When the Commis-
sion shall have determined upon the general plans for the
construction and equipment of said memorial , a full report of
such plans , together with a statement of the probable cost of
carrying out such plans , shall be submitted by the Commission
to the aforesaid Mayor , for the records of the city .
8. Bids for Work .-All work to be done , or supplies or
materials to be purchased , in carrying out the purposes of this
Act , when involving an expenditure of five hundred dollars
($500.00 ) or more , shall be , by contract , awarded to the lowest
and best bidder , but the Commission shall have the right to
reject any bid ; and it is also empowered to do any part or
parts of such work , under such conditions as it may prescribe ,

ever , as limiting the power of the Commission in the employ-

bank or banks , or trust company or trust companies , in such

shall constitute assets of the Commission for expenditure in

able to the provisions of this Act .
16. Tax for Construction .-In order to provide part of
money for the construction and equipment of said memorial ,
the city legislative body of such city is empowered and di-
rected to annually levy and collect a tax not exceeding ten
cents ( 10c ) on each one hundred dollars ( $ 100.00 ) worth of
taxable property in such city , as determined by the last regu-
lar assessment of such city, and the tax so levied , shall be col-
lected in the customary way , and shall be paid over to said
Commission for the purposes , except that such tax , shall not
be levied or collected for construction purposes , after the re-
port of the Mayor of the completion of the said memorial as
provided in section 9 in this Act .
17. Use of Funds Derived Through Bonds or Gifts.-
All of the funds realized from the aforesaid tax, together with
the aforesaid gifts or donations or funds , shall be expended
by the Commission for the acquirement of necessary grounds
and for the construction and equipment of said memorial , as
provided for in this act ; and the title to all such property ,
however acquired , shall vest in said Commission , and shall
be held and used in strict and inviolable trust for the purpose
contemplated by this act , and free from all taxation , impost
or assessment- state , county , district , municipal , or other-
wise . The Commission shall have the right to exchange or sell ,
for the purposes of this act , any of the property donated or
given to it for such purposes .
18. Partial Repeal of Section 94.110 , Kentucky Statutes .
-Section 94.110 Kentucky Statutes , vesting in the Board of
Public Works of cities of the third class , supervision and
control over the construction of all public buildings and public
improvements , shall, to the extent that same conflicts with
this act , stand repealed , but to no further extent .
19. Legal Services .- All legal services or advice re-
quired by said Commission shall be rendered by the city at-
torney of such city , and his assistants , without additional
compensation to them therefor .

20. Repealing Clause . All acts or parts of acts in con-
flict or inconsistent herewith are hereby repealed .
21. Invalidation of Any Provision - Effect . If any sec-
tion , paragraph , clause or separable provision of this act shall
be held to be invalid such fact shall not affect or render in-
valid any other section , paragraph , clause or separable pro-
vision of the act , it being the intention of the General Assem-
bly in enacting this act to enact each section , paragraph ,
caluse and provision separately .
22 . Liberal Construction of Act .- This act and all and
each of its various sections and provisions , shall be liberally
construed in fabor of the purposes of the act .
23. Emergency Clause .-Because of the fact that in
Henderson , a city of the third class , there are ready to be
made to the Commission which may , under provisions of this
act be created for said city , substantial gifts and donations ,
and for the further reason that it is very important that in
said city, the provisions of this act become effective at the
earliest possible moment in order to carry forward success-
fully the work of the Commission hereunder , an an emergency
is hereby declared to exist , and this act shall take effect on
and after its approval by the Governor .
Passed March 20 , enrolled March 21 , approved March 23 , 1946 .

CHAPTER 130
(H. B. 499)
AN ACT relating to insurance of State lands and buildings .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 56.160 KRS is amended to read as
follows :
With the approval of the Department of Finance , the
Division of Insurance may contract with any responsible fire .
and tornado insurance or reinsurance company authorized to
do business in Kentucky to reinsure any building and its con-
tents of which the total valuation has been fixed at over
$500,000 in such a way as to limit the net liability of the State
Fire and Tornado Insurance Fund with respect to such build-
ing and its contents to $500,000 , and during any fiscal year in
which the State Fire and Tornado Insurance Fund amounts
to less than $ 2,000,000 , the Division of Insurance , with the
approval of the Department of Finance , may reinsure any
building and its contents of which the total valuation has been
fixed at over $ 200,000 in such a way as to limit the net liabil-
ity of the State Fire and Tornado Insurance Fund with re-
spect to such building and its contents to $ 200,000 . The
premium for reinsurance shall be paid out of the State Fire
and Tornado Insurance Fund , on warrant of the Department
of Finance .

Section 2. Section 56.180 KRS is amended to read as

If at the end of any fiscal year the moneys and securities
to the credit of the State Fire and Tornado Insurance Fund
equal or exceed two million dollars , the Division of Insurance
shall certify to the State Treasurer a credit to each state
agency having control or custody of state property . The
credit shall operate to reduce the premium for the next fiscal
year . The credit shall be calculated by taking the total
amount of the premiums determined under KRS 56.090 , sub-
tracting from such premiums the total amount of the losses
and damage debited against the fund and the total expenses
debited against the said fund under KRS 56.150 and 56.160 ,
and dividing the result by the total amount of premiums as
determined under KRS 56.090 . The credit shall be applied
equally against each premium debitable against each state
agency having control or custody of state property , and shall
revert to the account of that agency without prejudice as to
its use .

Statutes of 1944 is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) Any board of education that has had its budgets
and salary schedule for the ensuing school year approved by
the State Board of Education , whose ratio of assessed valua-
tion of property to fair cash value is equal to the average
ratio throughout the state , as certified to the State Board of
Education by the Kentucky Tax Commission , and has levied
for school purposes a tax of at least seventy - five cents on
each one hundred dollars of property subject to local taxation
and not less than the ad valorem tax levy made for the pre-
vious school year , shall have the privilege of applying for
aid from the equalization fund .
( 2 ) The Superintendent of Public Instruction shall
make a careful study to determine annually the amount of
adjusted recurring revenue that is available for the educa-
tion of each pupil based on average daily membership and
average daily attendance in the public schools in each school
district in Kentucky . This study which shall be used as a
basis for the distribution of moneys from the equalization
fund , shall be based upon records and reports for the school
year ending June 30 immediately preceding such study .

After all school districts in the state have been arranged in
a rank order from high to low according to their net -ability
indices , the Superintendent of Public Instruction with the
approval of the State Board of Education , shall distribute
the equalization fund to districts qualifying under this Act

CHAPTER 132
(H. B. 509 )
AN ACT to amend and re - enact Section 201.170 , Kentucky Revised
Statutes , relating to the tax for the maintenance of a home in each
county containing a city of the first class for children who are de-
pendent , neglected or delinquent , or for other reasons received in
the home .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
That Section 201.170 , Kentucky Revised Statutes , be
amended and reenacted so that it shall read as follows :
201.170 . In order to provide money for maintaining the
home and for carrying out the purpose of this chapter other
than those named in Section 201.160 , Kentucky Revised
Statutes , the fiscal court of the county in which the home
is established , and the legislative body of the city of the
first class shall levy and collect a tax on each one hundred
dollars ' worth of taxable property in the county and city ,
respectively , as determined by the last regular assessment .
Such tax shall be such number of cents , not exceeding ten
cents , on each one hundred dollars ' worth of said taxable
property as in the judgment of the said fiscal court and said
legislative body shall be reasonably necessary for said pur-
poses . All funds derived from such levies and collections
shall be paid over to the board for the purposes mentioned
in this section .
Passed and enrolled March 21 , approved March 23 , 1946 .

All printing for which any county , city of other political

CHAPTER 134
(H. B. 514 )
AN ACT to provide homes for Kentucky Veterans of World War II.
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Article I.
Section 1. Definitions . As used in Article I of this
Act, unless the context otherwise requires :
( a) "Veteran " means any person inducted into any
of the armed services of the United States under the National
Selective Service Act , and any person who has served or
may hereafter serve in any branch of the United States
Army, the Navy , or other armed services during World War
II or during the present national emergency .
(b ) " Housing commission " or " commission " means
any housing commission created pursuant to this Act or any
housing commission now existing .
(c) " Housing project " means a building or buildings
containing rooms to be provided as living quarters , and other
facilities and appurtenances deemed reasonably necessary
by the housing commission to the successful and economical
operation of the project , including such buildings , land ,
equipment , facilities and other real or personal property for
necessary , convenient or desirable appurtenances ; streets ,
sewers , water services , and site preparation .

that a member of a veterans ' housing commission is incom-
petent or guilty of neglect of duty, or misfeasance or mal-
feasance , the appointed member may be required to appear
