the member may be heard and he may present such pertinent
evidence as he wishes or as requested . If after the hearing
the commission feels that a member has been incompetent ,
or has been guilty of neglect of duty , or misfeasance or mal-
feasance , it shall remove the member within seven days and
there shall be a vacancy in the office to be filled as in this Act
provided .

necessary buildings ; and

ercise or carry out any authority or power herein given it

Section 12. No contract or agreement with any con-
tractor for the construction of a veterans ' housing project ,

Section 13. A veterans ' housing commission may ac-
quire by gift , devise , bequest , lease , grant , or purchase , solely

for the low cost of veterans ' housing projects , upon terms ,

mission and agreed upon between the commission and the

Section 14. If it becomes necessary to condemn prop-
erty to construct a veterans ' housing project , or secure
rights -of-way leading thereto , the veterans ' housing commis-
sion may, by resolution reciting the need , order the con-
demnation of any land or improvements or interest in land

shall be the same as that provided in KRS 416.120 , and the

ings , or for the cost or expense of the proceedings , shall be

Section 15. The veterans ' housing commission shall
operate , manage and control veterans ' housing projects in
their entirety, fix the rate of rentals , and establish by -laws ,

projects not inconsistent with the provisions of this Act .

of a project located within the city.

CHAPTER 134 361
Section 16. The veterans ' housing commission shall
manage and operate its housing projects in an efficient man-
ner so as to enable it to fix the rentals for dwelling accom-
modations at the lowest possible rates consistent with de-
cent , safe and sanitary dwelling accommodations , and no
veterans ' housing commission shall construct or operate any
veterans ' housing project for profit, or as a source of
revenue to the city.
Section 17. A veterans ' housing commission may rent
or lease the dwelling accommodations of a veterans ' hous-
ing project only to Kentucky veterans , and they shall be
selected by the commission upon the basis of those in great-
est need of such housing . A veterans ' housing commission
may rent or lease to a veteran dwelling accommodations con-
sisting only of the number of rooms it deems necessary to
provide safe and sanitary accommodations to the proposed
occupants without overcrowding .
Nothing contained in this section shall limit the power
of a veterans ' housing commission to vest in an obligee of
the commission the right , in the event of a default by the
commission , to take possession of a veterans ' housing pro-
ject or cause the appointment of a receiver thereof , free
from all of the restrictions imposed by this section .
Section 18. Cities of all classes may provide funds for
carrying out the purposes of this Act by the issuance of
revenue bonds pursuant to a resolution of the veterans '
housing commission . The bonds or other obligations of a
veterans ' housing commission shall not constitute an obliga-
tion of the city . The bonds shall be payable solely and only
out of the property , revenues and assets of the veterans '
housing commission . Nothing contained in this Act shall
authorize or permit any city to incur any obligation of any
nature prohibited by the Constitution . Subject to the re-
strictions set forth in this Act, a city veterans ' housing com-
mission may incur any indebtedness and issue any obliga-
tions and give any security it deems necessary or advisable
in connection with any project undertaken by it. A city
veterans ' housing commission may issue its bonds to pay for
its indebtedness from time to time , in such amounts , with
such maturities , and upon such terms and conditions and
upon such security as it deems necessary or advisable in
connection with any project undertaken or to be undertaken

CHAPTER 134 373
provisions of this Act , including all powers specifically enu-
merated in Section 10 , Article I of this Act.
Article III .
Section 1. Bonds or other obligations issued by any
veterans ' housing commission for a veterans ' housing project
developed or administered pursuant to the provisions of this
Act , shall be eligible for security for public deposits and legal
investments to the same extent and for the same persons , in-
stitutions , associations , corporations , bodies and officers as
bonds or other obligations issued pursuant to KRS 80.010 to
80.250 and KRS 80.310 to 80.610 .
Section 2. Any veterans ' housing commission created
under the provisions of this Act may cooperate with , or act
as an agent for , the Federal Government in the development
or administration of similar projects by the Federal Govern-
ment . In acting under this authorization , a veterans ' housing
commission shall not be subject to any limitations , restrictions
or requirements of other laws , except those relating to land
acquisition. Any veterans ' housing commission created under
the provisions of this Act may do any and all things necessary
or desirable to cooperate with , or act as agent for , the Federal
Government , or to secure financial aid , in the expeditious de-
velopment or in the administration of projects to assure the
availability of safe and sanitary dwellings for Kentucky
veterans .
The enumeration of powers in this Act shall in nowise be
construed as a limitation upon the powers of any veterans '
housing commission , except as expressly provided or as lim-
ited by the Constitution of Kentucky . The provisions of this
Act shall be construed as supplementary and as in addition to
any other laws now in force .
Section 3. WHEREAS , there is now a critically acute
housing shortage , and
WHEREAS , thousands of returning Kentucky veterans
are in immediate need of housing for themselves and their
families , an emergency is declared to exist and this Act shall
become effective immediately upon its passage and approval
by the Governor .
Passed March 20 , enrolled March 21 , approved March 23 , 1946 .

AN ACT authorizing the State Board of Education to accept , on behalf
of the Commonwealth , a conveyance of the properties of the Lincoln
Institute in Shelby County , Kentucky , and to hereafter operate the
same as an education institution in accordance with the terms and
conditions of the instruments pursuant to which said institute was
established , and providing appropriations for the operation of the
institute pending and after such conveyance, and for capital outlay
in the event the conveyance is made .
WHEREAS , Lincoln Institute in Shelby County , Kentucky , has for many
years been operated as an educational institution , supported in main
part by private funds and donations , and
WHEREAS , the Commonwealth has received the benefit of a higher
educational level of its citizens by means of the operation of said
institute , and
WHEREAS , it is in the interest of the Commonwealth that said institute
be maintained and continued as an institution of education , and ,
WHEREAS , by reason of a substantial fire loss incurred on the 5th
day of March , 1946 , the future of the said institution as a privately
maintained institution of learning is in danger and an emergency
exists which requires immediate action to protect the interests of
the Commonwealth and its citizens ,
NOW, THEREFORE ,
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. In the event the Board of Trustees of the Lincoln
Institute in Shelby County , Kentucky , shall tender to the
Commonwealth a deed of conveyance conveying the properties
of said institute , the State Board of Education is hereby
authorized to accept said conveyance on behalf of the Common-
wealth and to thereafter operate the institute as a state insti-
tution of learning in accordance with the terms and conditions
of the instruments pursuant to which the institute was orig-
inally established .
§ 2. In order to make provision for the adequate opera-
tion of Lincoln Institute as a practice -teacher -training high
school pending transfer of the properties of said institute to
the Commonwealth , and in order that the private funds and
donations available to the institute may be released for recon-
struction of the buildings to replace the fire loss above referred
to , there is hereby appropriated out of the General Expendi-
ture Fund in the State Treasury , the sum of $ 30,000.00 for
each of the fiscal years 1946-1947 and 1947-1948 , said sums to

Sec . 6. Section 57.190 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes

Sec . 7. Subsection ( 1 ) of Section 57.230 of the Kentucky

( 1 ) For all composition , in any kind of type , except
bills , blanks and miscellaneous printing , the rates per one
thousand ems shall not exceed the following :
(a) Plain or book composition , sixty cents .
(b) Column matter , in three or more columns , depend-
ing upon each other , and reading across the page , without
rules , one dollar .
( c ) Rule , figure work , tabulated , indexing and side notes ,
one dollar and fifty cents .
( d ) Original composition transferred from one docu-
ment to another of whatever character , including leading or
unleading , over -running , changing head or foot line and mak-
ing up forms , 25c for each form so changed of whatever size .
Sec . 8. Section 57.240 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The maximum rates for presswork on book , pamph-
let or similar work shall be $ 2.50 per thousand impressions on
full size sheets of twenty -five inches by thirty -eight inches or
less , and $ 3.50 per thousand impressions on full size sheets
of twenty -eight inches by forty-two inches or more .
(2) Presswork on all forms other than book work ,
pamphlet and similar forms shall be at the rate of $1.50 per
thousand impressions or less regardless of the number of
forms that can be printed on a full size sheet of card board
or paper suitable for the purpose .

CHAPTER 140
(S. B. 5 )
AN ACT providing for persons living in cities of the Fifth or Sixth
Classes to demand of county school board to place their property in a
certain school district .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
( 1 ) Whenever any territory in any municipality of the
Fifth or Sixth Class or any territory which may become in-
corporated in any such municipality , is located in a county
school district , the owners of real property in such territory
are given the right to demand of the Board of Education of
the county school district in which their property is located
that said property should be placed in and become a part of
the school district in which the greater part of said municipal-
ity is located and embraced ; whether said school district be
a county or independent school district .

( 2) If 75 % of the owners of real property located in

ten petition to the Board of Education of the school district
in which their property is located demanding of said Board
of Education that their property be placed in another school

majority of the Board of Education of said recipient school

(4) Should the Board of Education of the school dis-
trict of which the demand is made for the transfer of certain
property as set out in Section 1 of this Act , refuse to comply
with the request presented by the petition as set out in Sec-
tions 2 and 3 of this Act , the property owners who have
signed said petition or one or more of them on behalf of
all of said property owners may resort to the Circuit Court
of the County in which said property is located and may
proceed by mandamus or other procedure .
(5) The purpose of this Act is to enable all property
located within any municipally incorporated territory to be
placed in one school district as such procedure tends to pro-
mote school and civic spirit .

edition of the Kentucky Revised Statutes are repealed , and

271.005 Definitons . As used in this chapter :
( 1 ) "Articles of incorporation " includes both the orig-
inal articles of incorporation and amendments thereto , ex-
cept in those instances where the context expressly refers to
the original articles only .
( 2 ) An " incorporator " is a signer of the original
articles of incorporation .
(3) A " subscriber' is one who subscribes for shares
in a corporation , whether before or after incorporation .
(4) " Shares " are the units into which the share-
holders ' rights to participate in the control of the corpora-
tion , in its surplus or profits or in the distribution of cor-
porate assets , are divided .
(6
(5) A shareholder " is one who owns one or more
shares . A subscriber becomes a shareholder upon the allot-
ment of shares to him . Nothing in this section shall forbid a
corporation to recognize a person registered on its books
as the owner of shares as the person exclusively entitled to
have and to exercise the rights and privileges incident to the
ownership of such shares , or to hold liable for calls and as-
sessments a person registered as the owner of shares .
(6) A "certificate of stock " is a written instrument
signed as required by KRS 271.165 , and evidencing the fact
that the person therein named is the registered owner of the
share or shares thereby represented .
(7) " Allotment " means the apportioning of a certain
number of shares to a subscriber in response to the applica-
tion contained in his subscription , or to a shareholder pur-
suant to the declaration of a stock dividend . The allotment
of shares to the incorporators , or to persons whose sub-
scriptions were approved by the incorporators before incor-
poration and were unrevoked at the time of incorporation ,
shall be considered automatically coincident with incorpora-
tion.
( 8) The " assets " of a corporation include all its prop-
erty and rights of every kind .

271.055 Filing and recording articles of " incorpora-
tion " ; issuing certificate of incorporation ; articles and state-

( 1 ) Subscriptions for shares of a corporation to be formed
shall be in writing . Unless otherwise provided in the writing ,
a subscription shall be :
(a) Irrevocable for a period of one year from the date
of signing except as provided in subsection ( 2 ) of this section ;
(b ) Revocable after a period of one year from the date
of signing , unless prior to such revocation a certificate of in-
corporation has been issued as provided in KRS 271.055 .
(2 ) Subscription for shares may be revoked at any time
by either party upon such grounds as exist at law or in equity
for the rescission of any contract .

subscriptions for shares may be enforced by the corporation
according to their terms unless revoked as provided in this
section .

271.085 Minimum amount of capital . The minimum
amount of capital with which a corporation may begin busi-
ness shall not be less than one thousand dollars in cash or

271.175 to 271.195 .
271.095 . Conditions precedent to beginning business ;
liability in case of violation of section . ( 1 ) A corporation
formed under this chapter shall not incur any debts or begin
the transaction of any business , except such as is incidental
to its organization or to the obtaining of subscriptions to or
the payment for its shares , until :
( a ) An original of the articles of incorporation has been
filed for record in the office of the clerk of the county court
as provided in KRS 271.055 .
(b) The amount of capital with which it will begin busi-
ness , as stated in the articles of incorporation , has been fully
paid in ;
( c ) The first board of directors has been elected by the
shareholders ; and
(d) The corporation has complied with KRS 271.385 .
( 2) If a corporation has transacted any business in vio-
lation of this section , the officers who participated therein
and the directors , except those who dissented therefrom and
caused their dissent to be filed at the time in the registered
office of the corporation , or who , being absent , so filed their
dissent upon learning of the action , shall be severally liable
for the debts or liabilities of the corporation arising there-
from .
271.105 Validity and effect of certificate of incorpora-
tion. The certificate of incorporation issued by the Secretary
of State in accordance with the provisions of KRS 271.055
shall be conclusive evidence of the fact that the corporation
has been incorporated . Proceedings may , however , be insti-
tuted by the state to dissolve , wind up and terminate a cor-
poration which should not have been formed under this chap-
ter , or which has been formed without a substantial compli-
ence with the conditions precedent to incorporation pre-
scribed by this chapter .

271.115 . Effect of filing or recording papers required to
be filed . The purpose of the provisions of this chapter re-
quiring the filing or recording of the articles of incorporation ,
amendments thereto , and other papers , is to afford all persons
the opportunity of acquiring knowledge of the contents there-
of, but no person dealing with the corporation shall be
charged with constructive notice of the contents of any such
articles or papers by reason of such filing or recording .
271.125 Corporate powers . Each corporation formed
under the provisions of this chapter shall have power :
( 1 ) To have a corporate seal and alter it at pleasure ;
( 2 ) To continue as a corporation for the time limited in
its articles of incorporation , or , if no such time limit is speci-
fied then perpetually ;
( 3 ) To sue and be sued in its corporate name ;
(4) To contract and be contracted with ;
( 5 ) To acquire , hold , sell , dispose of , pledge or mort-
gage any such property as its purposes may require , subject
to any limitation prescribed by law or the articles of incor-
poration ;
(6) To elect and appoint such officers and agents as the
business of the corporation shall require , and to remove them ,
to define their duties and fix their compensation and to re-
quire a bond for the faithful discharge of their duties ;
(7) To make bylaws not inconsistent with law ;
( 8) To conduct the business and promote the purposes
for which it was formed in this state and elsewhere as may be
permitted by law ;
(9 ) To dissolve and wind up as provided in KRS
271.495 to 271.545 ;
( 10 ) To acquire , hold , mortgage , pledge or dispose of
the shares , bonds , securities and other evidences of indebted-
ness of any domestic or foreign corporation ;
(11 ) To indemnify its directors and officers as set out
in KRS 271.375 ;
( 12 ) To accept gifts and to carry out any terms , re-
quests or conditions prescribed or made by the donor thereof .
271.135 Acquisition of own shares . Every corporation.
organized under this chapter shall have the power to acquire ,

CHAPTER 141 389
own shares , except that no such corporation shall use its
funds or property for the purchase of its own shares when
such use would cause any impairment of the capital of the .
corporation . Shares of its own capital stock belonging to the
corporation shall not be voted upon directly or indirectly .
Notwithstanding the foregoing limitations , a corporation may
acquire its own shares for the purpose of :
( 1 ) Eliminating fractional shares ;
(2 ) Collecting or compromising claims of the corpora-
tion , or securing any indebtedness to the corporation previ-
ously incurred ;
(3 ) Paying dissenting shareholders entitled to payment
for their shares in the event of a sale or exchange of assets or
a merger or consolidation ; or
(4 ) Effecting the retirement of its shares by redemp-
tion or purchase , if acquisition for this purpose would not
render the corporation incapable of paying its debts in the
ordinary course of business .
271.145 . Limitations on business and holding of real es-
tate; escheat procedure . ( 1 ) No corporation shall engage
in business other than that expressly authorized by its articles
of incorporation , or the law under which it was organized , nor
shall it hold any real estate , except such as may be proper and
necessary for carrying on its legitimate business , for a longer
period than five years , under penalty of escheat .
( 2 ) Any action to escheat real estate held contrary to
this section shall be brought in the name of the state in the cir-
cuit court of the county in which any part of it is situated . Be-
fore any such action may be instituted , the Attorney -General
or the Department of Revenue shall give written notice to
the corporation that, unless the property is disposed of within
two years after receipt of the notice , an action will be com-
menced to forfeit the real estate . No such action shall be
brought before that time . The notice shall contain a brief de-
scription of the real estate , and shall be served as provided
by Section 628 of the Civil Code . The serving of the notice
shall not invalidate or impair the corporation's title to the
real estate , nor abridge its right to convey it before the action ,
nor affect the title of the person to whom conveyed .

for in order to fix the extent of the outstanding obligation of
a shareholder to the corporation with respect to such shares ,
the following valuation shall be prima facie correct unless the

271.205 Validity of shares . The fact that shares are

CHAPTER 141 393
271.215 Liability of incorporators , subscribers , share-
holders, directors and officers . ( 1 ) A subscriber to or holder
of shares of a corporation formed under this chapter shall
be under no liability to the corporation with respect to such
shares other than the obligation of complying with the terms
of subscription therefor ; but one who became a shareholder
in good faith and without knowledge or notice that the shares
he acquired had not been fully paid for , shall not be liable
to the corporation with respect to such shares .
( 2 ) A shareholder of a corporation formed under this
chapter shall not be personally liable for any debt or liability
of the corporation .
( 3 ) No person holding shares in any fiduciary capacity
shall be personally liable merely by reason of so holding such
shares , but the estate in his hands shall be liable , in an action
against the fiduciary , in the same manner as the property of
other shareholders .
(4) Nothing in this chapter is intended to be in deroga-
tion of any rights which any person may have under the
common law or the principles of equity against an incor-
porator , subscriber , shareholder , director , officer or the cor-
poration because of any fraud practiced upon him by any
such persons , or the corporation ; or in derogation of any
rights which the corporation may have because of any fraud
practiced on it by any of such persons .
271.225 Transfer of shares . The transfer of shares of
stock may be regulated by the bylaws provided such bylaws
are not inconsistent with the provisions of Chapter 274 of the
Kentucky Revised Statutes .
271.235 Corporation's lien on shares . If any stock-
holder fails to pay any installment on the stock as provided
in the subscription for same or when requested by the
directors , it may be collected by action or the corporation may
sell enough of the shares of such delinquent by public sale
at the registered office to pay the amount due , with costs and
interest . Before selling the shares of the delinquent the cor-
poration shall give him twenty days ' written notice , by per-
sonal service if he resides in the county , or by mail to his
last known address if he does not live in the county . The
notice shall state the time and place at which the shares will
be sold . If no bidder can be found to pay the amount due on

fifth of the voting power of all shareholders , it shall be the
duty of the secretary to call a special meeting of the share-
holders to be held at the registered office at such time as the
secretary may fix , not less than ten nor more than thirty-
five days after the receipt of said request , and if the secre-
tary shall neglect or refuse to issue such call, the director
or shareholder or shareholders making the request may
do so .
( 3 ) An adjournment or adjournments of any annual
or special meeting may be taken without new notice being
given , but any meeting at which directors are to be elected
shall be adjourned only from day to day until such directors
have been elected .
(4 ) Persons authorized to call shareholders ' meetings
shall cause written notice of the time , place and purpose of
the meeting to be given all shareholders entitled to vote at
such meetings , at least ten days prior to the day named for
the meeting , unless otherwise provided in the articles of in-
corporation or in the bylaws . If such written notice is placed
in the United States mail , postage prepaid , and addressed to
a shareholder at his address appearing on the books of the
corporation , notice shall be deemed to have been given him .
Notice of any shareholders ' meeting may be waived in writ-
ing by any shareholder at any time before or after the
meeting .
271.315 Voting Rights . ( 1 ) Except as otherwise pro-
vided in the articles of incorporation , every shareholder of
record shall have the right at every shareholders ' meeting
to one vote for every share standing in his name on the books
of the corporation . Unless the articles or bylaws otherwise
provide , the board of directors may fix a time not exceeding
forty days preceding the date of any meeting of share-
holders , or the date fixed for the payment of any dividend or
distribution , or the date for the allotment of rights , or , sub-
ject to contract rights with respect thereto , the date when
any change or conversion or exchange of shares shall be made
or go into effect , as a record date for the determination of

( 3 ) Every shareholder shall have the right to cast his
vote either in person or by proxy duly authorized in writing
and filed with the secretary . Except as may be provided

any other agreement or any provision in the proxy to the con-

eleven months after the date of its execution unless some

therein, but in no event shall a proxy , unless coupled with an
interest, be voted on after three years from the date of its
execution . The revocation of a proxy shall not be effective
until the secretary of the corporation has received notice of
such revocation .

271.395 Corporate books and records ; share register ;
right of shareholders to inspect books . ( 1 ) Every domestic
corporation shall keep at its registered office a share register
or duplicate giving the names of the shareholders in alpha-
betical order , and showing their respective addresses , the
number and classes of shares held by each , and the dates on
which they acquired the same .
( 2 ) Every domestic corporation shall keep appropriate
and complete books of account , and records of the proceedings
of shareholders and directors .
(3) A corporation may open a share register in any
state of the United States . It may employ an agent to keep
such register and to record transfers of shares therein , in this
or any other state , or both , and the acts of such agent shall
be binding on the corporation . The duties and liabilities of
such agent shall be such as may be agreed to by the corpora-
tion .
(4 ) Every shareholder shall have a right to examine , in
person or by agent or attorney , at any reasonable times , for
any proper corporate purpose , the share register or duplicate ,
and the books of account and records of the proceedings of
the shareholders and directors , and to make extracts there-
from .

quired in KRS 271.415 and 271.470 , whenever it is provided
in this chapter that corporate action may be adopted at a
meeting of the shareholders called for that purpose , such
corporate action may be adopted without a meeting , if all the
shareholders who would have been entitled to vote upon the
action , if such meeting were held , shall consent in writing to
such corporate action being taken .
271.415 Voluntary transfer of corporate assets . ( 1 ) A
voluntary sale , lease or exchange of all the assets of a corpora-
tion may be authorized by it upon such terms and conditions
as it deems expedient , including an exchange in whole or in
part for shares in another corporation , domestic or foreign .
(2) If the corporation is able to meet its liabilities then

shareholders , duly called for the purpose , and by a vote of
the holders of a majority of the voting power of all share-
holders entitled to vote or by such vote as may be provided

271.440 Sale of corporate assets under court decree .
Sales of the assets of a corporation under court decree shall
be made on such credits , after such notice or advertisement ,
at such time and place , and by such person , as the court con-
siders proper and may direct . If a sale is made in the
enforcement of a mortgage or deed of trust , the court may
order it made for the whole amount of the outstanding bonds
or notes secured by the deed of trust or mortgage ; or if the
assets will bring that much , then for the amount of interest
due under the deed of trust or mortgage , subject to the pay-
ment by the purchaser of the outstanding bonds or notes and
subsequently accruing interest as they become due . In the
latter event , the court may require the purchaser to secure
the assumption of this payment . If the assets do not bring
enough to pay the interest due , the court shall direct the
officer making the sale to sell the assets free from encum-
brances .
271.445 Amendment of articles of incorporation . ( 1 ) A
corporation may , at a meeting of the shareholders duly called
upon notice of the specific purpose , and in the manner pro-
vided in this section and in KRS 271.450 and 271.455 , amend
its articles in any respect so as to include any provision
authorized by this chapter , or so as to add to its corporate
purposes or to diminish the same , or both , or substitute other
purposes in whole or in part for those prescribed in the
original articles or any previous amendment thereto , or so
as to extend the period of its duration for a further definite
time or perpetually .
( 2 ) An amendment of the articles of incorporation may
be adopted by vote of the holders of a majority of the voting
power of all shareholders entitled to vote or by such vote as
the articles of incorporation may require , which shall not be
less than the vote of the holders of a majority of such voting
power .
(3) If an amendment would alter or change the prefer-
ences , special rights or powers given to the holders of shares
of any class or classes so as to affect the same adversely , or
would increase or decrease the number of or the par value
of shares of any class or classes , then the holders of each

of each of the constituent corporations into or for shares or

other details and provisions as are deemed necessary .

remaining assets or proceeds shall be distributed among its
shareholders according to their respective rights and inter-
ests . The order appointing a liquidating receiver shall state
his powers and duties . Such powers and duties may be in-
creased or diminished at any time during the proceedings . A
receiver of a corporation appointed under the provisions of
this subsection shall have authority to sue and defend in all
courts in his own name as receiver of such corporation , and
the court appointing the receiver may issue writs in favor
of the receiver to the sheriff of any county in this state for
service .

ings , as hereinafter set forth in this subsection . When a com-

271.580 Disposition of distributive portions due share-
holders who are unknown or cannot be found . ( 1 ) Where , in

shareholders , a shareholder entitled to a distributive portion
is unknown or cannot be found , the directors of the corpora-
tion or the receiver , as the case may be , having control of

the Department of Revenue . In the event distribution is
made other than in cash , the distributive portion shall be re-
duced to cash before being so deposited . At the time of mak-

giving the name of the shareholder , if known , his last known
address , the amount of the distributive portion , and such
other information as the department may require . The de-
partment shall mail a copy of such report to the sheriff of
the county in which the registered office of the corporation
was located , at the time reports of abandoned property are
next mailed to sheriffs under the provisions of KRS 393.110 ,
and the sheriff shall post and publish the report , and certify
the fact thereof , the same as provided in KRS 393.110 for re-
ports of abandoned property .
( 2 ) Sums deposited with the Department of Revenue
pursuant to subsection ( 1 ) of this section shall be promptly
paid into the State Treasury by the department .

classification.title: Disposition of distributive portions due shareholders who are unknown or cannot be found
classification.summary: This statute outlines the procedure for handling distributive portions of a corporation's assets when a shareholder is unknown or cannot be located. It mandates that such portions be deposited with the Department of Revenue, reduced to cash if necessary, and accompanied by a report detailing the shareholder's information. The Department of Revenue is then to notify the sheriff of the corporation's registered office county, who will post and publish the report. The deposited sums are to be paid into the State Treasury, and shareholders retain rights to recover their portions.

and all of the provisions of said sections shall be applicable .
(4) If a distributive portion is not deposited with the
Department of Revenue as provided in subsection ( 1 ) of this
section , the directors of the corporation or the receiver , as
the case may be , having control of the distribution , shall be
jointly and severally liable to the shareholder for the amount
of such portion not so deposited .
271.585 Survival of remedy after dissolution . The dis-
solution of a corporation , either by the issuance of a cer-
tificate of dissolution by the Secretary of State , or by the
decree of a court when the court has not liquidated the assets
and business of the corporation , or by expiration of its period
of duration , shall not take away or impair any remedy avail-
able to or against such corporation , its directors , or share-
holders , for any right or claim existing , or any liability in-
curred, prior to such dissolution , if suit thereon is commenced
within two years after the date of such dissolution . Any
such suit by or against the corporation may be prosecuted
or defended by the corporation in its corporate name .
271.590 Revocation of corporate powers. If any corpo-
ration fails to comply with any provision of its articles or
certificate of incorporation under which it does business in
this state , or is guilty of any abuse or misuse of any corpo-
rate power , privilege or franchise , or becomes detrimental
to the interest and welfare of the state or its citizens , the
Attorney -General may institute proceedings necessary and
proper to revoke its corporate powers . In any such proceed-
ings , the court may grant the relief asked for , or such other
or partial relief as to it seems just and expedient .
271.595 Requirements as to bond . If bond is required
to be executed before using any corporate power , franchise
or privilege , no corporation shall use any such power , fran-
chise or privilege without first executing such bond .
271.600 Charters and grants since 1856 may be repeal-
ed . All corporate charters granted and grants made to
corporations since February 14 , 1856 , may be revoked
by the General Assembly , unless a contrary intent is plainly
expressed ; but no revocation or repeal shall impair other
rights previously vested .

CHAPTER 141 425
271.605 Secondary action by shareholders . In a suit
brought to enforce a secondary right on the part of one or
more shareholders of a corporation , because the corpo-
ration has refused to enforce rights which may properly be
asserted by it, the corporation by such shareholder or share-
holders shall be the plaintiff (For example : X Corporation
by John Doe , Shareholder ) and the petition shall allege the
ownership of shares by such shareholder or shareholders
at the time of the transaction complained of or that such
ownership devolved on such shareholder or shareholders by
operation of law. The petition shall also set forth with
particularity the effort of such shareholder or shareholders
to secure from the directors and , if necessary , from the other
shareholders the desired action , and the reasons for failure
to obtain such action or the reasons for not making such
effort to obtain such action .
271.610 Service of process on foreign corporation after
withdrawal from state , or in case of unauthorized doing of
business in state . ( 1 ) Any foreign corporation authorized
to do business in this state that desires to withdraw from
the state shall file with the Secretary of State a written
notice of its intent to withdraw , stating the date as of which
withdrawal will be made and the postoffice address of its
home office . For the purpose of any cause of action aris-
ing in this state in which this state is the plaintiff , brought
at any time after such withdrawal date , and for the pur-
pose of any cause of action arising in this state in which
a person other than this state is the plaintiff , brought
within four years after such withdrawal date , the Secre-
tary of State shall constitute an agent for the service of
process upon the corporation .
( 2) Any foreign corporation that does business in
this state without having complied with the provisions of
KRS 271.385 as to designation of process agent shall , by
such doing of business , be deemed to have made the Secre-
tary of State its agent for the service of process in any
civil action instituted in the courts of this state against
such corporation involving a cause of action arising out of or
connected with the doing of business by such corporation in
this state . The plaintiff in such an action shall set forth in
his petition the postoffice address of the home office of the
corporation .

is repealed , and a new section , to carry the same number ,
is created , reading as follows :
271.990 Penalties . ( 1 ) Any corporation that does
business in this state without complying with the provisions
of this chapter that require certain things to be done be-
fore doing business , and any officer , director or agent of
the corporation who knowingly participates in such un-
authorized doing of business , shall , for each offense , be
fined not less than one hundred dollars nor more than one
thousand dollars .
(2 ) Any corporation that violates any provision of
this chapter , and any officer , director or agent of the corpo-
ration who is responsible for such violation or who know-
ingly participates therein , shall, for each offense , be fined
not less than one hundred dollars nor more than one thou-
sand dollars .

296.135 Double liability of stockholder . ( 1 ) Stock-
holders in guaranty companies and insurance companies
shall be liable equally and ratably , and not one for the
other , for all contracts and liabilities of the company to
the extent of the amount of their stock at par value , in
addition to the amount of such stock .
(2 ) Persons holding stock as fiduciaries shall not be
personally liable as stockholders , but the estates in their
hands shall be liable the same as the property of other
stockholders .
(3) No transfer of stock shall release any such liability
cxisting at the time of the transfer if the action to enforce
such liability is commenced within two years from the time
of transfer .
(4 ) The liability of stockholders provided for by this
scction may be enforced as provided in KRS 287.365 for
stockholders of banks and trust companies .
9. A new section of the statutes is created , to em-
brace certain provisions formerly contained in sections
271.180 and 271.190 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , said
section to be numbered section 291.115 and to read as
follows :
291.115 Double liability of stockholders . ( 1 ) Stock-
holders in investment companies shall be liable equally and
ratably , and not one for the other , for all contracts and
liabilities of the company to the extent of the amount of
their stock at par value , in addition to the amount of such
stock .

wealth of Kentucky :
Notwithstanding the provisions of KRS 70.560 , as
amended by House Bill No. 73 of the 1946 General Assembly,
the maximum salary that may be paid an Assistant Chief of
the county police force in a county containing a population

collectors for the tax levying authorities that make the school

as the tax collector of the school district or districts for

(2) An independent school district may select a special

independent school district does so select a special tax col-
lector, a majority of the members of the independent school

tax collector at a rate of not less than 1 % and nor more than

special tax collector from the local school levy in such inde-
pendent school district . The special tax collector shall be
required to execute bond in the same manner as provided in
KRS 160.560 for the execution of a treasurer's bond , and the
penal sum of the bond shall not be less than the aggregate

collector .
( 3) The clerk shall include all school taxes on the regu-
lar tax bills furnished the tax collector except in case an inde-
pendent district has selected a special tax collector , in which
case the school taxes shall be listed by the clerk on a separate

for each separate school tax bill , to be paid by the independ-
ent district board of education .

442 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
providing for civil liability and penalties for violations hereof .
This Act shall be known and cited as the Ollie Montgomery Act.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. ( 1 ) Any person ( other than a person appointed
for a definite term and those persons who received a dis-
honorable discharge ) who has heretofore entered , or shall
hereafter enter , service in the Armed Forces of the United
States in the war with foreign powers which began on Decem-
ber 8 , 1941 , and who , at the time of entering such service , was
employed in a position in any agency or department of the
state government , or any political subdivision thereof , shall ,
upon being discharged from such service , be forthwith re-
employed for a period of not less than one year at the same
status in the same position in which he was employed at the
time he entered service , or if such position no longer exists ,
in an existing or created position of equal class and com-
pensation rate .
Salary increments which would have normally accrued
if the veteran had remained in the state service shall be added
to the salary he was receiving at the time he entered service
in the armed forces . If more than one person has entered
the Armed Forces from the same position in any agency or
department of the State Government , or any political sub-
division thereof , and each of such persons desires reemploy-
ment in such position , the one with seniority in time of
service shall be reemployed in such position and the other
shall be employed in the order of their entering military
service .
( 2 ) Any person entitled to reemployment under the
provisions of this act who , upon application , is not reem-
ployed as required by this act shall have a civil action for
damages against the officer having the power of appointment
to such position , and said action shall be brought in the courts
of the county where the office of the person violating this act
is located . In addition to the civil liability , any officer having
the power of appointment to a position in which any person
is entitled to reemployment under this section , who fails ,
upon due application , to make such reemployment , shall be
fined not less than One Hundred Dollars nor more than Five
Hundred Dollars , in the courts of the county where the
offense is committed .
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1946, p. 459

classification.title: Ollie Montgomery Act
classification.summary: This act, known as the Ollie Montgomery Act, mandates the reemployment of individuals who entered service in the Armed Forces of the United States during the war with foreign powers (beginning December 8, 1941). Upon discharge, these individuals are to be reemployed in their former positions or equivalent ones within state government or political subdivisions, with provisions for salary increments and seniority in cases of multiple applicants for the same position. It also outlines civil liability and penalties for officers who fail to comply with these reemployment requirements.
classification.reasoning: The statute concerns the reemployment rights of veterans returning from military service. It does not contain any language that explicitly or implicitly discriminates based on race, nor does it establish separate facilities or differential treatment for any racial group. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law. The document type is 'session_laws' as indicated by 'ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY' and the year 1946. The confidence is high because the statute's purpose is clearly defined and has no apparent racial component.

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 21.260 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
The Court of Appeals , the Chief Justice being chairman ,
shall constitute a board of commissioners for the purpose of
having the opinions of the court published . The commissioners
shall in April 1946 , advertise , by publication in the paper hav-
ing the largest circulation in each of the cities of Frankfort ,
Lexington and Louisville , for sealed bids for printing and
binding sixteen volumes of the Kentucky Reports beginning
with volume 302 to and in including volume 317. The contract
shall be let to the lowest and best bidder, and shall contain
such conditions and provisions as the commissioners desire to
incorporate in it , including the number of copies of each vol-
ume to be printed . All subsequent contracts shall be adver-
tised on the date actual printing of the last volume authorized
in the preceeding contract begins and shall be awarded as
provided herein . The person to whom the contract is let shall
enter into a bond to be fixed by the commissioners for the
faithful performance of his duties ; notify the commissioners ,
in writing , on the date actual printing of the last volume be-
gins in order that they may advertise and award subsequent
contracts as provided herein ; and the commissioners may ,
upon the failure or refusal of the contractor to perform his
contract , or any part thereof , cancel said contract and re - let
the same for the unexecuted portion thereof after due ad-
vertisement .

utes is amended to read as follows :

classification.title: AN ACT relating to the registration and purgation of voters. This Act shall be known as the Trivette-Waggoner Act.
classification.summary: This act mandates the use of rubber stamps with the words "PURGATION RECOMMENDED" and a space for "Reason" and signatures of election judges. These stamps are to be provided to each precinct, except those within a first-class city, for use during primary elections. The purpose is to facilitate the process of recommending the purgation (removal) of voters from the rolls.
classification.reasoning: The statute itself does not explicitly mention race. However, the 'Trivette-Waggoner Act' is historically known to be a Jim Crow-era law in Kentucky that was used to disenfranchise Black voters. The mechanism of 'purgation recommended' with specific stamping and signature requirements, while seemingly procedural, was a tool employed to target and remove Black voters from registration lists under the guise of administrative efficiency. Therefore, it is classified as a Jim Crow law with an implicit racial indicator. Human review is recommended to confirm the historical context and specific implementation of this act in relation to racial disenfranchisement.

cepting those precincts within the limits of a city of the first
class , the stamp and a sufficient quantity of registration sup-
plies and forms to enable registration of a number of voters
equal to at least five percent of the voters then on the regis-

2. Notwithstanding the provisions of KRS 117.050 , the
judges of election in each precinct excepting those precincts
within the limits of a city of the first class , shall register each
qualified voter who makes application to them for registra-
tion and who satisfactorily establishes his right to be regis-
tered . The manner of registering shall conform to that pro-
vided by law for registration by the county clerk . No person
registered on the day of a primary election shall be permitted
to vote in such election or at any election thereafter until the
following regular election in November . The judges of elec-
tion shall deliver the new registrations to the county clerk
immediately following the primary election , and he shall enter
them in the regular registration books .
3. It shall be the duty of the judges of election at each
primary election in all precincts excepting those precincts
within the limits of a city of the first class to recommend pur-
gation of the registration lists by stamping with the rubber
stamp provided in Section 1 of this Act the registration cards
of persons who have since the date of their registration , died ,
moved from the precinct , or otherwise become disqualified to
vote , upon reasonable proof of such disqualification of any
voter . The judges of election shall stamp the registration
record of such voter with the stamp provided for in Section
1 of this Act , and shall each sign their names on the blank
lines below the words " PURGATION RECOMMENDED ."
4. The judges of election may appoint other persons to
assist them in performing their duties under this Act , but the
persons so appointed shall serve without compensation .
5. Within ten days after the primary election day , the
County Board of Election Commissioners shall meet at the
county court clerk's office for the purpose of passing upon
the purgation recommendations made by the precinct judges .
The county court clerk shall meet with the board and act as its
secretary for the purpose . During the time the board is so in
session the members of the board and its secretary shall re-
ceive a reasonable per diem , to be fixed and paid by the fiscal
court, not exceeding the per diem allowed for the services of

classification.title: An Act relating to elections and registration.
classification.summary: This act outlines procedures for voter registration and the purging of registration lists by election judges in primary elections. It specifically excludes precincts within cities of the first class from certain provisions, such as the requirement for judges to register qualified voters who apply and establish their right to be registered. It also details the process for recommending the purgation of registration lists for voters who have died, moved, or become disqualified, with the county board of election commissioners reviewing these recommendations. The exclusion of certain precincts from these registration and purgation duties, particularly in the context of Jim Crow era voting laws, suggests a potential for discriminatory application.
classification.reasoning: The statute deals with voter registration and the purging of voter lists. While it does not explicitly mention race, the exclusion of precincts within 'cities of the first class' from certain registration duties is a common tactic used in Jim Crow laws to disenfranchise Black voters. These larger cities often had a higher concentration of Black residents, and by exempting them from certain registration requirements or oversight, it could lead to discriminatory practices. Therefore, it is classified as a Jim Crow law with implicit racial indicators. The confidence is moderate because the discriminatory intent is inferred rather than explicit. Human review is needed to confirm the historical context of 'cities of the first class' in Kentucky and their racial demographics during this period.

fused ." If any voter to whom notice is mailed as provided in
this section fails to appear within five days , the board may
thereupon purge his name without further notice or hearing .

CHAPTER 148
( S. B. 27)
AN ACT relating to the police force in cities of the fourth and fifth
classes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 95.740 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
(1 ) The chief of police and every member of the police
force in cities of the fourth and fifth classes may , whether di-
rected to them or not , execute warrants of arrest , processes ,
subpoenas and attachments for witnesses . They may arrest
as prescribed by law for offenses against ordinances or mu-
nicipal regulations of the city , and shall have the same power
of arrest for offenses against the state as a sheriff .
( 2 ) The chief of police and members of the police force
shall be entitled to the same fees , and the same remedies for
collecting them , that are allowed by law to sheriffs and other
officers for similar services , but the city legislative body may ,
by ordinance , direct that the fees be paid into the city trea-
sury when the chief of police and members of the police force
are employed on a salary .
( 3 ) The chief of police may appoint deputies , by and
with the advice and consent of the city legislative body , who
shall have the same powers and duties as the chief of police
except that they shall not command the police force .

CHAPTER 149
( S. B. 28 )
AN ACT amending Sections 41.290 and 45.140 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes , relating to the financial administration of the State .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
1. Section 41.290 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
Every department , institution or other agency of the
state government having private funds or contributions avail-
able for its support or for the purpose of defraying the ex-
penses of any work done under its direction shall deposit such
funds or contributions with the Treasurer , through the De-
partment of Finance , and shall certify to the Department of
Finance the sources from which the funds or contributions
were received , the terms and conditions under which , and the
purpose for which, they were received , the names of the trus-
tees or administrators of the funds or contributions , and the
name of the person authorized to approve expenditures from
each fund or contribution . The Treasurer shall keep each such
fund or contribution entirely separate and distinct from all
other funds . All disbursements from such funds and contri-
butions shall be made by the Treasurer on the warrants of the
Department of Finance , which shall issue such warrants only
upon adequate vouchers approved by the person authorized to
approve the expenditures . Separate sets of accounts with each
of such funds and contributions , and the receipts and dis-
bursements thereof , shall be maintained by the Department
of Finance . This section does not apply to private funds of
students in state educational institutions nor to the private
funds of patients or prisoners in state charitable or correc-
tional institutions , when such funds are deposited with an of-

2. Section 45.140 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) Revolving fund accounts may be established by ap-
propriations for financing the operations of industries , farms ,
hospitals , sanatoria , dining halls , dormitories , book stores , re-
freshment booths , athletics or other activities self -supporting
in whole or in part , or by depositing the operating receipts of
such activities and agencies in the State Treasury to the
credit of a revolving fund account , or by both methods . The
head of the budget unit , the treasurer or other authorized
fiscal agent of the unit or any division thereof responsible
for the collection of receipts shall deposit with the State
Treasurer all receipts from the operations of all such activi-
ties so financed and also receipts from other similar sources ,
including tuition and incidental fees , and the State Treasurer
shall credit all such funds to a revolving fund account for the
budget unit or division thereof, as directed in writing by the
head of the budget unit or division thereof . The Department
of Finance shall keep a separate account for each revolving
fund .
( 2) The amounts credited to any revolving fund so pro-
vided , together with any amount appropriated therefor , may
be expended for the purposes of the fund without further
specific appropriation . All money received for the fund , ex-
cept direct appropriations from the state , shall at all times be
reserved in the State Treasury available for disbursement
for the purpose of the fund in the manner provided by law
and shall not be diverted to any other purpose , except that
such amounts as are over and above maintenance and operat-
ing costs of activities and agencies so financed and direct
appropriations by the state to the revolving fund may , upon
the order of the Department of Finance , be paid into the ordi-
nary recurring expense fund of the budget unit or division
thereof . Revolving funds shall be subject to withdrawal from
the State Treasury by the head of each budget unit or division
thereof when actually needed , on requisition to the Depart-
ment of Finance in the same manner as other state funds are
withdrawn .
Passed February 7 , enrolled February 8 , approved February 20 , 1946 .

CHAPTER 150
(S. B. 31 )
AN ACT to amend Section 273.090 Kentucky Revised Statutes relating
to holding of real estate by religious societies .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
That Section 273.090 K.R.S. be amended and re-enacted
to read as follows :
I.
No religious society may take or hold legal or equitable
title to more than fifty (50 ) acres of ground for a longer
period than one year under penalty of escheat .
II.
Any action to escheat real estate held contrary to this
Act shall be brought in the name of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky in the Circuit Court of the County in which any
part of it is situated . Before any such action may be insti-
tuted , the Attorney General or the Department of Revenue
shall give written notice to the religious society taking or
holding the title to the real estate that, unless the property
is disposed of within one year after receipt of the notice , an
action will be commenced to forfeit such real estate . No such
action shall be brought before that time . The notice shall
contain a brief description of the real estate and shall be
served as provided by Sections 625 to Section 628 of the Civil
Code . The service of this notice shall not invalidate or im-
pair the title of such religious society to the real estate and
shall not abridge its right to convey the real estate before
the action is filed.
III .
All Acts or parts of Acts inconsistent with the provisions
of this Act are hereby repealed , to be extent of such conflict .
Passed February 14 , enrolled February 19 , approved March 1 , 1946 .

AN ACT transferring balances of funds of toll free bridges to Sinking

wealth of Kentucky :

directed to transfer to the Highway Bridge Bond Sinking

equal proportions , the balances in the Highway Bridge Bond

CHAPTER 153
(S. B. 39 )
AN ACT relating to the mining of clay .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
1. ( 1 ) As used in this Act , unless the context requires
otherwise :
(a ) "Chief " means Chief of the Department of Mines
and Minerals .
(b) " Commercial mine " means any clay mine from
which the product is mined for sale or exchange .
(c) " Department " means the Department of Mines and
Minerals .
(d ) " Drift " means an opening through the strata , with
opening grades such as to permit clay to be hauled by mules
or mechanical traction power , and capable of being used for
the purpose of ventilation , drainage , ingress , egress and other
purposes in connection with mining operations .
(e) "Gaseous Mine " means a mine in which the per-
centage of explosive gas exceeds one per cent at the return
of any one split of ventilation .
(f) " Mine " means a group or system of shafts , slopes
drifts or inclined planes connected by one general system of
mine railroads over which clay may be delivered to one or
more points outside the mine , and connected with excavations
that penetrate clay strata and are ventilated by one general
air current or division thereof.

ner that seems to it just and reasonable and necessary or de-

this Act .

5. ( 1 ) The Chief of the Department of Mines and
Minerals shall have full authority over the department and
shall superintend and direct the activities of the district
mine inspectors and other personnel of the department .
( 2 ) He may , whenever necessary , divide the clay fields
of the state into as many inspection districts as there are dis-
trict mine inspectors , so as to equalize as nearly as practicable
the work of each inspector , and may assign to the inspectors
their respective districts .
(3) He may , whenever he deems it necessary in the in-
terest of efficient supervision of the mines , temporarily em-
ploy the service of two or more mine inspectors or change
inspectors from one district to another .
(4) He shall superintend and direct the inspection of
mines and cause to be investigated the character and quality
of air in mines whenever conditions indicate the necessity of
doing so.
(5 ) He shall collect statistics relating to clay mining in
the state and make an annual report of such statistics .
(6 ) He shall see that maps , plans , projections and pro-
posed developments of all clay mines are made and filed in
his office .
(7) He shall keep a properly indexed , permanent record
of all inspections made by himself and the personnel of the
department .

6. ( 1 ) The Board of Examiners for the certification
of clay mine foremen shall consist of the following members :
( a ) The Chief of the Department of Mines and Min-
erals ;
(b) Any two district mine inspectors , the selection of
whom is optional with the chief, who may select them alter-
nately or otherwise ;
(c) One miner with a minimum of seven years ' practi-
cal expenience in the mining of clay, four years , at least , of
which experience have been in the mines of this state , and who
is actually employed in mining clay at the time of the appoint-
ment ; and
(d) One operator who at the time of his appointment is
actually engaged in operating a clay mine .
(2 ) Each member of the board except the chief shall be
recommended by the chief and appointed by the Governor for
a term of four years .
(3) The Chief of the Department of Mines and Minerals
shall be chairman of the board and whenever it is necessary
for him to be absent from any meeting of the board he may
designate any other member to preside during his absence .
A majority of the members shall constitute quorum , with
authority to transact business , hold examinations and issue
certificates . The chairman shall keep a record of all proceed-
ings of the board , including the names and addresses of per-
sons who have been granted certificates and of those who have
failed to pass the examination .

with a duplicate certificate . Any such person applying for a

10. Each district mine inspector shall give his entire
time and attention to the duties of his office , which shall con-
sist of the following :
( 1 ) Inspecting clay mines and siding under the direction
of the chief of the department in carrying out and enforcing
the provisions of the laws relating to the inspection of clay
mines ;
( 2 ) Training officials and workmen in and about the
mines in first aid and mine rescue methods ;
(3) Advising officials and workmen in methods per-
taining to safety in all its phases and in methods pertaining to
the prevention of mine fires and explosions ;
(4) Taking charge of mine rescue and recovery work
whenever a mine fire , mine explosion or other serious acci-
dent occurs within his district , and the chief is not present ,
and assisting in such work in other districts when so directed
by the chief ;
(5 ) Reopening mines of portions of mines that have
been sealed on account of fire or any other cause , when so
called upon by the owner or operator and directed by the chief
to do so ; and
( 6 ) Visiting each mine in his district at least once every
three months , or oftener if practicable , and whenever any
danger to the workmen may exist , making a personal exam-
ination of the interior of each mine with respect to ventila-
tion , drainage , timering , blasting, electricity and general se-
curity , and also a personal examination of the outside of the
mine .
11. ( 1 ) The chief of the department shall annually
make a report to the Governor of his activities during the
preceding calendar year . The annual report shall be prepared
and printed as soon as possible within the four months after
the close of the calendar year . The annual report shall cover
the complete operation of the clay mines in this state during
the preceding calendar year , enumerating all accidents occur-
ring in or about such mine during that year , and giving such
other information as the chief deems useful , and making such
suggestions as he deems important as to further legislation
on the subject of mining .

15. ( 1 ) The ventilation of all clay mines at which five
or more men are employed shall be produced by means of
mechanically operated fans located outside the mine . Mines
at which less than five men are employed shall be ventilated
by means approved by the Department of Mines and Minerals .
If inside fans are required to ventilate working places on
longwall or modern systems of mining the chief of the De-
partment of Mines and Minerals must first approve the in-
stallation .
( 2) The operator , superintendent or foreman of every
clay mine worked by shaft , slope or drift shall provide and
maintain for every mine adequate ventilation affording at
least 100 cubic feet of air per minute for each person em-
ployed in the mine and at least 200 cubic feed of air per minute
for each animal working in the mine , unless the mine liberates
explosive gas , in which case he shall provide at least 150
cubic feet of air per minute for each person and 300 cubic
feet of air per minute for each animal , and as much more
air as the district inspector deems necessary . The air shall
be circulated around the main headings , cross headings , room
headings , and working places to such an extent as to dilute ,
render harmless and carry off any noxious or dangerous
gases .
(3) As working places advance , breakthroughs for air
shall be made sixty feed apart , except where it is now per-
mitted , with the approval of the district mine inspector and
the chief of the department , to drive to ninety feet before
making breakthroughs , or the method of ventilating such
longwall or modern systems of mining . If any break -throughs
between intake and return airways are not required for the
passage of air , they shall be closed with stoppings . All stop-
pings shall be substantially built with suitable material sub-
ject to the approval of the district mine inspector so as to
keep the working places well ventilated . Doors on main haul-
ways shall be avoided in gaseous mines where practicable ,
and overcasts , built of concrete or other suitable material and
of ample strength , shall be adopted . Where doors are used

it condemned , by proceedings similar to those allowed for
securing a private passway , in the county court of the county
where the mine is situated .
23. (1 ) In every shaft used as an escape outlet there
shall be provided a stairway or ladderway of suitable strength ,
design and angle , with platforms or landings at each turn ,
the stairway or ladderway to be approved by the district
mine inspector and the chief of the department , or in lieu
thereof a properly equipped hoisting arrangement , consist-
ing of a cage suitable for hoisting men , a wire hoisting rope
of ample strength and an adequate and safe hoisting engine
and drum , which hoisting arrangement shall be ready for
use at any time of emergency .
( 2 ) When the escape and fan shafts are combined ,
the side or end used for an escape outlet and the part used
for ventilating purposes shall be separated by a good sub-
stantial air -tight partition from top to bottom , so that the
use of the escape shaft shall not interfere with the entilation
of the mine .
(3 ) All shafts by which men enter or leave the mine
and all passageways to escape outlets shall be carefully
examined by the mine foreman , or by a person designated
by him , at least once each week that the mine is operating,
and the date and finding of the examination shall be entered
in a book kept at the mine for that purpose .
(4 ) No debris , water , timbers or mine cars that would
obstruct the free passage of men shall be allowed to accumu-
late in the passageway to the escape outlet, and if any such
obstructions accumulate or exist they shall be promptly re-
moved .
(5 ) At points where the passageway to the escape out-
let is intersected by roadways or entries calculated to produce
doubt as to the proper direction to the outlet , conspicous
signboards shall be placed indicating the direction to the
escape outlet.
24. ( 1 ) At every mine operated by shaft , there shall
be an approved safety catch , and on all cages used for lower-
ing and hoisting persons there shall be a sufficient and sub-
stantial overhead cover . At the top of each shaft a safety
gate shall be provided . An adequate brake , so adjusted that
it may be operated by the engineer without leaving his post.

at the levers , shall be attached to every drum or equivalent
machine used for lowering or raising persons at any shaft
or slope , and in connection with the hoisting engine an efficient
indicator or dial , which will show the engineer the positions
of the cages in the shaft at any time , shall be used . Ap-
proved means for signaling to the bottom man , the top man
and the engineer shall be provided , and all shafts used for
lowering or hoisting men shall be equipped with metal tubes
or pipes or with telephones , through which conversations
may be freely held between persons at the bottom and top
landings .
(2 ) No cages on which men are riding shall be lifted
or lowered at a rate of speed greater than six hundred feet
per minute.
( 3 ) The hoisting rope , the safety catches and the cage
attachments shall be examined by the mine foreman or by
some competent person designated by him , every morning
before the men descend into the mine , and care shall be taken
to keep thm in good working condition at all times .
(4 ) No person shall ride on any cage when clay , slate
or similar material is being raised .
(5) No person shall enter a cage at the bottom to be
raised to the top during the running hours of the mine , or
when leaving work at the close of the days ' run , without first
being authorized by the bottom man or cager to do so , the
bottom man or cager having first signaled to the engineer
that men are to be raised .
(6) At every underground landing where persons enter
or leave the cage and where persons must pass from one side
of the shaft to the other there shall be a passageway not less
than three feet wide and of suitable height , kept free from
obstruction and as dry as possible , around the shaft ; and all
employees when passing from one side of the shaft to the
other side shall use the passageway only .
(7) Whenever the hoisting or lowering of men occurs
before daylight or after dark , or when the landing at which
men take or leave the cage is obscured by steam or other-
wise , there shall be maintained at each landing a light suf-
ficient to show the landing and objects in immediate prox-
imity thereto , and as long as men are underground a good

470 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
light shall be maintained at the bottom of the shaft , so that
persons coming to the bottom may discern the cage and ob-
ject in the vicinity thereof .
25. ( 1 ) No operator or superintendent or any clay
mine worked by shaft , slope or incline shall place in charge
of any engine or drum used for lowering or hoisting any
persons employed in the mine any but competent and sober
engineers or drum runners .
(2 ) No engineer in charge of such machinery shall
allow any person except such as may be designated for this
purpose by the operator or superintendent to interfere with
any part of the machinery .
(3 ) No person shall interfere with or intimidate the
engineer or drum runner in the discharge of his duties .
(4 ) In a mine worked by shaft , slope or incline , no
more than ten persons shall ride in any cage or car at one
time , without the approval of the district mine inspector and
the chief of the department , and no person shall ride on a
loaded cage or car , except that where special man cars or
cages are employed to haul workmen on inclines the chief
may regulate the method and procedure of handling of man
cars , cages and workmen .
26. ( 1 ) On single -track haulage roads in mines , which
the persons employed in the mine must use while perform-
ing their work or while traveling on foot to and from their
work , there shall be places or refuge on one side not less
than three feet in depth from the side of the mine car , and
four feet wide , and not more than sixty feet apart , where
haulage is done by machines , and not less than thirty inches
in depth from the side of the mine car , and four feet wide , and
not more than sixty feet apart where haulage is done by draft
animals .
( 2 ) Special places of refuge are not required on haul-
age roads on which room necks or break-throughs occur at
regular intervals not exceeding sixty feet , and thus furnish
places of refuge , or on haulage roads in which the track is
so laid as to give a minimum clearance on one side of not less
than thirty inches from the side of any haulage engine or any
mine car , the clearance to be on the side of the road opposite
that upon which electric wires are strung , if electric wires
are strung in the road.

CHAPTER 153 471
(3 ) No person shall travel on foot to or from work
upon any slope used for haulage or upon any haulage road
when other roads in proper condition for travel are available .
(4 ) On all main haulage roads where hauling is done
by machinery the mine foreman shall provide a proper sys-
tem of signals , and a conspicuous light on the front and a
light or marker on the rear of every trip or train or cars when
in motion in the mine .
27. ( 1 ) For every fifty men and fraction thereof em-
ployed underground , the operator of each mine shall keep
on hand at the mine one properly constructed stretcher , one
waterproof woolen blanket , and all other necessary equip-
ment advised by the department .
(2 ) Each operator shall see that all employees receive
the necessary first -aid training for the safety of themselves
and fellow workmen as recommended by the department and
each employee shall accept the necessary training .
29. ( 1 ) Except as provided in subjection ( 2 ) , every
miner shall thoroughly examine the roof and general condi-
tions of his working place before commencing work there ,
and if he finds loose rock or slate or other dangerous con-
ditions that he can remedy he shall take down the loose rock
or slate or securely prop it, and otherwise make his place
safe before doing any other work there , and during the
progress of his work each miner shall carefully watch his
working place , and if dangerous conditions develop shall
either make it safe or abandon the place until it is made safe .
( 2 ) Where the operator elects to do the timbering the
miner shall forsake his working place until the place has
been made safe .
30. No person shall knowingly injure any shaft lamp ,
instrument , air course or brattice , obstruct or throw open
airways , disturb any part of the machinery or appliances ,
open a door used for directing ventilation without closing it
afterwards , enter any part of the mine against caution , dis-
obey any order given in carrying out any of the provisions
of this Act , do any act endangering the security of the mine
or enter or be about any mine while intoxicated or under the
influence of alcohol .

31. ( 1 ) On all haulage roads , landings and partings ,
where men are required to regularly work or pass under bare
power wires placed less than six and one-half feet above the
top of the rail suitable protection shall be provided . This
protection shall consist of channeling the roof , placing boards
along the wires and extending below them , or the use of
some other approved device that affords protection .
(2 ) All machine feed wires shall be placed on glass or
porcelain insulators which shall be so placed as to prevent
the wires coming in contact with the coal or clay .
(3) When machine or feed wires are carried in the same
entry as the trolley wire , they shall be placed on the same side
as the trolley wire , between the trolley wire and rib , and shall
be protected from contact therewith . Positive feed wires
crossing places where persons or animals are required to
travel shall be safely guarded or protected from such persons
or animals coming in contact therewith, as provided in sub-
section (1).
(4 ) All trolley and positive feed wires shall be placed
on opposite sides of track from refuge holes or necks or room ,
when so ordered by the department , but wires , when protected
as provided for in subsection ( 1 ) , may be placed across the
necks of rooms . Switches or circuit breakers shall be provided
to control the current at the mine , and all important sections
in the mine .
(5 ) All power wires and cables in hoisting shafts or
manway compartments shall be properly insulated , substan-
tially fixed and well protected .
32. ( 1 ) No employee shall take into any mine any
larger quantity of powder or other explosives than he may
reasonable expect to use in any one shift , and all explosives
shall be carried into the mine in an approved insulated or
nonconductive receptacle or a capacity not to exceed one
shift's supply , and properly closed with an approved top .
All detonators or electric squibs shall be carried into the mine
in an approved nonconductive receptacle with approved top
and of a capacity not to exceed one shift's supply. Each miner
shall see that all explosives are separated from detonators ,
squibs or fuses by at least twelve feet in all working places .
(2 ) No shots shall be fired in any place known to liber-
ate explosive gas until the place has been properly examined

shots shall be fired in any one place where gas is detected
until the gas has been removed by means of ventilation . After
firing any shot no workman shall return to the working place
until the smoke has cleared away , and before starting to work
he shall make a careful examination as to the condition of the
