( 3 ) No more than one kind of explosive shall be used in
the same drill hole , except in strip mining operations . Every
blasting hole shall be tamped full from the explosive to the
mouth and no inflammable material shall be used for tamping.
Where dynamite or keg powder is used the method and pro-
cedure shall be approved by the district mine inspector and
the chief of the department . No fuses shorter than the depth
of the drill hole shall be used .
(4) No explosives or detonators shall be hauled on any
teip operated by electric haulage motors unless they are en-
closed in nonconducting boxes , containers or cars approved
by the district mine inspector and the chief of the depart-
ment .
(5 ) Trips hauling explosives shall not carry workmen
other than those operating the trip , and explosives shall not
be hauled into or out of the mine within five minutes preced-
ing or following any trips in which men are handled . When
traveling with the air current , the explosive trip shall precede
the man trip ; when traveling against the air current , the man
trip shall precede .
(6 ) If a safer method of transporting and handling ex-
plosives or detonators is made available after proper and
suffieient tests and experiments , the chief may approve the
method , and that method may be used in lieu of the methods
provided in this section .
33. Buildings used for the storage of oils or similar in-
flammable material shall not be located within one hundred
feet of any hoisting or escape shaft or mine opening . Oil and
grease used in mines shall be taken into the mine from day
to day with one day's supply being the maximum .
34. ( 1 ) Where six or more persons are employed in a
commercial clay mine in a period of twenty -four hours, the
operator shall employ a certified mine foreman therein to

chief of the department and to see that the mining law is com-
plied with . If explosive gas can be detected with a flame safety
lamp , the operator shall employ a mine foreman of the first
class . If explosive gas is not present , the operator may employ
a mine foreman of the second class .
( 2 ) When the mine workings become so extensive that
the mine foreman is unable to personally carry out the duties
required of him by law , the operator or superintendent shall
employ a sufficient number of persons having had at least
three years ' experience in clay mines to act as assistants to
the mine foreman , and under his instructions . The mine fore-
man shall be responsible for their conduct in the discharge of
their duties .
35. The mine foreman shall exercise general supervision
over the ventilating apparatus and the airways , traveling
ways , working places , pumps and drainage, and shall see that
as the miners advance their excavations proper break-
throughs are made as required by law to properly ventilate
the mine . He shall see that employees are provided with suf-
ficient props , cap pieces , and timbers of suitable size , which
shall be delivered to the working place when requisitioned by
the employees , and shall see that the props are cut as square
as practicable at both ends and as near as practicable to the
proper length required or designated for the places where
they are to be used . The mine foreman shall see that the water
is drained as nearly as practicable out of the working places ,
and that the working places are kept as free from water ast
practicable during working hours . The owner , lessee , or oper-
ator of each mine shall see that the water drained from the
mine is drained as directly as practicable to the adjacent
streams or water -courses . He shall see that every person em-
ployed to work in the mine is , before beginning to work , in-
structed in the particular danger incident to his work in the
mine and furnished a copy of the rules of the mine . He shall
immediately report all violations of mining laws to the oper-
ator or superintendent , who shall report the violations to the
district mine inspector .
36. The mine foreman shall visit and carefully examine
each working place in the mine each day or oftener while the
mine employees are at work , and shall direct the miners so

classification.title: ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
classification.summary: This section of the Acts of the General Assembly from 1946 details regulations for mine foremen and operators in Kentucky. It outlines the qualifications for mine foremen (first and second class based on the presence of explosive gas), the employment of assistants when mine workings become extensive, and the responsibilities of the mine foreman. These responsibilities include general supervision of ventilation, airways, pumps, and drainage; ensuring proper breakthroughs for ventilation; providing and ensuring the proper use of props and timbers; managing water drainage; instructing new employees on mine dangers and rules; and reporting violations of mining laws. The owner, lessee, or operator is also responsible for ensuring water drained from the mine is directed to adjacent streams. The mine foreman is required to visit and examine each working place daily to ensure it is secured by props or timbers.

CHAPTER 153 475
where necessary . He shall examine as live workings , on regu-
lar inspections , all places in live sections that are temporarily
abandoned . If the mine foreman finds any place to be in a
dangerous condition , he shall not leave the place until it is
made safe , or until the employees working therein are re-
moved until the place is made safe . He shall not allow any
person who may be endangered by the presence of explosive
or noxious gases to enter that portion of the mine until the
gases have been removed . He shall direct and see that all
dangerous places and the entrances to worked -out and aban-
doned places in all mines are properly fenced off across the
openings so that no person can enter , and that danger signals
are posted upon the fencing to warn persons of existing
danger . He shall give prompt attention to the removal of all
dangers reported to him by his assistants , the fire boss or any
person working in the mine , and if it is impracticable to re-
move the danger at once he shall notify every person whose
safety is menaced thereby to remain away from the portion
where the dangerous condition exists . He or his assistants
shall, at lease once every week , travel and examine all the air
courses , roads and openings that give access to old workings
or falls , and make a record of the condition of all places where
danger has been found . The record shall be made with ink or
indelible pencil in the record book provided for that purpose .
37. ( 1 ) The mine foreman shall notify , in writing , the
operator or superintendent of the mine of his inability to
comply with any of the requirements of the mining laws , and
the operator or superintendent shall at once attend to the
matter complained of by the mine foreman so as to enable him
to comply with the mining laws.
( 2 ) The mine foreman shall each days enter and sign
plainly with ink or indelible pencil , in a book provided for
that purpose , a report of the conditions of the mine . The re-
port shall clearly state any danger that comes under his ob-
servation during the day or is reported to him by his assist-
ants or by the fire bosses , and shall state whether or not there
is a proper supply of material on hand for the safe working
of the mine , and whether or not the mining laws are being
complied with . The mine foreman shall each day read care-
fully , and countersign with ink or indelible pencil , all reports
entered in the record book of the fire bosses .

476 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
38. ( 1 ) The mine foreman shall see that break-
throughs are made as required by law , and shall see that ven-
tilation is conducted by means of the break-throughs through
the rooms by means of check doors or curtains placed on the
entries or other suitable places , and shall not permit any room
to be opened in advance of the ventilating current .
( 2 ) At least once a month the mine foreman , with an
anemometer furnished by the operator , shall measure the air
current at the inlet and outlet and at or near the faces of ad-
yanced headings , and shall keep a record of such measure-
ments in a book furnished and having a form prescribed by
the chief of the department .
(3) The mine foreman shall see that signboards direct-
ing the way to outlets or escape ways are conspicuously
placed throughout the mine .
39. The operator, superintendent or mine foreman shall
have bore -holes kept not less than twelve feed in advance of
the face , and , where necessary , on the sides , if the working
places that are being driven are in dangerous proximity to an
abandoned mine , or part thereof , that is suspected of contain-
ing noxious gases , or is filled with water . If there is a map
of the abandoned mine or part thereof, the boreholes shall
be started when the working places have approached within
fifty feet of the abandoned mine or part thereof , but if there
is no such map the boreholes shall be started when the ad-
vanced working places are within one hundred feet of where
the abandoned workings are supposed to be .
40. In case of the death or resignation of a mine fore-
man , the superintendent shall select a competent certified
man , if one is available , and if not , he may temporarily select
any other competent man , who , if approved by the district
mine inspector and the chief of the department , may serve
until the next examination . He shall, while acting as mine
foreman, have the same duties and be liable to the same
penalties .
41. The Board of Examiners may revoke the certificate
of a mine foreman if it is established in the judgment of the
board that the holder has become unworthy to hold the certi-
ficate by reason of violation of law , intemperate habits , in-
capacity , abuse of authority , wilful failure to comply with the
instructions of the district mine inspector to obey the mining

CHAPTER 153 477
laws , or for other just cause . The certificate holder against
whom charges are made may appear before the board and de-
fend against the charges , and shall have five days ' notice in
writing of the charges previous to the hearing .
42. ( 1 ) The operator of any mine may adopt special
rules consistent with this Act , for the government and opera-
tion of his mine , and covering all the work pertaining thereto
in and out of the mine . Before the rules are put into effect
they shall be approved in writing by the chief of the depart-
ment . The rules , when adopted and approved in writing by
the chief, shall be printed on cardboard in the language spoken
by seventy -five per cent or more of the employees at the mine ,
and shall be posted on a bulletin board or some other con-
spicuous place about the mine where they may be seen by the
employees at the mine . When the rules are so adopted , ap-
proved and posted , all employees at the mine shall be deemed
to have notice thereof , and to have agreed thereto , and they
shall obey the rules .
( 2 ) The mine rules shall be printed in a form prescribed
by the chief of the department .
( 3 ) Each mine operator , when requested by an em-
ployee , shall furnish him a copy of the mine rules .
43. ( 1 ) Each superintendent shall , on behalf and at the
expense of the operator , keep on hand at or within convenient
distance of the mine a sufficient quantity of all materials and
supplies required to preserve the safety of employees , as ord-
ered by the mine foreman and required by law . If the superin-
tendent cannot procure the necessary materials or supplies ,
he shall at once notify the mine foreman who shall withdraw
the men from the mine until the materials or supplies are re-
ceived .
( 2 ) The superintendent shall , at least once each week ,
read and examine carefully and countersign all reports en-
tered in the mine record book of the mine foreman .
(3 ) The operator or superintendent shall cooperate
with the mine foreman and other officials in the fulfillment
of their duties under this Act , and shall direct that the mine
foreman and all other employees under him comply with the
law , especially when his attention is called by the district mine
inspector or by the chief of the department to any violations
of the laws .

The special rules must be printed in the language spoken by 75% or more of the employees and posted conspicuously.

44. ( 1 ) The operator or superintendent of every clay
mine shall furnish the district mine inspector proper facili-
ties for entering the mine and making examinations or ob-
taining information .
( 2 ) If any inspector discovers that any mine does not
conform to the provisions of this Act in respect to appliances
for the safety of employees , or that by reason of any defect
or practice in or about the mine the lives or health of persons
employed therein are endangered , he shall immediately notify
the operator or superintendent thereof, orally and in writing ,
stating the particulars in which he considers the mine to be
defective or dangerous , and if he deems it necessary for the
protection of the lives and health of the persons employed in
the mine , he shall, after giving written notice to the operator
or superintendent , immediately notify the chief of the depart-
ment . The chief shall forthwith summon the operator , super-
intendent and the district mine inspector to discuss at the
mine the unsafe practices , defects or unhealthful conditions in
the mine , and , if the chief determines that for the safety of
the employees the mine should be closed , he shall forthwith
order the mine to be closed until it is placed in a safe and
proper condition for mining operations .
(3 ) The owner or operator of any mine so closed may
be petition apply to the circuit court of the county where the
mine is located , or to the judge thereof in vacation , for an
order directing the mine to be re -opened , and the court or
judge shall immediately hear and determine the matters aris-
ing upon the petition . If , upon full hearing thereof, the court
or judge finds that the mine is in a reasonably safe condition ,
the prayer of the petition shall be granted .
(4) Notice of the hearing shall be given to the . district
mine inspector and the chief of the department at least three

CHAPTER 153 479
days before the hearing , and in all such hearings the At-
torney -General , the Commonwealth's Attorney or the county

mine is situated shall appear for the state and defend the
action .
45. If seventy -five per cent of the employees of any
mine are dissatisfied with the inspection of the mine made by
a district mine inspector , they may appeal in writing to the
Department of Mines and Minerals , setting forth clearly and
in detail the reasons for their dissatisfaction . The chief of
the department shall carefully consider the appeal and
promptly take action by ordering another inspection to be
made by the district mine inspector , either alone or in com-
pany with another district mine inspector detailed by the
chief for the purpose , if that course appears to him to be
warranted by the facts set forth in the appeal , or the chief
may take such other action as appears to him to be just and
proper . The appeal shall be signed by the dissatisfied persons .
46. ( 1 ) The operator or superintendent of each clay
mine shall annually not later than January 6 , make or cause
to be made an accurate map of the workings of the mine , on a
scale of one hundred , two hundred , three hundred or four hun-
dred feet to the inch , showing the area mined and the forms
of the excavations up to January 1 , together with the location
and connection of the lines of all adjoining lands within one
thousand feet of the excavations , and the name of each owner
so far as know , marked on each tract . The operator or super-
intendent shall deposit a true copy of the map with the chief
of the department within forty-five days after January 1 ,
and another copy of the map shall be kept at the office of
the mine .
( 2 ) After making and filing the map with the chief,
the operator or superintendent thereafter is only required to
file annually with The chief, within the time specified , such
additional map and statement as is necessary to show the
progress of the workings , the amount and forms of excava-
tions and the property lines within one thousand feet of the
excavations extended since the date of the preceding map .
The chief shall annually , before January 1 , give notice that
the map is required .

CHAPTER 156
(S. B. 44 )
AN ACT relating to law clerks for the Court of Appeals .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
1. The Court of Appeals may appoint not more than
two law clerks , to hold office at the pleasure of the Court ,
to perform such duties as may be required of them by the
Court , and to receive such compensation as may be fixed
by the Court .
§ 2. The law clerks shall be residents of Kentucky and
Graduates in good standing of an approved School of Law.
and shall have been admitted and licensed to practice law

CHAPTER 157
(S. B. 46 )
AN ACT relating to the hours during which public offices may be closed .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 61.160 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
Public offices in counties containing a city of the first or
second class or third class may close at 1 p . m. every Satur-
day.
Passed and enrolled March 21 , approved March 23 , 1946 .
CHAPTER 158
(S. B. 47 )
AN ACT amending Section 96.320 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 96.320 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , 1944
edition , is amended to read as follows :
Cities of the second class that own a water works may
operate such water works as a department of the city , or
may appoint a commission to operate such water works . If
such a commission is appointed , it shall be styled " Commis-
sioners of Water Works " and shall be composed of three
members to be appointed by the mayor , subject to the ap-
proval of the city legislative body. They shall be residents
and legal voters of the city . The terms of the members shall
be fixed by the city legislative body , or they may be appointed
for indefinite terms , subject to removal by the city legislative
body for cause . The commissioners shall give bond for the
faithful performance of their duties in the sum of five thou-
sand dollars . The commissioners shall manage the water
system of the city . They may appoint a superintendent ,
secretary , treasurer and other necessary employes and fix
their salaries . They shall make full monthly reports to the
city legislative body of the operation and condition of the
water system , including all receipts and expenditures . Two
members of the board shall constitute a quorum for the
transaction of business .
Passed and enrolled March 21 , approved March 23 , 1946 .

at its Second Extraordinary Session, for ordinary recurring expenses
of operation of the Legislative Council .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. There is hereby appropriated out of the General Ex-
penditure Fund in the State Treasury , for the fiscal year
ending June 30 , 1946 , the sum of $7,500.00 , for ordinary re-
curring expenses of operation of the Legislative Council ,
this appropriation being intended to supplement the appro-
priation for such purposes made by the 1944 General As-
sembly at its Second Extraordinary Session , and to supply a
deficiency arising because of the insufficiency of the original
appropriation . The Commissioner of Finance shall issue
his warrants upon the State Treasurer , chargeable against
the appropriation hereby made , for the amount of unpaid
vouchers of the Legislative Council , certified by the Chair-
man of the Legislative Council , and now in the hands of the
Commissioner of Finance .
§ 2. WHEREAS , the amount appropriated by the 1944
General Assembly at its Second Extraordinary Session for
ordinary recurring expenses of the Legislative Council has
not been sufficient for the purpose , an emergency is de-
clared to exist and this Act shall take effect upon its pas-
sage and approval by the Governor .
Passed February 8 , enrolled February 9 , approved February 14 , 1946 .

Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-

Paragraph ( c) of Sub - Section ( 1 ) of Section 391.030
of the Kentucky Revised Statutes of 1944 is hereby repealed ,
amended and reenacted to read as follows :
"Personal property or money on hand or in bank to the
amount of $1500.00 shall be exempt from distribution and
sale and shall be set apart by the appraisers of the estate
of an intestate to his widow and infant children , or , if there
is no widow , to his infant children surviving him . The widow
of any deceased person , may at any time before the ap-

having jurisdiction over administration of his estate , a order
authorizing her to withdraw from any bank or other de-

said sum and shall lodge said order , endorsing thereon the
amount withdrawn , with the Clerk of said County Court ,
and he shall deliver it to the appraisers when they are ap-

CHAPTER 164
(S. B. 61 )
AN ACT to establish Junior Colleges in school districts in counties con-
taining a city of the fourth class .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
1. Boards of education of school districts in counties
containing a city of the fourth class , whether the boundary
lines of the district are contiguous with that of the city or not ,
are hereby authorized to establish or to acquire by purchase ,
gift or lease , or by lawful conveyance , junior colleges for the
purpose of promoting public education as defined in this act .
For the support and maintenance thereof the board of educa-
tion of such district may levy an ad valorem tax not to exceed
50 cents on each $ 100.00 worth of taxable property within the
said district , such levy to be made at the same time that the
levy is made for general school purposes . This tax shall be
levied annually and collected by the regular tax collector for
said district as other school moneys and turned over to the
treasurer of said district . Before such a junior college shall
be established , the question shall be submitted to the qualified
voters of the district at any general election and shall be
deemed authorized when a majority of the voters in said
election voting upon said proposition are in favor thereof .
2. A junior college within the meaning of this act is a
college established or supported in whole or in part by funds
raised by taxation and controlled and administered by the
board of education of such district as a part of the public
schook , and affording instruction in arts, and sciences , pre-
professional and vocations , and conferring such certificates
of attainment as may be authorized by other like or similar
institution of learning above high school grade or standing .

CHAPTER 165 491
CHAPTER 165
(S. B. 62 )
AN ACT to amend and re - enact Secton 70.320 of Kentucky Revised
Statutes providing for the appointment and removal of Deputy
Constables in Counties containing a population of over two hundred
fifty thousand and fixing their compensation ; and declaring an emer-
gency .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
That Section 70.320 of Kentucky Revised Statutes be and
the same is hereby repealed and re -enacted so that when it is
re-enacted it shall provide as follows :
Section 70.320 (1 ) A constable may appoint one or more
deputies by and with the consent of the County Judge and
the Constable and his surety shall be liable on his bond for all
the acts and mission of the deputies . Deputy Constables
must be residents of the same district as the Constable by
whom they are appointed .
( 2 ) Deputy Constables may be removed at any time , for
any cause deemed sufficient by the Constable , by order of the
County Court , entered after the filing of a written direction
by the Constable .
(3 ) In Counties containing a population of over two
hundred fifty thousand , each Deputy Constable appointed
by the Constable shall be exclusively compensated for the
performance of the duties of his office by a salary of Twenty-
One Hundred Dollars per annum and paid in equal monthly
installments out of the County Treasury .
Whereas in view of the present inadequate compensation
paid Deputy Constables in Counties containing a population
of over two hundred fifty thousand it is most difficult to ob-
tain competent , capable and qualified men for these positions
and in order that the services of such competent men may
be obtained at the earliest possible date , an emergency is here-
by declared to exist and this act shall become effective im-
mediately upon its passage and approval by the Governor .
All acts or parts thereof in conflict with the provisions
of this act, are , to the extent of such conflict hereby repealed .
Passed March 5 , enrolled March 7 , approved March 14 , 1946 .
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1946, p. 508

In large counties, Deputy Constables will receive an annual salary of $2,100, paid monthly from the County Treasury. The act declares an emergency due to inadequate compensation making it difficult to attract qualified individuals.

CHAPTER 166
(S. B. 68 )
AN ACT making provisions for the representation and protection of
the rights and interests of minor children in divorce proceedings ,
and for the proper enforcement of orders and decrees concerning
the care, custody and maintenance of such children , and for the
proper application of sums ordered to be paid for the care and main-
tenance of such children ; providing for a permanent "friend of the
court " in certain counties ; and providing for the appointment , re-
moval , powers , duties and compensation of such friends of the
court .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
I. ( 1 ) In any county containing a city of the second
class , the fiscal court may , by resolution , authorize the ap-
pointment of a " friend of the court " . If the circuit court of
the county has but one judge , the appointment shall be made
by the judge . If the court has two judges , the appointment
shall be made by joint action of both judges . The appoint-
ment shall be for a term of two years , subject to removal at
the pleasure of the appointing authority . Vacancies shall be
filled by appointment by the appointing authority for the un-
expired term . The person appointed shall be a licensed prac-
ticing attorney. He shall take the Constitutional oath of
office and shall give bond in such sum as may be fixed by the
appointing judge or judges .
(2 ) It shall be the duty of the friend of the court to
supervise and enforce the payment of sums ordered or ad-
judged by the circuit court in divorce actions to be paid for
the care and maintenance of minor children . All persons who
have been ordered or adjudged by the court, in connection
with divorce actions , to make payments for the care and main-
tenance of children , shall, if so ordered by the court, make
such payments to the friend of the court, and he shall see
that the payments are porperly applied in accordance with
the order or judgment . However , if the court so directs , the
payments may be made through the juvenile court of the
county ; in such case the friend of the court shall render such
assistance as may be required in keeping records concerning
such payments and in the enforcement of delinquent
payments , and the circuit court may direct that a desig-
nated amount or portion of the funds appropriated by the
fiscal court for expenses of the friend of the court to be paid.

CHAPTER 167
(S. B. 69 )
AN ACT relating to users of narcotic drugs .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
( 1 ) Any person who habitually uses narcotic drugs , as
defined in KRS 218.010 , shall be imprisoned in the work-
house or county jail for not more than twelve months .
( 2 ) In the event a conviction is had under subsection
( 1 ) of this section in a county , quarterly , justice's or police
court, such court shall have the same authority to post-
pone the entering of judgement and the sentencing of the
defendant , and to place the defendant on probation , as the
circuit courts have under the provisions of KRS 439.020 ,
and all of the provsions of KRS 439.020 to 439.060 shall
apply, and the probation officers shall perform the same
functions with respect to probations made under this section
as they do in the case of probations made by the circuit
court .
( 3 ) Any peace officer who apprehends a person under
the influence of narcotic drug , or who hears a person state
that he is addicted to the use of narcotics , shall immediately
arrest such person and take him before the proper court
to be dealt with according to law .
Passed and enrolled March 21 , approved March 23 , 1946 .

CHAPTER 169 495
CHAPTER 168
(S. B. 70 )
AN ACT relating to county law libraries .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
(1 ) The provisions of KRS 172.100 to 172.160 shall not
apply to any county containing a city of the second class
which county has a law library that was acquired under the
provisions of Chapter 2 of the Acts of 1916. The fiscal court
of such county may make such rules and regulations regard-
ing the maintenance and operation of the library as the court
deems proper and as are approved by the judge or judges
of the circuit court of the county , and may employ a librarian
at a salary not to exceed one hundred dollars per month , and
pay said salary out of the general funds of the County . The
library shall be for the use of the court officers and county
officers of the county , and the fiscal court may provide for the
use of the library by others than the court and county officers ,
on such terms as the fiscal court deems advisable and proper .
( 2 ) All books belonging to the state heretofore or here-
after sent to the county officials directed by law to receive
such books , all books sent to the library by the state , and all
books now owned or hereafter acquired by the county for the
library , shall constitute part of the library. The county may
acquire books , maps or other articles for the library by pur-
chase, gift or devise .
Passed and enrolled March 21 , approved March 23 , 1946 .

496 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
WHEREAS , no taxing authority undertook to levy any taxes upon said
securites when they were originally issued but taxes are now
threatened to be levied by the United States ; and
WHEREAS , if the counties are required to refund such taxes it will
seriously affect their ability to carry on their obligations and duties
and perhaps cause them to be without courthouses ; and
WHEREAS , it is necessary to provide a means for such counties to
acquire said courthouses from their present owners , and finance the
purchase by the issuance of revenue bonds ; and
WHEREAS , there are no statutory provisions authorizing a county to pur-
chase , construct or extend buildings necessary for county purposes
and to pay the cost thereof by the issuance of revenue bonds ; and
WHEREAS , any delay in authorizing the purchase , construction or ex-
tension of necessary county buildings causes larger tax refund
liability to accrue , and unnecessarily cripple the operation of the
various counties ;
Now , Therefore , Be It Enacted by the General Assembly
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky :
1. That for the purpose of making it possible for counties
to purchase , construct or extend buildings and the land upon
which said buildings are situated to be used by counties for
courthouses or other proper county purposes , the fiscal court
of any county is hereby authorized and empowered to pur-
chase , construct or extend courthouses and other buildings
used for county purposes and the land upon which said build-
ings are situated , such buildings to include all furniture and
equipment of every nature used in connection therewith .
2. For the purpose of defraying the cost of acquiring
such buildings , land and appurtenaces thereto by purchase ,
construction or extension , any county may borrow money and
issue negotiable bonds , provided no such bonds shall be issued
unless and until authorized by an order specifying the pro-
posed undertaking , the amount of bonds to be issued and the
maximum rate of interest such bonds are to bear , which shall
not be more than six per cent ( 6 % ) per annum . Such order
shall further provide that the proposed building or buildings
and appurtenances which are to be acquired , are to be con-
structed pursuant to the provisions of this act .
3. All bonds under the provisions of this act may be
issued bearing interest at a rate not exceeding six percent
per annum , payable semi -annually , and shall be executed in
such manner and be payable at such times , not exceeding
forty years from the date thereof , and at such place or places
as the Fiscal Court of said county shall determine .

bonds . Any holder of said bonds or of any of the coupons
may , either at law or in equity , protect and enforce the statu-
tory mortgage lien hereby conferred and may by suit , action ,
mandamus or other proceedings , enforce and compel perform-
ance of all duties required by this act , including the making
and collecting of sufficient rates , the segregation of the in-
come and revenue , and the application thereof .
7. If there be any default in the payment of the prin-
cipal or interest of any of said bonds , any court having juris-
diction of the action may appoint a receiver to administer
said buildings on behalf of the bondholders , with power to
charge and collect rents or obligations outstanding against
said buildings and for the payment of the operating ex-
penses and apply the income and revenues in conformity with
this act and the orders referred to in section eight and nine
hereof .
8. At or before the issuance of such bonds the Fiscal
Court shall by order , set aside and pledge the income and
revenues of such building or buildings and land into a sepa-
rate and special fund to be used and applied in payment of
the cost thereof and in the maintenance thereof including a
sufficient sum to pay the cost of insuring such building or
buildings against loss or damage by fire and wind storm or
other calamity in such sum as may be acceptable to the pur-
chaser or purchasers of such bonds .

CHAPTER 173
(S. B. 79)
AN ACT appropriating money out of the General Expenditure Fund in
the State Treasury for the fiscal year ending June 30 , 1946 , supple-
menting an appropriation heretofore made by the General Assembly
for such fiscal year for capital outlay for state tuberculosis sanatoria .
WHEREAS , the sum appropriated by the 1944 General Assembly for
capital outlay for state tuberculosis sanatoria has proved insufficient
for the purpose , NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. There is hereby appropriated out of the General
Expenditure Fund in the State Treasury , for the fiscal year
ending June 30 , 1946 , the sum of $ 725,000.00 , for capital out-
lay for state tuberculosis sanatoria . This sum is intended to
be in addition and supplemental to the sum appropriated by
the 1944 General Assembly for such purpose , and shall be
available for expenditure in the same manner and under the
same provisions as the sum appropriated for capital outlay in
paragraph ( k ) of Section 26 of Part One of Chapter One of
the Acts of the Second Extraordinary Session of 1944 .
§ 2. WHEREAS , negotiations leading to the construc-
tion of state tuberculosis sanatoria , now pending , will not be
able to be completed unless additional funds for the purpose
are made available , an emergency is declared to exist and this
Act shall take effect upon its passage and approval by the
Governor .

CHAPTER 175 505
CHAPTER 174
(S. B. 88 )
AN ACT relating to Rural Highway Funds .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
KRS 179.410 is amended and re -enacted to read as fol-
lows :
The Department of Highways shall allocate the sum ap-
propriated by the state for the construction , reconstruction ,
improvement and maintenance of county roads and bridges
as follows :
( 1 ) One -third of the sum appropriated shall be appor-
tioned equally between the one hundred and twenty counties .
(2 ) One -third of the sum allocated shall be apportioned
among the several counties on the basis of the ratio which the
rural population of each county bears to the total rural popu-
lation of the state . Rural population , as used herein , means
the population outside cities and towns having a population
of five thousand or more as shown by the most recent decen-
nial census of the United States Bureau of the Census .
(3 ) One -third of the sum allocated shall be apportioned
among the several counties on the basis of the ratio which the
square mile area of the county bears to the total square mile
area of the state .
The Department of Highways at the close of each quarter
of the current fiscal year , or as soon thereafter as practicable ,
shall furnish to the fiscal courts of the several counties a
statement showing the total amount of all disbursements , the
unliquidated and unencumbered balances of the funds allo-
cated to the respective counties for that fiscal year .
Passed March 20 , enrolled March 21 , approved March 23 , 1946 .

classification.title: AN ACT relating to Rural Highway Funds
classification.summary: This act amends KRS 179.410 to outline the allocation of state funds for county road and bridge construction, reconstruction, improvement, and maintenance. The funds are to be divided equally into three parts: one-third apportioned equally among all counties, one-third based on the ratio of each county's rural population to the state's total rural population (defining rural population as outside cities and towns of 5,000 or more), and one-third based on the ratio of each county's square mile area to the state's total square mile area. The Department of Highways is required to provide quarterly statements to fiscal courts regarding disbursements and fund balances.

utes be , and the same is hereby repealed , amended and re-
enacted so that as reenacted it shall read as follows :
“ 287.060 ( 165a -20 ) Oath required of directors before
transacting business .
( 1 ) Before any financial institution mentioned in KRS
287.040 may transact any banking or trust business , it shall
file a written oath with the director of banking . The oath
shall be taken by each director of the institution , and shall
state in substance :
(a) That such director is a citizen of the United States ,
and the State of Kentucky , or , if not , the place of his resi-
dence ;
( b) That he will faithfully discharge the duties of his
office and administer the affairs of the institution , so far as
the duties of his office require ;
(c) That he will uphold the laws of the state , and par-
ticularly the banking laws ; and
(d) That he is the bona fide owner of unencumbered
shares , representing at least five hundred dollars par value ,
of the capital stock of the corporation of which he is a
director .
(2 ) The oath shall be taken before any officer author-
ized to administer oaths , and shall be taken upon the election
of any subsequent director or reelection of any director . The
oath shall be transmitted to the director of banking immedi-
ately upon its execution , and shall be filed and preserved by
the director .
(3) After the oath has been filed , if the director of bank-
ing is satisfied that all requisite laws have been complied
with , he shall issue a certificate to the institution , setting
out therein that such institution has subscribed and paid in
the required capital , and otherwise fully complied with all
pertinent laws and regulations . The certificate will entitle
the institution to at once transact the business for which it
was organized .
Passed March 20 , enrolled March 21 , approved March 23 , 1946 .

CHAPTER 176 507
CHAPTER 176
( S. B. 100 )
AN ACT relating to banks and trust companies , amending Section
287.230 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 287.230 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
(1 ) Any trust company or bank empowered to act as a
fiduciary under the laws of this state and subject to examina-
tion by state or Federal banking authorities may :
(a) Set apart in a separate common trust fund certain
definite securities and properties which conform with state
requirements as to investments ;
(b) Issue shares or participation certificates in such
trust fund ; and
(c) Invest trust funds in its hands for investment in
such shares or participation certificates .
( 2 ) Before establishing a common trust fund as pro-
vided in subsection ( 1 ) , the trust company or bank shall file
with the director of banking a statement of the plan under
which it proposes to establish , maintain , operate and ulti-
mately liquidate the trust fund , and shall secure the written
approval of the plan by the director of banking .
(3 ) After such a trust fund has been established , it
may be modified or amended by filing with the director of
banking a statement setting forth the proposed modification
or amendment , and securing the written approval of the
change by the director of banking .
(4) The bank or trust company shall at all times main-
tain definite records showing all securities and properties held
in such fund , and all participation certificates issued against
such fund .
( 5 ) The beneficiaries of any trust or estate shall have
a claim , prior to any and all other claims against the bank
or trust company , for any money due the trust or estate .
Passed March 20 , enrolled March 21 , approved March 23 , 1946 .

classification.title: AN ACT relating to banks and trust companies and to amend Section 287.290 Kentucky Revised Statutes.
classification.summary: This act repeals, amends, and reenacts Section 287.290 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes, which pertains to exceptions to maximum debt limitations for banks and trust companies. The exceptions listed are for obligations of the United States or the State of Kentucky, obligations guaranteed by the United States or the State of Kentucky, obligations covered by agreements from Federal Reserve Banks or US entities, obligations issued under the Federal Farm Loan Act, obligations insured by the Federal Housing Administrator, and obligations of national mortgage associations. It also includes obligations of Kentucky Counties and school districts incurred in anticipation of tax receipts.
classification.reasoning: The statute amends a section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes concerning exceptions to debt limits for banks and trust companies. The exceptions listed relate to federal and state government obligations, and specific financial instruments and entities. There is no mention of race, nor any provisions that would implicitly or explicitly create racial segregation or discrimination. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law. The document type is 'codes' as it explicitly refers to amending the 'Kentucky Revised Statutes'.

CHAPTER 178
(S. B. 102 )
AN ACT relating to inquests concerning the mental condition of per-
sons , and the commitment and discharge of persons mentally incom-
petent or whose mental condition is questions .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
1. Section 202.120 of the 1944 edition of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) If the certificate of the medical examiners men-
tioned in KRS 202.110 states in substance that in the opinion
of such examiners the mental condition of the person is so
abnormal that the safety of the person or of the public re-
quires that he be committed for a period of care and further
medical examination and observation of his mental condition ,
the court may commit such person to a state hospital for
the insane , or to a city or county hospital in the county having
a psychopathic ward or facilities suitable , in the court's opin-
ion , for the care and medical examination and observation
of mental diseases , or , upon receipt of a certificate in writing
from the United States Veterans Administration or other
agency of the United States Government that facilities are
available and that such person is eligible for care or treat-
ment therein , to the said United States Veterans Administra-
tion or other agency of the United States Government , for a
period of care and further medical examination and observa-
tion of the mental condition of such person not to exceed
thirty -five days .
( 2 ) Within thirty days after such commitment , if the
superintendent of the institution , or the responsible medical
officer of the United States Veterans Administration or other
federal agency to which the person has been committed , finds
that the person is sane, he shall so certify to the judge who
committed the person . The judge shall thereupon order the
discharge of the person, and the clerk shall mail an attested.
copy of the order to the superintendent or medical officer
from whom the certificate was received .
(3 ) Within thirty days after such commitment , if the
superintendent , and the clinical director or staff physician
acting in the latter capacity , of the institution to which the
person has been committed , or, in the case of a commitment to

classification.title: AN ACT relating to inquests concerning the mental condition of persons, and the commitment and discharge of persons mentally incompetent or whose mental condition is questions.
classification.summary: This act amends KRS 202.120 to outline the process for committing individuals deemed mentally incompetent or whose mental condition is questionable. It specifies that if medical examiners believe a person's mental condition is abnormal and requires commitment for care and observation, the court can commit them to a state hospital, a city or county hospital with suitable facilities, or a United States Veterans Administration facility if applicable. The commitment period is not to exceed thirty-five days. The act also details the procedure for discharge if the person is found to be sane within thirty days of commitment.
classification.reasoning: The statute deals with the commitment and discharge of individuals based on their mental condition. It outlines procedures for medical examination, court commitment to various institutions (state hospitals, city/county hospitals, VA facilities), and subsequent discharge if found sane. There is no mention of race, nor are there any provisions that implicitly or explicitly discriminate based on race. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law. The document type is 'codes' as it amends an existing Kentucky Revised Statute (KRS).

CHAPTER 179
( S. B. 103 )
AN ACT to amend Section 403.030 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes ,
relating to actions for divorce .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 403.030 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
A petition for divorce shall not be taken for confessed
or be sustained by the admission of the defendant alone , but
must be supported by other proof . Two witnesses , or one wit-
ness and strong corroborative circumstances , shall be neces-
sary to sustain the charge of adultery or of lewd and lascivious
behavior . The credibility or good character of the witnesses
must be personally known to the judge or personally known to
and certified to by the officer taking the deposition , or else
it must be proved .
Passed March 20 , enrolled March 21 , approved March 23 , 1946 .
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1946, p. 527

classification.title: AN ACT to amend Section 403.030 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , relating to actions for divorce .
classification.summary: This act amends Section 403.030 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes concerning divorce actions. It specifies that a petition for divorce cannot be taken for confessed or sustained solely by the defendant's admission. Instead, it requires support from other proof, with two witnesses or one witness and strong corroborative circumstances needed to sustain charges of adultery or lewd and lascivious behavior. The credibility or good character of witnesses must be personally known to the judge or certified by the deposition officer, or otherwise proven.
classification.reasoning: The provided text contains two distinct legislative acts. The first part discusses amendments to statutes concerning the commitment of individuals found to be insane or feeble-minded, including provisions for federal agencies like the Veterans Administration. The second part, identified by 'CHAPTER 179', is an act to amend Section 403.030 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes relating to divorce actions. This second act sets evidentiary standards for divorce proceedings, specifically regarding charges of adultery and lewd behavior. Neither of these sections contains any language that explicitly or implicitly suggests racial discrimination or segregation. Therefore, they are not Jim Crow laws. The document type is 'codes' because it refers to the 'Kentucky Revised Statutes' which are compiled legal codes. The confidence is high as the content is clearly about legal procedures and not race-based restrictions. No human review is needed.

CHAPTER 181 511
CHAPTER 180
(S. B. 105 )
AN ACT appropriating money out of the General Expenditure Fund
in the State Treasury for the fiscal year ending June 30 , 1946 , to the
Department of Welfare , for ordinary recurring expenses in the
operation of Darnall General Hospital , a state institution to be
established in Boyle and Mercer Counties .
WHEREAS , the Department of Welfare , on behalf of the Common-
wealth , is about to acquire from the United States Army or from
the Federal Government the institution known as Darnall General
Hospital , located in Boyle and Mercer Counties , and
WHEREAS , provision must be made for ordinary recurring expenses of
operation of such hospital as a state institution , NOW, THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. There is hereby appropriated to the Welfare De-
partment out of the General Expenditure Fund in the State
Treasury , for the fiscal year ending June 30 , 1946 , the sum
of $250,000.00 for ordinary recurring expenses of operation
during the fiscal year ending June 30 , 1946 , of the institution
known as Darnall General Hospital , which is to be acquired
and operated as a state institution . This sum is intended to
be in addition and supplemental to any sum appropriated by
the 1944 General Assembly for operation of state hospitals
and institutions during said fiscal year .
§ 2. WHEREAS , negotiations are now pending with
the United States Army and the Federal Government for the
acquisition of Darnall General Hospital , and in order to
acquire such hospital on terms beneficial and advantageous
to the state , assurance must be made that funds for the op-
eration of such hospital as a state institution will be available ,
an emergency is declared to exist and this Act shall take
effect upon its passage and approval by the Governor .
Passed February 8 , enrolled February 9 , approved February 14 , 1946 .

CHAPTER 182
(S. B. 109 )
AN ACT relating to chiropractors .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 312.030 of the 1944 Edition of the
Kentucky Revised Statutes is repealed , amended and re-
enacted so that when amended and re -enacted it will read as
follows :
Any person of good moral character , with a preliminary
education or experience equivalent to a high school educa-
tion , who has been graduated from a chiropractic school or
college giving a course of four academic years and requiring
actual attendance of at least four thousand 45 -minute academic
hours in anatomy , physiology , neurology , symptomatology ,
hygiene , sanitation , pathology , chiropractic analysis , philoso-
phy , and the principles and practice of chiropractic , shall be
eligible to take the examination for a license . Provided , this
act shall not be applicable to those persons who were in at-
tendance at Chiropractic schools or colleges prior to the
passage of this act .
Passed March 12 , enrolled March 14 , approved March 23 , 1946 .

CHAPTER 183 513
CHAPTER 183
( S. B. 110 )
AN ACT to amend section 92.430 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes re-
lating to the procedure for assessing property for taxation purposes
in cities of the second class .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 92.430 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is hereby
amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The assessor in each city of the second class shall
assess in one book all property subject to city taxation, and
in making the assessment for any year shall assess any prop-
erty subject to taxation that he finds to have been omitted
in any former year .
( 2 ) On or before July 1 of each year the city assessor
shall give notice , by advertisement in the official newspaper
of the city and by handbills posted through the city , that all
persons owning real estate on July 1 and all persons owning
or having in their possession or under their control on July 1
any personal property subject to city taxation shall , before
September 1 , appear at the assessor's office and furnish him
a true and complete list of the property , with its true cash
value as of July 1 , under oath , upon forms to be furnished
by the assessor on application at his office , and that all mer-
chants doing business in the city shall in likę manner , in
addition to listing their personal property , state the highest
amount in value of all merchandise owned or kept on hand
for sale by them during the three months next preceding July .
The assessor and his deputies may administer oaths , and ex-
amine any person on oath concerning his real and personal
property and its value , and may examine merchants on oath
as to the statements required of them . The assessor may also
gather information by other means .
( 3) For the purpose of assessing property for taxation ,
the assessor shall keep his office open , with himself or a
deputy in attendance , during the hours from 8 A. M. to 6
P. M. , or such other or additional hours as are fixed by ordi-
nance , from July 1 , to September 1 , except Sundays and
legal holidays . The assessor shall constantly keep on hand ,

(7) On or before December 1 of each year the assessor

by him to be a full , careful and honest assessment of all
property in the city subject to assessment . He shall take the
auditor's receipt therefor in duplicate , and shall transmit
one of the duplicates to each board of the general council
at its first regular meeting in December . The assessment
books and reports shall remain in the auditor's office , subject
to public inspection , until transmitted to the board of
equalization .
Passed and enrolled March 21 , approved March 25, 1946 .

CHAPTER 187
( S. B. 114 )
AN ACT relating to purchases by the boards of education within the
Commonwealth of Kentucky from the United States of America , or
agencies thereof , and declaring an emergency to exist :
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
(1 ) The provisions of KRS 162.070 shall not apply to
purchases made by the boards of education within the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky from the United States of America ,
or any agency thereof .
( 2 ) WHEREAS , the United States of America and its
agencies are now offering for sale goods and supplies needed
immediately by the boards of education within the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , and for which goods and supplies said
United States of America will not receive bids , an emergency
is hereby declared to exist , and this Act shall become effective
immediately upon its passage and approval by the Governor .
Passed March 20 , enrolled March 21 , approved March 23 , 1946 .

or other help , such help shall be employed only by the written
consent of the Commissioner of Highways , the Commissioner
of Finance and the Governor .
(8) The members of said Board and the Assistant At-
torney General assigned to the Department of Highways shall
serve without additional compensation now allowed them by
law . All awards or judgments made by the Board and all
costs incident to the operation of said Board shall be paid
out of the State Road Fund upon warrants drawn by the
Commissioner of Finance upon the State Treasurer .
(9 ) Appeals may be taken to the Franklin Circuit Court
from all awards or judgments of the Board where the amount
in controversy , exclusive of interest and cost , is over $ 50.00 .
Appeals shall be taken within thirty days from the rendition
of the award or judgment, and the method of appeal shall
follow as nearly as may be the Civil Code of Practice , Sec-
tion 724 , sub -section 1 , except the state shall not be required
to execute bond .
( a ) On appeal no new evidence may be introduced , ex-
cept as to fraud or misconduct of some person engaged in
the hearing before the Board . The Court shall hear the cause
upon the record before it , and dispose of the appeal in a sum-
mary manner , being limited in determining : Whether or not
the Board acted without or in excess of its powers ; the award
or judgment was procured by fraud ; the award or judgment
is not in conformity to the provisions of this act ; and whether
the findings of fact support the award or judgment . The
Court shall enter its findings on the order book as a judg-
ment of the court , and such judgment shall have the same
effect and be enforcible as any other judgment of the court
in civil causes .
( 10 ) Appeals may be taken to the Court of Appeals
under the same conditions and under the same practice as
appeals are taken from judgments in civil causes rendered
by the Circuit Court , but no motion for a new trial or bill
of execptions shall be necessary . The Court of Appeals shall
review only the matters subject to review by the Circuit
Court and also errors of law arising in the Circuit Court

heirs , or devisees , of any obligors the right to pay , upon sixty days
prior written notice , any and all obligations of decedents which may
be created or come into being after the effective date of this Act :
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
That any and all obligations , oral , written or implied ,
which come into being or are made or undertaken after the
effective date of this Act , may , upon the death of the obligor ,
or any one of the joint obligors , upon any such obligations ,
be paid to the creditors of such obligations by the representa-
tives , real or personal, or by the heirs or devisees , of such
decedents upon sixty days written notice to the holder or
holders , or payees or beneficiaries , of any such obligation or
obligations ; and that all such contracts or obligations which
come into being after the effective date of this Act shall be
subject to the provisions hereof.

CHAPTER 191
( S. B. 122 )
AN ACT making provision for a Department of Banking ; providing for
the organization , functions and personnel of the department ; and
abolishing the Division of Banking in the Department of Business
Regulation .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
§ 1. ( 1 ) There is created a Department of Banking ,
which shall constitute a statutory administrative depart-
ment of the state government within the meaning of Chap-
ter 12 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes . The department
shall be headed by a Commissioner of Banking , who shall
be the executive head of the department and shall be
charged with the administration of the department .

as the Governor's Palace , has been vacant for several years , and ;
WHEREAS , This Mansion was built by order of the Kentucky Legisla-
ture in 1796 , and was the home of thirty - eight ( 38 ) Governors ,
to wit : Isaac Shelby , Jas . Garrard , Christopher Greenup , Chas .
Scott , Geo . Madison , Gabriel Slaughter , John Adair , Joseph Desha ,
Thomas Metcalfe , John Breathitt , Jas . T. Morehead , Jas . Clark ,
Chas . A. Wickliffe , Robt . P. Letcher , Wm . Owsley , John J. Crittenden ,
John L. Helm , Lazurus W. Powell, Chas . S. Morehead , Beriah Ma-
goffin , Jas . F. Robison , Thos . E. Bramlett , John W. Stevenson , Preston
H. Leslie , Jas . B. McCreary , Luke P. Blackburn , J. Proctor Knott,
Simon B. Buckner , John Young Brown , W. O. Bradley , W. S. Taylor,
J. C. W. Beckham , Augustus E. Wilson , and ;
WHEREAS , During this period it was visited by men of national and
international fame , such as the Marquis de LaFayette , Henry Clay ,
Presidents Jas . Monroe , Andrew Jackson , Zachary Taylor , Wm . H.
Taft and Theodore Roosevelt and other notables were entertained
therein ; and by reason of these facts it is of great historic and
intrinsic value to the State of Kentucky and to Kentuckians every-
where , and ;
WHEREAS , A revenue could be derived as a museum from admission
fees charged , as is being done at Old Kentucky Home in Bardstown ,

AN ACT relating to bridges of cities of the first class amending Section

Section 1. Section 181.500 , Kentucky Revised Statutes ,
is hereby amended and as amended reads :
When the principal and interest on the bonds have been
paid or their payment fully provided for , the commission shall
stand dissolved . Thereupon : ( 1 ) the cash reserve on hand ,
if any , after all outstanding obligations for the maintenance ,
repair and operation of the bridge have been paid, shall be
paid or turned over to the city to be used in such manner as
shall be determined by the Mayor of said ciay ; ( 2 ) all right ,

maintenance and repair of the bridge, as part of the State
Highway System , but it shall be the duty of the city to sweep ,
sprinkle , light and police the travel portion of said bridge ,
provided the ordinary duties of the State Highway Patrol
may be exercised there over ; and ( 4 ) said bridge shall there- .
after be operated free of tolls .

is hereby repealed .

Section 1. Section 173.050 , Kentucky Revised Statutes ,

173.050 ( 2801b - 3 ; 2801b - 7 ) Powers of board of trustees .
(1 ) The board of trustees may , in its corporate name :
( a) Contract and be contracted with , sue and be sued ,
and have and use a corporate seal , which it may alter at
pleasure ;
(b) Acquire , by itself or jointly with the city in which
the library is located , by gift , purchase or otherwise , and

use of the public library , for the purpose for which it is
dedicated ;
(c ) Use , manage and improve , sell and convey , rent or
lease property , and erect suitable buildings .
( 2 ) The board of trustees may mortgage any real or
personal property owned by the library to secure any sums
borrowed for making repairs or improvements , or paying off
any indebtedness it may owe . The mortgage shall not exceed
$1,000,000.00 .
Section 2. Section 173.070 , Kentucky Revised Statutes ,
is hereby amended and as amended reads as follows :
173.070 ( 2801b -6) Tax for public library ; disposition of
revenue ; report to legislative body . ( 1 ) To raise money for
the establishment and maintenance of the library , the city
legislative body shall annually levy a tax of not less than
three and one-half cents or more than ten cents on each one
hundred dollars ' worth of property assessed for taxation for
city purposes . When the assessment is completed the amount
levied annually shall be credited to the library fund of the
city, and the revenue , as collected , shall be paid over to the
board of trustees by the director of finance in regular monthly
installments .
( 2 ) The board shall make a report to the city legislative
body each July , showing the use of the library for the fiscal

173.080 ( 2801b -9 ) Issuance of library bonds . The board

as ' library bonds ' and the board of trustees shall, by resolu-

The board shall determine when , at what price and how the

trustees shall be first applied to the payment of interest on

CHAPTER 199
(S. B. 136 )
AN ACT authorizing any county in which there is located a city of the
first class and said city to make and carry out contracts and agree-
ments for the joint acquisition , ownership , and use of a city - county
governmental building .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1 . Definitions :
" CITY " includes city or cities .
" CITY -COUNTY BUILDING " includes building or build-
ings , main or auxiliary or supplementary building or build-
ings , and includes new or adaptation of existing building or
buildings , and also includes such land , and all types of inter-
ests in land , such equipment , furnishings , and facilities and
such other real or personal property as is necessary , con-
venient or desirable for the use and enjoyment of said build-
ing or buildings .
" GOVERNMENTAL PURPOSE " includes the gov-
ernmental and administrative activities of the city and county
proper or either or both and city or county commissions ,
boards , agencies and instrumentalities and joint city - county
commissions , boards , agencies or instrumentalities .
Section 2. In any county of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky in which there is located a city of the first class , the
said county and said city of the first class are authorized to
erect , purchase , lease , condemn or otherwise acquire , keep
in repair and improve , own and use a joint city-county
building .
Section 3. Said city-county building may be used for
any governmental purpose in which either or both the said
county and city are engaged .
Section 4. The city through its Legislative body and
the county by its Fiscal Court are authorized to make and
enter into contracts and agreements to provide for the joint
acquisition , ownership and use of such city-county building
including but not limited to contracts and agreements provid-
ing for the means and method of acquisition of said building ,
the method of allocation and payment of original cost , cost
of repair , upkeep and improvement , cost of maintenance and

CHAPTER 200 535
certain offenses and providing penalties therefor ; and providing
for lien and foreclosure ; and declaring an emergency.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
1. This Act is in exercise of the police powers of the
state for the protection of the safety , health and welfare of
the people of the state . It is hereby found and declared that
the public welfare requires control and regulation of the
operation of refrigerated lockers and of the sale , handling
and processing of articles of human food in connection there-
with , and the control , inspection and regulation of persons
engaged therein, in order to prevent or eliminate unsanitary ,
unhealthful , fraudulent , and unfair or uneconomic practices
and conditions in connection with the refrigerated locker
business , which practices and conditions endanger public
health , defraud customers , jeopardize the public source of
supply and storage facilities of essential food products , and
adversely affect an important and growing industry . It is
further found and declared that the regulation of the refriger-
ated locker business , as above outlined , is in the interest of
the economic and social well -being and the health and safety
of the state and all of its people .
§ 2. DEFINITIONS . As used in this Act, unless the
context requires otherwise :
"Food " shall include any article used by man for food ,
drink , confectionery or condiment , or which enters into the
composition of the same whether simple , blended , mixed or
compounded .
" Frozen food locker plant " shall mean a location or
establishment in which space in individual lockers is rented
to persons for storage of frozen food and is equipped with a
chill room , sharp freezing facilities and facilities for cutting ,
preparing , wrapping and packaging meats and meat prod-
ucts , fruits and vegetables .
" Branch frozen food locker plant " shall mean a loca-
tion or establishment in which space in individual lockers is
rented to persons for storage of frozen food after prepara-
tion for storage at a frozen food locker plant .
" Sharp frozen " shall mean the freezing of food in a
room in which the temperature is zero or below .

" Commissioner " shall mean the Commissioner of Agri-
culture, Labor and Statistics .
§ 3. ( a ) Each frozen food locker plant or branch
frozen food locker plant operated in this state shall be licensed
under and subject to the provisions of this Act .
(b ) There shall be paid to the Commissioner with each
application for a refrigerated locker license or for renewal
of such license an annual license fee of ten dollars ( $10.00 )
and the funds therefrom shall be disbursed by the Com-
missioner for the enforcement of this Act ; provided , how-
ever , that funds disbursed for enforcement of the Act shall
in no case exceed funds derived from license fees thereunder .
(c) Each such license shall expire on December 31 fol-
lowing its date of issue , unless sooner revoked for cause . Re-
newal may be obtained annually by surrendering to the Com-
missioner the old license certificate and paying the required
annual license fee . Such license fee shall not be transferrable
to any person nor be applicable to any location other than
that for which originally issued .
§ 4. ( a ) The Commissioner may cancel or suspend any
such license if he finds after proper investigation that ( 1 ) the
licensee has violated any provisions of this Act or of any
other law of this state relating to the operation of refrigerated
lockers or of the sale of any human food in connection there-
with , or any regulation effective under any Act the adminis-
tration of which is in the charge of the Commissioner of Agri-
culture , or ( 2 ) the licensed refrigerated locker premises or
any equipment used therein or in connection therewith is in an
unsanitary condition and the licensee has failed or refused
to remedy the same within ten ( 10 ) days after receipt from
the Commissioner of written notice to do so .
( b ) No license shall be revoked or suspended by the
Commissioner without delivery to the licensee of a written
statement of the charge involved and an opportunity to an-
swer such charge within ten ( 10 ) days from the date of such
notice .
(c) Any order made by the Commissioner suspending
or revoking any license may be reviewed by the circuit court
of the county in which the licensed premises are located ,
within ten days ( 10 ) days from the date of notice in writing
of the Commissioner's order revoking or suspending such
license has been served upon him .

CHAPTER 200 537
§ 5. (a ) No person shall work or be permitted to work
in or about any refrigerated locker in the handling , process-
ing or dealing in any human food or any ingredient thereof
without holding a certificate from a physician , duly accredited
for that purpose by the State Board of Health , certifying that
such person has been examined and found free from any con-
tagious or infectious disease . The State Board of Health
may fix a maximum fee , not exceeding two dollars ( $ 2.00 )
which may be charged by a physician for such examination .
Such certificate shall be effective for a period of six (6 )
months and thereafter must be renewed following proper
physical examination as aforesaid . Where such certificate is
required and provided under municipal ordinance upon ex-
amination deemed adequate by the State Board of Health ,
certificates issued thereunder shall be sufficient under this
section .
(b) Any such certificate shall be revoked by the State
Board of Health at any time the holder thereof is found , after
proper physical examination , to be afflicted with any com-
municable or infectious disease . Refusal of any person em-
ployed in such premises to submit to proper and reasonable
physical examination upon written demand by the State
Board of Health or of the Commissioner of Agriculture shall
be cause for revocation of that person's health certificate .
§ 6. The locker operator , or the Commissioner , or their
authorized agents , may at any reasonable time inspect all
foods , wares or merchandise before same is placed in the
locker storage . The locker operator or the Commissioner of
Agriculture , or their agents , may reject any foods , wares or
merchandise and may refuse or place same for storage , if in
the locker operators opinion said food , wares or merchandise
is not in suitable or proper condition for storage .
§ 7. The Department of Agriculture , through its Com-
missioner or his authorized agents , shall have the authority
of supervising and enforcing all of the provisions of this Act .
8. The Commissioner may prescribe and enforce such
rules and regulations and to make such definitions , and pre-
scribe such procedure with regard to hearings , as he may deem
necessary to carry into effect the full intent and meaning of
this Act .

$ .9. The Commissioner shall cause to be made periodi-

$ 10. Every operator of a refrigerated locker plant shall
provide a complete refrigeration system with adequate capac-
ity and accurate and reliable controls for the maintenance
of the following uniform temperatures of the various re-
frigerated rooms if provided , under extreme conditions of
outside temperatures and under peak load conditions in the
normal operation of the plant . The temperatures of the
following rooms shall not exceed :
( a) Chill room , temperature within two ( 2 ) degrees

(Fahrenheit with a tolerance of ten ( 10 ) degrees ( Fahren-

(b) Sharp freeze room , sharp freeze compartments ,

ance of ten ( 10 ) degrees ( Fahrenheit ) for either type of in-
stallation after fresh food is put in for freezing ;
(c) Locker room temperature of zero ( 0 ) degree ( Fahren-
heit ) with a tolerance of twelve ( 12 ) degrees (Fahrenheit )
plus.
§ 11. In any proceeding under this Act the Commis-
sioner may administer oaths and issue subpoenas , summon
witnesses and take testimony of any person within the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky .
§ 12. The liability of the owner or operator of refriger-
ated lockers for loss of goods in lockers or in operator's care
shall be limited to negligence of operation or of employes .

13. No tainted food shall be stored in a refrigerated
locker , and food for non -human consumption shall be stored
in a separate room .
§ 14. The owner of any frozen food locker plant shall
have a lien on the contents of each locker , compartment or
space for the payment of the locker rental thereon and any
processing or other charges . Such lien may be foreclosed
by giving a written notice by registered mail to the renter of
such locker , compartment or space , and , after ten days from
date of the notice , the owner may proceed to sell the contents
of the locker, at either public or private sale , for the reason-
able value of its contents . If the proceeds from the sale ex-
ceeds the amount of the lien , the balance shall be duty paid
to the renter . The place , time and kind of sale shall conform
to the written notice to the renter .
§ 15. Persons who own or operate refrigerated locker
plants shall not be construed to be warehousemen , nor shall
receipts or other instruments issued by such persons in the
ordinary conduct of their business be construed to be negoti-
able warehouse receipts .
§ 16. Any person violating any provision of this Act
shall be guilty of a misdemeanor , and upon conviction thereof
shall be fined not less than fifty ( $ 50.00 ) dollars for the first
offense , and not less than one hundred ( $100.00 ) dollars for
the second and for each and every subsequent offense , and
each day that any violation continues shall constitute a sepa-
rate offense .
17. This Act is necessary for the immediate preserva-
tion of the public peace , health and safety ; and an emergency
hereby is declared to exist , and this Act shall take effect and
be in full force and effect from and after its passage and ap-
proval by the Governor .

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section I. Definitions .
1. The term " non -instructional employees ' includes all
accountants , secretaries , clerical employees , janitors , engi-
neers , custodians , nurses , and other employees not engaged
in teaching and/or research professions .
2. The term " classified service " signifies the competi-
tive classified civil service of the Independent School Dis-
tricts under this Act .
3. The term " Commission " signifies a Civil Service
Commission created by this Act .
4. "Civil Service Commissioner" shall be a member
of the Commission defined in the preceding paragraph .
5. The term " appointing authority " signifies the
Board of Education in a city of the Second Class .
6. The term " employee " signifies any person holding a
position subject to appointment , removal , promotion , by the
appointing authority .
7. A " full -time employee " shall be defined as an indi-
vidual who is employed a minimum of ten months in a calen-
dar year.
Section II . Manner of Establishing a Civil Service
system .
That the Board of Education of Independent School Dis-
tricts embracing a city of the Second Class in the Common-
wealth of Kentucky is hereby authorized and empowered to
establish a system of Civil Service applicable to and control-
ling the classification , employment and discharge of non-
instructional employees all in the manner herein provided .
When a majority of the regular and/or full -time non -instruc-
tional employees of the Board of Education in any Independ-
ent School District embracing a city of the Second Class shall
