CHAPTER 201 541
file with such Board a petition requesting the establishment
of a Civil Service System for such employees said Board
may at its next regular meeting by resolution spread upon its
minutes , recite the filing of such petition and declare the
Board's purpose thereby to establish and thereafter admin-
ister and operate such System of Civil Service by the Com-
mission hereby created under the powers herein conferred
and in . the manner authorized and pursuant to provisions of
this Act . Thereupon the Board of Education shall appoint
and constitute the Civil Service Commission in the manner
and consisting of the persons in the next succeeding section
of this Act provided .
Section III . Provision for the Adinistration of the Civil
Service System .
Upon the adoption by the Board of Education of any
Independent School District embracing a city of the Second
Class of the resolution in the preceding section provided ,
and the appointment of the Civil Service Commission therein
provided for, there shall thereby be created in such school
district a Civil Service Commission consisting of the Chair-
man of the Board of Education , the Superintendent of
Schools , the Attorney for the Board of Education , and two
other elected and qualified members of the Board to be named
by the Chairman . All of said appointments shall be for a
period of two years . Any vacancies occurring in the member-
ship of said Commission shall be immediately filled for the
unexpired terms by the Chairman of the Board of Education .
Said members of Commission , except the Attorney , shall
serve without compensation . The Board of Education shall
compensate the Attorney with a sum of not less than three
hundred ( $300 ) dollars per annum nor more than six hundred
( $600 ) dollars per annum . The Chairman of the Board of
Education shall be Chairman of the Commission and the Sup-
erintendent of Schools shall be Secretary and Chief Examiner .
The Commission shall meet regularly each calendar
month at such hour and at a place convenient and suitable to
be designated and provided and equipped by the Board of
Education . Special meetings may be called by the Chairman
or by any three members by delivering reasonable written
notice to each member . A quorum of said Commission shall
consist of not less than three members for the transaction of
such business as may come before it .

542 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section IV . Secretary , Chief Examiner and Assistants :
Selection of Employees from Lists ; Annual Budget to Cover
Salaries and Expenses .
Each Board of Education Civil Service Commission may
employ such examiners and clerical and other assistants as
the Secretary - Chief Examiner may in the judgment of the
Board of Education require , and shall determine by order
of record, the terms of office , duties , services to be performed
and the compensation of all persons so employed .
All accountants , secretaries , nurses and other clerical
eployees of the Board of Education shall be selected and
appointed by the Board of Education from lists furnished
said Board by the Civil Service Commission .
All custodians , janitors , engineers , and other non -instruc-
tional employees of the Board of Education shall be selected
and appointed by the Board of Education from lists pre-
viously submitted to the Board for that purpose by the Com-
mission . These lists shall include only those names appearing
upon the eligibility lists as provided for in this Act .
The Board of Education of said City upon recommenda-
tion of the Civil Service Commission , shall make an annual
appropriation sufficient to cover all salaries and necessary
expenses of said Commission and its employees .
Section V. What Rules shall Provide , Tests , Eligibility
Lists , Period of Probation , Reinstatement , Promotion.-
Such rules and regulations shall, among other things , provide :
( 1 ) For standardization and qualifications for all po-
sitions . All salaries shall be uniform for like grade and years
of service . Grades of service shall be standardized and classi-
fied by the Board of Education .
(2 ) For keeping minutes of its own proceedings and
records of its examination and other official actions . The Com-
mission shall prepare , continue and keep in its office , a com-
plete roster of all persons in the classified service .
( 3 ) For open and competitive tests , ascertaining the
relative fitness of all applicants for appointment to classified
positions . All tests shall be practical and related only to mat-
ters which fairly measure the relative fitness of the candi-
dates to discharge the duties of the positions to which they
seek appointment . Notice of such tests shall be given not
less than ten days in advance by public advertisement in at

ing for admission to any examination , provided for by this

(e ) Such other information as may be reasonably re-

ments of any material fact , or practiced or attempted to prac-
tice , any deception or fraud in his application or in his exami-
nation , in establishing his eligibility or securing his appoint-
ment .
( 6 ) That from the returns of the examinations the Com-
mission shall prepare an eligible list of the persons whose

544 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
or class is not less than the minimum fixed by the rules of the
Commission and who are otherwise eligible ; and such per-
sons shall take rank upon the eligible list as candidates in the
order of their relative excellence as determined by the exami-
nation without reference to priority of time of examination .
In the event of two or more applicants receiving the same
mark in an examination , priority in the time of the applica-
tion shall determine the order in which their names shall be
placed on the eligible list . The term of eligibility of each list
shall be fixed by the Commission at not less than one year
nor more than two years . When an eligible list is reduced to
three names or less a new list shall be prepared .
(7) That any soldier , sailor , marine , member of the
army nurse corps who has served in the army, navy or hos-
pital service of the United States in the war with the central
powers of Europe between the dates of April 6 , 1917 and
November 11 , 1918 , or in World War II, who has been honor-
ably discharged therefrom and is a resident of Kentucky ,
upon filing with the Commission a certificate of service and
honorable discharge , shall receive additional grade of twenty
per cent to the grade he otherwise rates .
( 8 ) For the creation of the above eligibility lists , upon
which shall be entered the names of successful candidates in
the order of their standing in examinations for the filling of
positions by the Board of Education from the three candi-
dates graded highest on such eligibility list , which list shall
remain in force not longer than two years . In the absence of
an appropriate eligibility list , any place may be filled tem-
porarily by the Board of Education without examination for
a period not to exceed six months , during which time the Civil
Service Commission shall hold the necessary examinations
for the filling of the place permanently .
(9) That no person shall be appointed or employed
under any title not appropriate to the duties to be discharged ,
and no person shall be transferred or assigned to perform
any duties of any position subject to competitive tests equiva-
lent to that required for the position to be filled .
( 10 ) That any person appointed from the eligibility list
and laid off for lack of work shall be placed at the head of the
eligibility list for reappointment for a period of eligibility as
provided by the rules of the Board .

( 11 ) For a period of probation not exceeding one year

Section VI. Emergency Appointments ; Promotion ;
Classified Service for Employees .
(1 ) Emergency Appointments . Whenever there are
urgent reasons for filling a vacancy in any position in the
classified service and the Commission is unable to certify to
the appointing authority , upon request of the latter , a list of
persons eligible for appointment after a competitive exami-
nation, the appointing authority may make a provisional
appointment to fill a vacancy until a selection and appoint-
ment can be made after competitive examination ; but such
provisional appointment shall continue in force only until
regular appointment can be made from eligible lists prepared
by the Commission and such eligible lists shall be prepared
within ninety days thereafter .
( 2 ) Promotions : Vacancies in positions in the classified
service shall be filled in so far as practicable by promotions .
All examinations for promotions shall be competitive . In pro-
motional examinations efficiency and seniority in service shall
form a part of the maximum mark attainable in such examina-
tion .
(3 ) Classified Service for Employees : Within six
months after the Civil Service Commission has promulgated
rules herein provided for , all employees then in the service
of the Board of Education are to be classified by the Com-
mission upon the basis of a recommendation from the chief
examiner and if the service of any such employee has been
satisfactory to the Board of Education over a period of one
year his appointment shall become permanent .
Section VII . Tenure of Classified Service Employees of
the Board of Education .
Under this Act tenure of employees of the Board of Edu-
cation shall remain in force during good behavior and effi-

546 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
cient and competent service , and shall not be terminated
except for any of the following causes : Insubordination , im-
moral character , conduct unbecoming the employee , ineffi-
ciency , incompetency , physical or mental disability , neglect
of duty, dishonesty , drunkenness , violation of the provisions
of this act or the rules of the Commission .
No employee shall be dismissed or suspended unless
furnished with a written statement , specifying in detail the
charge or charges against said employee , signed by the Chair-
man of the Board of Education and naming a date and place
at which the employee may appear before the Board of
Education and answer said charge or charges . Said date for
the hearing shall not be less than twenty nor more than
thirty days after the service of such charges upon the em-
ployee . The employee shall within ten days after the receipt
of the written statement of such charges notify the Board
of Education of his intention to appear and answer such
charges , and upon failure of the employee to give such
notice the Board of Education may dismiss the employee
by a majority vote and such dismissal shall be final .
Upon receipt of the employee's notice of intention to
appear and answer such charges , the Board of Education
shall issue such subpoenas as shall be necessary for the
determination of the issues involved . The issues shall be
heard at the time and place set and the hearing shall be
public or private at the discretion of the employee .
Both parties may be represented by counsel and may
require the presence of witnesses upon subpoena . Each
witness shall be required to take oath or affirmation before
the Chairman of the Board of Education . The Board of
Education shall provide for a stenographic report of the
proceedings and furnish the employee with a copy . Upon
completion of both sides of the case , the Board of Edu-
cation may by a majority vote dismiss the employee or may
defer its action for not more than five days .
The Board of Education may on recommendation of the
Superintendent suspend an employee pending similar action
to terminate his employment if, in its judgment , the charac-
ter of the charges warrants such action . If after the hearing
the decision of the Board is against termination of employ-
ment , the suspended employee shall be paid for his full
salary for the period of such suspension .

CHAPTER 201 547
The employee shall have the right to make an appeal
both as to law and as to fact to the Circuit Court . If said
appeal is not made within thirty days after the final action.
of the Board , then the decision of the Board of Education
shall be final . Either the employee or the Board may appeal
from the action of the Court to the Court of Appeals .
Section VIII . Solicitations , Political Activity Forbidden .
No person in the service described under this Act
shall directly or indirectly give , solicit , or receive or be in
any manner concerned in giving , soliciting , or receiving
any assessment , subscription , or contribution for any po-
litical party or for any purposes whatever . No person con-
nected with said service shall orally or by letter solicit or
in any manner бe concerned in the soliciting of any assess-
ment , subscription or contribution for any political party
or for any purpose from any person holding a position
under this Act .
No person in any branch of this service shall act as
an officer of any political organization or be a member
thereof or take any active part in any political campaign
or serve as a member of any committee of any political
organization or circulate or seek signatures to any petition
provided for any primary or general election or act as a
worker in the poles in favor of or opposed to any can-
didate for election or nomination to any public office whether .
Federal , State , County , or Municipal .
Section IX . Political or Religious Opinions Not to Aƒ-
fect Appointments .
No question in any examination held under the pro-
visions of this Act which relate to any political or religious
opinions , affiliations , or service , and no appointment , trans-
fer , promotion, increase or reduction in compensation , sus-
pension , or removal shall be affected or influenced by such
opinions or affiliations .
Section X. Violation of Act . Penalty .
Any person charged with the administration of this Act
who shall violate any of the provisions thereof , shall be
guilty of a misdemeanor and shall upon conviction be pun-
ished by a fine of not less than fifty ( $50.00 ) dollars nor
more than one thousand ( $ 1,000 ) dollars .

548 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section XI . Manual of Instructions .
The Civil Service Commission created herein shall
provide a Manual of Instructions consistent with the pro-
visions of this Act for the use of persons who wish to acquire
a knowledge of the rules and regulations of said Com-
mission and the duties of the members of the employees
group under this Act .
Section XII . Accepting Money in Addition to Compen-
sation Forbidden .
No member of any Civil Service Commission , Officer ,
or Employee shall receive or accept any money or compen-
sation not provided by law for any services or neglect of
duty in addition to this salary or compensation provided
for herein .
Section XIII . Salaries of Employees ; Notice of : In-
crease or Reduction .
Each Board of Education shall cause notice to be
given annually not later than July 1 , to each employee under
this Act for the succeeding school year stating the best
estimate as to the salary to be paid each employee during
such year . Such salary shall not be lower than the salary
paid during the preceding year unless such reduction be
a part of a uniform plan affecting the entire district . But
nothing herein shall prevent increases of salary after the
Board's annual notice has been given.
Section XIV . Leaves of Absence .
Upon written request of an employee a Board of Edu-
cation may grant a leave of absence for a period of not
more than two consecutive years where illness , maternity ,
or other disability is the reason for the request . Upon
subsequent request , such leave may be renewed by the
Board . Upon the return to service of an employee at the
expiration of a leave of absence , he shall resume the status
which he held prior to such leave .
Section XV . Transfers and Reinstatements .
With the consent of the Commission the Board of
Education may transfer a person holding an office or posi-
tion in the classified service to a comparable position in
another office , department or institution having the same
pay and similar duties ; but no transfer shall be made from

classification.summary: This act amends Kentucky law to allow domestic life insurance companies to invest their capital, surplus, and accumulations in loans to World War II veterans guaranteed by the U.S. government under the "Service Men's Readjustment Act of 1944," and in bonds, notes, or debentures of solvent, incorporated companies that have not defaulted on payments in the preceding ten years and whose obligations are not subordinate to other liens.

CHAPTER 203
(S. B. 143 )
AN ACT to amend chapter 342 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , re-
lating to workmen's compensation , by amending and reenacting
Section 342.005 , and by adding to said chapter and enacting a new
section thereto , to be designated as section 342.995 , so as to amend
the system of workmen's compensation in idustrial accidents by
requiring employers engaged in business or occupations , defined
herein as "hazardous employment ," to secure , provide and pay
compensation for accidental injury or death resulting therefrom
arising out of and in the course of employment , and providing
penalties for non -compliance herewith .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
1. That section 342.005 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes be and the same is hereby amended and reenacted so
that the same , when amended and reenacted , shall read as
follows :
§ 342.005 ( 1 ) The term " employer " as used in this
chapter shall mean and include an individual , partnership , as-
sociation , corporation or other legal entity, including the legal
representatives of a deceased employer , and a receiver or
trustee of an individual , partnership , association or corpora-
tion or other legal entity. This chapter shall apply to all
employers having three or more employees regularly engaged
in the same occupation or business , and to their employees ,
except that it shall not apply to domestic employment , agri-
culture , steam railways , or such common carriers other than
steam railways for which a rule of liability is provided by the
laws of the United States : Provided however , it shall apply
to the operators of threshing machines used in threshing or
hulling grain or seeds. It shall affect the liability of the em-
ployers subject thereto to their employees for a personal in-
jury sustained by the employee by accident arising out of and

classification.title: AN ACT to amend chapter 342 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes, relating to workmen's compensation
classification.summary: This act amends Kentucky Revised Statutes Chapter 342 concerning workmen's compensation. It re-enacts Section 342.005 and adds Section 342.995 to modify the system for industrial accidents. Employers engaged in 'hazardous employment' are required to provide compensation for accidental injury or death arising out of and in the course of employment, with penalties for non-compliance. The definition of 'employer' is broadened, and the chapter applies to employers with three or more regularly engaged employees, with specific exceptions for domestic employment, agriculture, steam railways, and certain common carriers covered by federal law. It also includes operators of threshing machines.
classification.reasoning: The statute pertains to workmen's compensation and industrial accidents. It defines 'employer' and outlines requirements for compensation for accidental injuries. There is no mention of race, nor are there any provisions that implicitly discriminate based on race. The exceptions listed (domestic employment, agriculture, etc.) are based on industry type, not race. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law.

shall , except as provided in subsection ( 2 ) of section 342.015
and in section 342.170 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , be

d . Should any such employer fail to comply with the
provisions of this subsection ( 2 ) or with any of the provisions
of subsection ( 1 ) of said section 342.340 then such employer
shall not , in any suit at law by an employee or his repre-
sentative for personal injury or death by accident arising out
of and in the course of his employment be permitted to defend
any such suit at law upon the defenses that the employee was
guilty of contributory negligence , that the injury was caused
by the negligence of a fellow servant of the injured employee
and that the employee has assumed the risk of injury , or upon
any of said defenses ; and in addition thereto such an employer
shall be punished by a fine of not more than five hundred
dollars ( $ 500.00 ) or by imprisonment for not more than one
year , or both . Failure of an employer after imposition of the
foregoing penalty , to provide for the payment of compensa-
tion under this chapter after notice by the board to such em-
ployer to do so , shall , as to each such notice be deemed a fur-
ther violation in respect thereof and the same penalty shall
be imposed . If such employer is a corporation , the president
or treasurer , or both , shall be liable for such penalty .
e. Every employer subject to the provisions of this sub-
section ( 2 ) shall post and keep posted a notice in such form
and in such manner and at such places as the board shall
require, stating the fact as to whether or not the employer
has provided compensation under the provisions of said sub-
section in behalf of his employees and stating also that it is
optional with his employees to elect to accept compensation
as provided for in this chapter , as well as any other matter
which the board may deem necessary so that the employee
may be advised of his rights relating to the matter of com-
pensation . Any such employer who knowingly violates the
provisions hereof or who knowingly posts or keeps posted
any notice which states that the employer has provided com-
pensation for his employees when such is not the fact shall
be punished by a fine of not less than one hundred dollars
( $ 100.00 ) nor more than five hundred dollars ( $ 500.00 ) or by
imprisonment for a period of not less than thirty days nor
more than one year , or both .

(3) The term " hazardous employment " shall mean and

a. All work in or about quarries and mines of all kinds

b. All work when making , using or necessitating dan-
gerous proximity to gunpowder , blasting powder , dynamite ,
compressed air, or any other explosive ;
C. The erection or demolition of any bridge , building or

d . The operation of all elevators , elevating machinery
or derricks or hoisting apparatus used within or on the out-

such bridge, building or structure ;
e . All work on ladders or scaffolds of any kind elevated
twenty ( 20 ) feet or more above the ground or floor beneath
in the erection , construction , repair , painting or alteration of
any building , bridge , structure or other work in which the
same are used ;
f. All work of construction , operation , alteration or re-
pair where wires , cables , switchboards , or other apparatus
or machinery are in use charged with electrical current ;
g. All work in the construction , alteration or repair of
pole lines for telegraph , telephone or other purposes ;
h. All work in the construction and repair of tunnels ,
subways , dams , and viaducts ;
i . All work in mills , shops , works , yards , plants and fac-
tories where steam , electricity , or any other mechanical power
is used to operate machinery and appliances in and about
such premises .

It also specifies which sections of the Kentucky Revised Statutes are applicable to employers in these hazardous employments.

(4 ) Any employer subject to the provisions of this chap-
ter , but not subject to the provisions of subsection ( 2 ) of this

utes ; and any employers and employees who are by the pro-
visions of this section excepted from the provisions of this
chapter , including employers having less than three em-

§ 2. That there shall be added to chapter 342 of the
Kentucky Revised Statutes a new section to be designated as
section 342.995 which , when enacted , shall read as follows :
§ 342.995 The provisions of this act or chapter shall be
construed to be severable ; and if any of said provisions are
held unconstitutional or otherwise invalid , such invalidity
shall not affect the operation of the remaining portions
thereof . If any of the provisions of section 342.005 of this
chapter , shall be held unconstitutional or otherwise invalid ,
all elections by employers made prior to the passage of this
act shall be construed as being in full force and effect to the
same extent as though said invalid provisions had not been
enacted . Should the provisions of this act be held unconsti-
tutional or otherwise invalid , the period of time intervening
between the occurrence of injury to an employee or his death
and the determination of invalidity or unconstitutionality
shall not be computed as a part of the time limited by law for
the commencement of any action relating to such injuries or
death ; but the amount of any compensation which may have
been paid on account of such injury or death shall be de-
ducted from damages recovered on account of such injury or
death .
Passed March 6 , enrolled March 7, 1946 , not approved .

RE : SENATE BILL NO . 143

classification.title: An Act to amend Chapter 342 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes relating to Workmen's Compensation.
classification.summary: This act amends Chapter 342 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes concerning Workmen's Compensation. It allows employers not subject to subsection (2) to elect to operate under the chapter, and permits employers and employees excepted from the chapter (including those with fewer than three employees) to voluntarily subject themselves to it. It also adds a new section, 342.995, regarding the severability of the act's provisions and how to handle invalid or unconstitutional parts, particularly concerning elections made prior to the act's passage and the computation of time for actions related to injuries or death.

CHAPTER 204
(S. B. 144 )
AN ACT providing for county cooperation in the control of forest fires .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
1. County governing bodies may elect to cooperate with
the Division of Forestry in the county - wide control of forest
fires , and share in the expense of establishment and operation
of forest fire protection, forest fire detection , prevention
and suppression . When forest fire control is established in
any county under the terms of this act , then Sections 149.110
and 149.120 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes shall not ap-
ply to timberland owners in that county .
2. When a county governing body has elected to coop-
erate with the Division of Forestry , and has officially notified
the Division of Forestry in writing , the Division may estab-
lish the organization and facilities necessary for adequate
county -wide forest fire protection in such county.
3. When any forest warden sees or there is reported to
him a forest fire, in a county which has elected to cooperate
with the Division of Forestry under this Act , he shall repair
immediately to the scene of the fire and employ such persons
and means as , in his judgment, are expedient and necessary
to extinguish the fire , within the limits of the expense he has
been authorized to incur in his instructions from the Direcor
of the Division of Forestry . He shall keep an itemized account
of all expenses thus incurred and immediately send the ac-
count verified by affidavit to the Director of the Division of
Forestry for examination . Upon approval by the Director
of the Division of Forestry , the account shall be paid from
such fund or funds as are available to the Division for such
entries.citation: Acts of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky, 1946, p. 573

CHAPTER 205
(S. B. 145 )
AN ACT relating to forest fire protection .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
1. Section 149.100 , KRS 1944 , is amended to read as
follows :
149.100 ( 2007j -4a-4 ; 2007j -4a- 5 ) CONSTRUCTION OF
KRS 149.110 and 149.120 ( 1 ) " Timberland " as used in KRS
149.110 and 149.120 means any land which has enough timber
or woody brush , standing or down , to constitute , in the judg-
ment of the Division of Forestry , a fire menace to itself or
adjoining lands , but does not include lands under cultivation
or entirely in grass , nor land that is an isolated fire risk
unless a forest fire on it would imperil the lands of an ad-
joining landowner .
(2 ) Nothing in KRS 149.110 and 149.120 shall relieve
the owner , lessee or user of any land from civil liability for
damage resulting from any fire for which he, his agents or
employees are responsible .
(3) " Owner " as used in KRS 149.110 and 149.120
means owner in fee simple except where the rights to cut or
use timber on any timberland are leased , in which case the
lessee shall be considered the owner for the duration of his
lease , for the purposes of this law.
2. Section 149.110 , KRS 1944 , is amended to read as
follows :
149.110 ( 2007j -4a ; 2007j -4a-2 ; 2007j -4a-3) OWNER OF
LAND TO PROVIDE FOREST FIRE PATROL .
( 1 ) During the seasons of the year when there is danger
of forest fires , every owner of timberland shall provide a
sufficient forest fire patrol , meeting with the approval of the
Division of Forestry , or in lieu thereof pay into the Division
of Forestry , at such time as is specified by the Division , one

(a ) In criminal cases the fees shall be paid , upon the
order of the presiding judge , by the county in which the court
is situated , if the transcript is ordered for the use of the
Commonwealth's Attorney . If the transcript is ordered upon
the motion of the defendant , he shall pay for it if, in the
opinion of the court, he is able to do so . If it appears that

shall direct the same to be paid by the county .
(b) In civil cases the fees shall be paid forthwith by

and shall be taxed as part of the costs of the action . The re-
porter shall not be required to file a transcript in any civil
case without payment therefor by the party in whose behalf
it is ordered.
( 2 ) For duplicate copies of transcripts reporters of cir-
cuit courts , other than courts of continuous session and courts
not of continuous session in counties containing a city of the
second class , shall be allowed one-half the amount they are
allowed for the original , to be paid for in the same manner
as the original .

CHAPTER 207
(S. B. 159 )
AN ACT relating to admission of persons to practice law in Kentucky ,
and making appropriations for the purposes thereof .
Be it Enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 30.030 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes of 1944 is amended to read as follows :
The Court of Appeals of Kentucky shall make such rules
and regulations as it deems proper regulating the admission
of persons to practice law in this state , including , among other
things , regulations as to qualifications , application and license
fees , and examinations . The full and complete power and
authority of the Court of Appeals to make such rules and regu-
lations and to change and amend the same from time to time is
hereby recognized and confirmed .

CHAPTER 209
( S. B. 162 )
AN ACT prohibiting the operation of a motor vehicle while under the
influence of intoxicating liquors or narcotic drugs and requiring
the enforcement thereof by peace officers and Highway Patrolmen .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 189.520 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , 1944
edition , is amended to read as follows :
" (1) No person under the influence of intoxicating
liquors or narcotic drugs shall operate a vehicle that is not
a motor vehicle upon a highway.
" (2) No person shall operate a motor vehicle on a
highway while under the influence of intoxicating liquors or
narcotic drugs .
" (3) No peace officer or Highway Patrolman shall fail
to enforce rigidly this section . '
99
Passed and enrolled March 21 , approved March 23 , 1946 .
CHAPTER 210
(S. B. 164 )
AN ACT to repeal sections 325.010 to 325.990 both inclusive of Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes ; to regulate the practice of public accounting ;
to create a state board of accountancy and to prescribe its powers
and duties ; and to provide penalties for violations of the provisions
of this Act.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
SEC . 1. NAME . This Act may be cited as the " Public
Accounting Act of 1946. "
SEC . 2. BOARD OF ACCOUNTANCY . There is here-
by created a State Board of Accountancy in and for the State
of Kentucky . The Board shall consist of three members , ap-
pointed by the Governor . Whenever an appointment is to
be made the Kentucky Society of Certified Public Account-
ants shall submit to the Governor the names of three persons
qualified for membership on the Board and the Governor
shall appoint one of the three . Members of the Board shall
be citizens of the United States and residents of this State

Act , the term of each to be designated by the Governor . Their

Vacancies occurring during a term shall be filled by appoint-

Governor shall remove from the Board any member whose

revoked or suspended , and may , after hearing , remove any
member of the Board for neglect of duty or other just cause .
The Board shall elect annually a president , a secretary
and a treasurer from its members . The office of secretary
and treasurer may be held by the same individual . The Board
may adopt , and amend from time to time , regulations for the
orderly conduct of its affairs and for the administration of
this Act . The Board may also promulgate , and amend from
time to time , rules of professional conduct appropriate to
establish and maintain a high standard of integrity and
dignity in the profession of public accounting . A majority
of the Board shall constitute a quorum for the transaction of
business . The Board shall have a seal which shall be judicially
noticed . The Board shall keep records of its proceedings , and
in any proceeding in court , civil or criminal , arising out of
or founded upon any provision of this Act , copies of said
records certified as correct under the seal of the Board shall
be admissible in evidence as tending to prove the content of
said records . The Board shall have printed and published
for public distribution , in July of each year , an annual regis-
ter which shall contain the names , arranged alphabetically by
classifications , of all individuals and partnerships holding
permits to practice under this Act ; the names of the members
of the Board ; and such other matters as may be deemed
proper by the Board . Copies of said registers shall be mailed
to each registered practitioner . The Board may employ clerks
and arrange for assistance in the performance of its duties .

Examinations provided for herein shall be held by the
Board as often as may be necessary in the opinion of the
Board , but not less frequently than once each year . A candi-
date for the certificate of " certified public accountant " who
fails in all subjects shall have the right to any number of re-
examinations . A candidate who passes a satisfactory exami-
nation in any subject shall have the right to be re-examined in
the remaining subjects only, at subsequent examinations held
by the Board , and if he passes in the remaining subjects
within a period of time specified in the rules of the Board , he
shall be considered to have passed the examination . The
Board shall charge each candidate a fee of $ 25 for the initial
examination . This fee shall be payable by the candidate at
the time of making application for examination . Should the
candidate fail to pass the examination , re -examinations will
be given the same candidate for an additional fee of $ 10 for
each examination irrespective of the number of subjects
therein covered .
Any individual who has received from the Board a cer-
tificate of " certified public accountant " and who holds an
annual permit to practice issued under sec . 8 of this Act shall
be styled and known as a " certified public accountant " and
may also use the abbreviation " CPA . " The Board shall keep
a list of certified public accountants . Any certified public
accountant may also be known as a " public accountant . "
Individuals who at the time of the enactment of this Act
held certified public accountant certificates heretofore issued
under the laws of this State shall not be required to secure
additional certificates under this Act , but shall otherwise be
subject to all of its provisions ; and such certificates hereto-
fore issued shall, for all purposes , be considered certificates

ant " to , any person possessing the other qualifications men-
tioned in this section who is the holder of a certificate as

SEC . 4. REGISTRATION OF FOREIGN ACCOUNT-
ANTS . The Board may , in its discretion , permit the regis-
tration of any person of good moral character who is the
holder of a certificate , license , or degree authorizing him to
practice public accounting in a foreign country . A person
so registered shall use only the title under which he is gen-
erally known in his own country , followed by the name of
the country from which he received his certificate , license or
degree .
SEC . 5. PARTNERSHIPS COMPOSED OF CERTI-
FIED PUBLIC ACCOUNTANTS - REGISTRATION
THEREOF . A partnership engaged in this State in the prac-
tice of public accounting may register with the Board as a
partnerhsip of certified public accountants provided it meets
all of the following requirements :
(a ) At least one general partner thereof must be a
certified public accountant of this State in good standing , and
(b ) Each partner thereof personally engaged within
this State in the practice of public accounting as a member
thereof must be a certified public accountant of this State in
good standing, and
(c ) Each partner thereof must be a certified public
accountant of some state in good standing , and
(d ) Each resident manager in charge of an office of
the firm in this State must be a certified public accountant
of this State in good standing . Application for such registra-
tion must be made upon the affidavit of a general partner of
such partnership who is a certified public accountant of this
State in good standing . The Board shall in each case deter-
mine whether the applicant partnership is eligible for regis-
tration . A partnership which is so registered and which holds
a permit issued under sec . 8 of this Act may use the words
"certified public accountants " or the abbreviation " CPA's"
in connection with its partnership name . Notification shall
be given the Board , within one month , after the admission to
or withdrawal of a partner from any partnership so regis-
tered .

SEC . 6. PUBLIC ACCOUNTANTS -REGISTRATION
THEREOF . Any person (a ) who is a resident of this State ,
or has a place of business , or is employed therein, and ( b )
who has attained the age of twenty -one years , and ( c ) who
is of good moral character , and ( d ) who meets the require-
ments of subdivision ( 1 ) or ( 2 ) or ( 3 ) of this section , may
register as a public accountant with the Board on or before
the first day of July , 1946 .
(1 ) Persons who held themselves out to the public as
public accountants and who were engaged within this State
at the effective date of this Act in the practice of public ac-
counting as their principal occupation .
(2) Staff accountants employed by certified public ac-
countants or by public accountants and regularly assigned to
accounting engagements at the effective date of this Act.
(3 ) Persons serving in the armed forces of the United
States or any of the United Nations who immediately prior
to entering such service held themselves out to the public as
public accountants and were engaged in the practice of public
accounting as their principal occupation , or who were em-
ployed as staff accountants by certified public accountants or
by public accountants and regularly assigned to accounting
engagements . In the case of any such person serving in the
armed forces of the United States or any of the United Na-
tions on the effective date of this Act , the time for registra-
tion shall be extended for a period of twelve months from the
time such person is honorably discharged from such service .
The Board sahll in each case determine whether the ap-
plicant is eligible for registration . Any individual who is so
registered and who holds a permit issued under sec . 8 of this
Act shall be styled and known as a " public accountant . "
SEC . 7. PARTNERSHIPS COMPOSED OF PUBLIC
ACCOUNTANTS -REGISTRATION THEREOF . A part-
nership engaged in this State in the practice of public ac-
counting may register with the Board as a partnership of
public accountants provided it meets all of the following re-
quirements :
(a ) At least one general partner thereof must be a cer-
tified public accountant or a public accountant of this State
in good standing, and
(b) Each partner thereof personally engaged within

SEC . 10. REVOCATION OR SUSPENSION OF
PARTNERSHIP REGISTRATION OR PERMIT . After

shall revoke the registration and permit to practice of a
partnership if at any time it does not have all the qualifica-
tions prescribed by the section of this Act under which it
qualified for registration .
After notice and hearing as provided in sec . 11 of this
Act the Board may revoke or suspend the registration or
may revoke , suspend or refuse to renew the permit to prac-

SEC . 11. HEARING BEFORE BOARD-NOTICE-
PROCEDURE- REVIEW . ( a ) Commencement of Proceed-
ing . The Board may initiate proceedings under this Act either
on its own motion or on the complaint of any person .
(b ) Notice - Service and Contents . A written notice
stating with reasonable particularity the nature of the charge
and such acts of professional misconduct as are complained
of against the accused and the time and place of the hearing
before the Board on such charges shall be served on the ac-
cused not less than 30 days prior to the date of the hearing
either personally or by mailing a copy thereof by registered
mail to the address of the accused last known to the Board .
( c ) Failure to Appear . If, after having been served
with the notice of hearing as provided for herein , the accused
fails to appear at said hearing, the Board may hear evi-
dence against him and may enter such order as shall be justi-
fied by the evidence , which order shall be final unless he
petitions for a review thereof as provided herein : Provided ,
however , that within 30 days from the date of any order ,
upon a showing of good cause for failing to appear , the
Board may reopen said proceedings and may permit the ac-
cused to submit evidence in his behalf .
( d) Counsel - Witnesses - Cross -examination . At any
hearing the accused may appear in person or by counsel , pro-
duce evidence and witnesses on his own behalf, cross -examine
witnesses , and examine such evidence and documents as may
be produced against him. The accused shall be entitled , on
application to the Board , to the issuance of subpoenas to
compel the attendance of witnesses on his behalf .
( e ) Subpoenas -Oaths . The Board , or any member
thereof, may issue subpoenas to compel the attendance of wit-
nesses and the production of documents , and may administer
oaths , take testimony, hear proof and receive exhibits in
evidence in connection with or at the hearing under this Act .
In case of disobedience to.a subpoena the Board may invoke
the aid of the Franklin Circuit Court . Any order or sub-
poena of such court requiring the attendance and testimony

(f) Evidence . The Board shall not be bound by tech-

(g ) Record . A stenographic record of the hearing shall
be kept and a transcript thereof filed with the Board .
(h) Attorney for the Board . At all hearings the At-
torney General of this State , or one of his assistants desig-
nated by him shall appear and represent the Board .
(i ) Decision . The decision of the Board shall be by
majority vote thereof.
(j ) Review by Court . Any person adversely affected
by any order of the Board may obtain a review thereof by
filing a written petition for review with the Franklin Circuit
Court within 60 days after the entry of said order . The
petition shall state the grounds upon which the review is
asked and shall pray that the order of the Board be modi-
fied or set aside in whole or in part . A copy of such petition
shall be forthwith served upon any member of the Board and
thereupon the Board shall certify and file in the Court a tran-
script of the record upon which the order complained of was
entered . The case shall then be tried de novo on the record
but the parties shall be permitted to file briefs . The Court
may affirm , modify or set aside the Board's order in whole
or in part , or may remand the case to the Board for further
evidence , and may in its discretion, stay the effect of the
Board's order pending its determination of the case . The
Court's order shall have the force and effect of a decree in
equity and may be appealed from to the Court of Appeals .
SEC . 12. REINSTATEMENT . The Board may re-
issue the certificate of any certified public accountant whose
certificate shall have been revoked , or may permit the re-
registration of any person whose registration has been re-
voked , or may reissue or modify the suspension of any per-
mit to practice public accounting which has been revoked or
suspended .

person shall assume or use the title or designation " certified

CHAPTER 210 579
SEC . 14. EXCEPTIONS -ACTS NOT PROHIBITED .
Nothing contained in this Act shall prohibit any individual
not a certified public accountant or public accountant from
serving as an employee of , or an assistant to , a certified public
accountant or public accountant or partnership composed of
certified public accountants or public accountants holding a
permit to practice under sec . 8 of this Act ; provided that
such employee or assistant shall not issue any accounting
or financial statement over his name .
Nothing contained in this Act shall prohibit a certified
public accountant or a registered public accountant of another
state , or any accountant of a foreign country holding a cer-
tificate , degree or license which permits him to practice
therein, from temporarily practicing in this state on pro-
fessional business incident to his regular practice .
SEC . 15. INJUNCTION AGAINST UNLAWFUL
ACT . Whenever in the judgment of the Board any person
has engaged , or is about to engage , in any acts or practices
which constitute , or will constitute , a violation of sec . 13 of
this Act , the Board may make application to the Franklin Cir-
cuit Court for an order enjoining such acts or practices , and
upon a showing by the Board that such person has engaged ,
or is about to engage , in any such acts or practices , an in-
junction , restraining order , or such other order as may be
appropriate shall be granted by such court . The same shall
be enforceable and shall be valid anywhere in this State and
shall be reviewable as provided in the code of Civil Practice
in the case of other injunctions and restraining orders .
SEC . 16. MISDEMEANORS-PENALTY . Any per-
son who violates any provision of sec . 13 of this Act , shall be
guilty of a misdemeanor , and upon conviction thereof , shall
be subject to a fine of not more than five hundred dollars , or
to imprisonment for not more than one year , or to both such
fine and imprisonment . Whenever the Board has reason to
believe that any person is liable to punishment under this
section it may certify the facts to the Attorney General of
this State , who may , in his discretion, cause appropriate pro-
ceedings to be brought .
SEC . 17. SINGLE ACT EVIDENCE OF PRACTICE .
The display or uttering by a person of a card, sign , advertise-
ment or other printed , engraved or written instrument or de-

SEC . 18. OWNERSHIP OF ACCOUNTANTS '
WORKING PAPERS . All statements , records , schedules ,
working papers , and memoranda made by a certified public
accountant or public accountant incident to or in the course
of professional service to client by such certified public ac-
countant or public accountant , except reports submitted by a
certified public accountant or public accountant to a client,
shall be and remain the property of such certified public ac-
countant or public accountant , in the absence of an express
agreement between the certified public accountant or public
accountant and the client to the contrary .
SEC . 19. EXEMPTION FROM JURY SERVICE . An
individual holding a permit issued under sec . 8 of this Act
shall be exempt from service on any jury .
SEC . 20. PRIVILEGED COMMUNICATION. A cer-
tified public accountant or public accountant shall not be re-
quired by any court to divulge information or evidence which
has been obtained by him in his confidential capacity as such .
SEC . 21. DEFINITIONS . (1) Board- The term
"Board " when used in this Act means the " State Board of
Accountancy' created by sec . 2 of this Act .
(2) State The term " state " when used in this Act
includes and means any state , territory or insular possession

CHAPTER 211
(S. B. 171 )
AN ACT relating to cities of the first class and to counties containing
a city of the first class and enabling and authorizing said city and
county to create a joint city and county Board of Public Welfare
and combine and consolidate in said Boards the several departments ,
boards and other organizations handling the same or similar func-
tions and to transfer to said Board the authority , functions , powers
and duties of its constituent departments , boards or other organiza-
tions ; and to authorize and empower the said Board when created
to function as a joint board of the ctiy and county to carry out the
Public Welfare activities of said ciy and county .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. The Fiscal Court of any county containing a
city or cities of the first class and the legislative body of any
city of the first class in said county may , upon their joint
action , combine , consolidate , and jointly control, as herein
provided , such departments , boards , and other organizations
now existing or which in the future shall be created for the
purpose of serving either the county or the city , or both
in the administration and supervision of : all forms of public
assistance , general relief , and social services to adults and
children in homes or eleemosynary and correctional insti-
tutions ; city and county eleemosynary institutions ; city and
county penal institutions including the establishment of rules
and regulations for the custody , government , and parole of
inmates ; other public welfare activities or services which
may be placed within control of the Board .

a quorum. Two members shall be appointed by the mayor

fifth member of the Board . The members first appointed
shall serve for terms of one , two , three , and four years respec-
tively , with the county judge's appointees serving the two
and four year terms , the mayor's appointees serving the one
and three year terms , and the fifth appointee serving a four
year term ; thereafter the terms of office of all members shall
be four years . Upon any vacancy being created through
expiration of the term of office or otherwise , appointment
shall be made as herein provided with the persons or persons
who appointed the member leaving the board appointing his
successor . In the event of a vacancy being created by any
reason other than the expiration of the term, the appointment
shall be for the remainder of the unexpired term only . All
vacancies shall be filled by appointment made within 30 days
from the creation of the vacancy . Should the appointing of-
ficer having the right to make such appointment fail to do so,
then the appointment shall be made by the Board . Members
shall be eligible for reappointment . Members may be re-
moved by the joint action of the county judge and mayor for
inefficiency , neglect of duty , malfeasance , misfeasance , or im-
proper conduct , providing , however , that the removed Board
member shall have the right within ten days from his re-
moval , by a request in writing , to require the appointing of-
ficers to file with the Board a written statement setting
forth the reason for such removal .

CHAPTER 212
( S. B. 172 )
AN ACT relating to the operation of electric and water systems and
facilities by cities of the third class .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. The provisions of this Act shall not apply in
the case of any municipality of the third class now operating
an electric or water system or plant under any existing law ;
but the governing body of any municipality of the third class
now or hereafter owning an electric and water system and
operating them as one combined system or plant may elect
to operate under the provisions of this Act, in which case ,
from the time of the exercise of such election and the appoint-
ment of a board hereunder , the electric and water system of
such municipality shall be operated under the provisions of
this Act as an electric and water plant .
Section 2. Any municipality of the third class now or
hereafter owning and operating an electric system and a
water system and operating them as one combined system or
plant may elect to operate such systems as an electric and
water plant under the provisions of this Act by enacting an
ordinance declaring therein the desire and intention of the
municipality to accept and operate its electric and water
system or plant under the provisions of this Act and by pro-
viding in said ordinance that the municipality accepts and
agrees to all of the provisions of this Act . The ordinance
shall further authorize the mayor or chief executive to ap-
point a board , subject to the approval of the appointments
by the governing body of the municipality . Upon the passage
of such ordinance the mayor or chief executive of any such
municipality , shall with the approval of the governing body
of the municipality , appoint a board of public utilities , con-
sisting of five ( 5 ) citizens , taxpayers , voters , and users of
electric energy or water. Said board shall be appointed and
qualified before the municipality shall have any authority
to proceed further under the provisions of this Act . Said
board , when so appointed and qualified , shall be and hereby
is declared to be a body -politic and corporate , with perpetual
succession ; and said board may contract and be contracted
with , sue and be sued , in and by its corporate name , and have

four years , respectively , from the date of their appointment ,
as the said mayor or chief executive officer of the municipality
shall designate . Successors to retiring members so appointed

manner , prior to the expiration of the term of office of the
retiring members . Appointments to complete unexpired
terms shall be made in the same manner as original ap-
pointments .
Section 5. A majority of the board shall constitute a
quorum and the board shall act by vote of a majority present
at any meeting attended by a quorum. Vacancies in the board
shall not affect its power or authority so long as a quorum
remains . Within ten ( 10 ) days after appointment and quali-
fication of members , the board shall hold a meeting and elect
a chairman. The board shall at the same time elect a secre-
tary -treasurer , and shall fix his compensation , which, to-
gether with his compensation as a member of the board, shall
not exceed One Thousand Five Hundred ( $ 1500.00 ) Dollars
per annum . The secretary may be removed by the board for
cause . The board shall hold public meetings at least once
each month , at such regular time and place as the board may
determine . Any special meeting of the board may be called
by the chairman or by two members ; but if such special meet-
ing is called , written notice shall be sent to all members by
the secretary . Except as otherwise expressly provided here-
in, the board shall establish its own by-laws and rules of

classification.title: An Act Relating to Municipal Boards and Their Powers
classification.summary: This act outlines the structure, appointment, and operational procedures for municipal boards, particularly concerning the financing of plant operations and maintenance. It specifies how board members are appointed, their terms, quorum requirements, and the election of officers. Crucially, it states that the municipality is not liable for the board's salaries, compensation, or expenses, which must be paid solely from the revenues generated by the board's operations.
classification.reasoning: The provided statute text details the administrative and financial framework for municipal boards. It focuses on the appointment of board members, their terms, the establishment of a quorum, the election of officers, and the financial responsibilities of the board, emphasizing that the municipality is not liable for the board's debts. There is no mention of race, segregation, or any discriminatory practices within the text. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law. The document type is 'session_laws' as indicated by '590 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY'.

Section 6. Any member of the board may be removed
from office upon a vote of a majority of the members of the
governing body of the municipality for inefficiency , neglect
of duty , misfeasance , nonfeasance , or malfeasance in office .
Section 7. Any board operating under the provisions
of this Act shall have the legal power and capacity to per-
form any act not repugnant to law and shall have the express
power and capacity to do any and all acts or things necessary
or convenient for the carrying out of the purposes of this
Act, including , but not by way of limitation , the following
express powers :
( 1 ) Acquire property , real and personal , tangible and
intangible , necessary or incident to the proper conduct of its
business .
(2 ) Operate , maintain , improve and extend the electric
and water plant , and provide electric and water service to
any user or consumer within and without the boundaries of
any municipality , and charge and collect reasonable rates
therefor .
( 3) To fix and determine classifications , rates and
charges for services ; provided , however , the rates and
charges so fixed and determined at all times shall be suf-
ficient to produce revenues sufficient to pay all operating
expenses , interest, and bond requirements , sinking fund re-
quirements , adequate depreciation reserves , taxes , or pay-
ments in lieu of taxes , and reserves for contemplated exten-
sions and improvements .
(4 ) Construct , lease , operate and control any and all
works, lines , buildings and other facilities across , along or
under any street or public highway, and over any lands which
are now or may be the property of the Commonwealth or of
any county or municipality within this Commonwealth . The
board shall , however , at its own expense , restore any such
street or highway to its former condition and state as nearly
as may be possible and shall not use the same in a manner
as to impair its usefulness or to interfere with or obstruct
the maintenance thereof . Before exercising these powers the
board shall obtain a permit or consent or approval in writing
from the governing authority of the municipality , or the
fiscal court , or the Department of Highways , having appro-

classification.title: An Act relating to the powers of boards operating electric and water plants.
classification.summary: This act grants broad powers to a board appointed to manage electric and water plants. These powers include entering into contracts, acquiring property through eminent domain, making surveys, and establishing pension plans for employees. It also allows the board to operate under the Kentucky Workmen's Compensation Act.

CHAPTER 212 595
forcement and execution of all rules , regulations , programs ,

( 5 ) The superintendent shall let all contracts , subject
to the approval of the board ; but may , without such ap-
proval , obligate the board on purchase orders for materials
necessary in the operation of the electric and water system
up to an amount to be fixed by the board , not , however , to
exceed five hundred dollars . All contracts for the purchase
of materials , equipment and supplies in excess of one
thousand dollars shall be let only after competitive bidding ;
provided, however , when any material , equipment or sup-
plies be not available in the open market , such material ,
equipment or supplies may be purchased without competitive
bidding . All contracts shall be in the corporate name of the
board and shall be signed by the superintendent and attested
by the secretary -treasurer or the chairman of the board .
The superintendent shall make and keep or cause to be made
and kept full and proper books and records , subject to the
supervision and direction of the board and the provisions
of applicable contracts .
Section 9. The board shall charge the municipality and
all departments and works thereof for any electric and water
service furnished to them at the rate applicable to other
customers taking servce under similar conditions . Revenues
derived from such service shall be treated as other revenues .
Section 10. Any board proceeding under this Act shall
have the right to acquire by the exercise of the power of
eminent domain , all lands , easements , rights of way , either
upon or under or above the ground, reasonably necessary
or desirable in connection with the operation , maintenance ,
improvement or extension of its electric and water plant .
The condemnation or eminent domain proceedings shall be
brought in the name of the board , and proceed in the
form and manner now prescribed for the condemnation
of land by cities of their respective class .
Section 11. In lieu of taxes , the board may each year
pay to each school district and municipality in which its
property is located an amount equivalent to an annual ad
valorem tax on the fair cash value of the property of the
board located in each such jurisdiction determined upon
the tax rate prevailing in such year .

classification.title: An Act relating to the establishment and operation of electric and water systems by boards.
classification.summary: This act establishes a board to oversee the operation of electric and water systems. It outlines the powers and duties of the superintendent and the board, including contract letting, purchase orders, eminent domain for land acquisition, and payments in lieu of taxes to school districts and municipalities. The board is to charge municipalities for services at rates applicable to other customers and can acquire necessary property through eminent domain.

late the form of pension plan to be used ; determine and
prescribe the eligibility of employees or their dependents
to a pension or other benefits ; determine and prescribe the

pendents so determined to be eligible for a pension or bene-
fits under the pension plan , not to exceed , however , a sum
equal to one -half of the monthly salary or wages of any em-
ployee at the time of his or her retirement, but which in
no event shall be less than twenty dollars ($ 20.00 ) per
month nor more than eighty -five dollars ( $ 85.00 ) per month ;
appoint a commission , which shall consist of three members
possessing the qualifications of a member of the board, for
the administration of the pension plan and prescribe the
powers and duties of such commission ; appoint a trustee of
the pension fund , fix the term of his office and the com-
pensation of such trustee , and prescribe the powers and
duties of such trustee and do and perform any other or
further acts necessary to effectuate such pension plan . When
a pension plan shall have been adopted , a commission ap-
pointed to administer such plan and a trustee of the pension
fund appointed and qualified , the board may annually ap-
propriate and pay out of its operating revenue , as an op-
erating expense , into the sinking fund , a sum sufficient ,
when determined on a fair actuarial basis , to maintain the
pension plan so adopted , not exceeding , however , a sum
equal to one -half of one per cent of the fair value of the
Utility property and assets . The board may assess , and
cause to be paid into the pension fund monthly such amount
or percent of the salary of all employees eligible under and
electing to accept the pension plan as may be equitably de-
termined on a fair actuarial basis , not to exceed , however ,
three per cent ( % ) of the monthly salary of any employee .
The trustee of the pension fund shall give such bond as
required by the board , the cost of which shall be payable
out of the pension fund . The trustee of the pension fund
shall each year file with the board a report showing his
actions and his accounts as such trustee .
The board shall have full power to receive any and all

(b) If a majority of all the qualified voters of the muni-

classification.title: An Act relating to the operation of electric and water systems by municipalities
classification.summary: This act outlines the process by which a municipality can hold an election to decide whether to terminate the operation and management of its electric and water system by an Electric and Water Plant Board. It details the election procedures, including ballot printing, advertising, and voting rights. If a majority votes in favor of termination, the governing body can pass an ordinance to transfer operations back to the municipality. The act also addresses the issuance and sale of revenue bonds for plant improvements and refunding bonds.
classification.reasoning: The provided statute text details the administrative and financial procedures for a municipality to manage its electric and water systems, including the process for holding an election on the termination of a plant board's operation. There is no mention of race, segregation, or any discriminatory practices. Therefore, it is not a Jim Crow law. The document type is 'session_laws' as indicated by '598 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY' and the year 1946.

and accrued interest, as may be fixed by the board by reso-
lution prior to the sale of the bonds .
( 3 ) All revenue bonds issued pursuant to the pro-
visions of this Act in the hands of bona fide holders shall
have all the qualities and incidence of negotiable instruments
under the law of merchant . All bonds shall be sold to the
highest responsible bidder at such time and place as shall
have been fixed by the board in the notice of sale of said
bonds , which notice shall have been advertised once each week
for at least two consecutive weeks in a newspaper of general
circulation in said municipality . The board shall receive
written , sealed competitive bids , which shall be publicly opened
and read at the time and place specified in the notice of sale .
The board may reject all bids and readvertise . No revenue
bonds shall be sold for less than par , and no bond shall bear
interest at a rate in excess of six per cent ( 6 % ) per annum ,
interest payable semi -annually .
(4 ) No holder or holders of any revenue bonds issued
under this Act shall have the right to compel any exercise of
taxing power of the municipality to pay the bonds or the
interest thereon . Each bond issued under this Act shall recite
in substance that the bond , including interest thereon , is pay-
able solely from the revenues pledged to the payment thereof ,
and that the bond does not constitute a debt of the muni-
cipality within the meaning of any statutory or constitutional
provision or limitation .
(5 ) Any holder or holders of bonds issued pursuant to
this Act, shall have the right , in addition to all other rights :
(a) By action in court , to enforce his or their rights
against the board , and any other proper officer , agent or em-
ployee , including , but without limitation , the right to require
the board, and any proper officer , agent or employee thereof ,
to fix and collect rates and charges adequate to carry out any
agreement as to , or pledge of , revenues from the plant , and
to require the board and any officer , agent or employee thereof,
to carry out any other covenants or agreements and to per-
form its and their duties under this Act.

It details aspects such as the place of payment, sale blocks, terms of purchase and redemption, interest rates, and the negotiable nature of the bonds. It also specifies that bondholders cannot compel the municipality to exercise its taxing power for repayment, and that bonds are payable solely from pledged revenues.

Section 18. No board operating an electric and water
plant under the provisions of his Act shall construct any
facilities or extend its existing facilities in competition with
any existing facilities of any Rural Electric Cooperative Cor-
poration .
Section 19. Any action challenging the validity of any
ordinance electing to operate under this Act, or any bond
resolution of the board , or any election resolution or election
held hereunder shall be brought within sixty days from the
date on which such ordinance , election resolution or bond
resolution was adopted or election held , as the case may be,
and , if not brought within said time , shall be forever barred .
Section 20. Every ordinance or resolution adopted by
the governing body of the municipality or board under the
provisions of this Act shall become effective from and after
its passage , and no such resolution or ordinance shall be sub-
ject to any referendum or election except as expressly pro-
vided in this Act .
Section 21. All sections , clauses , sentences , and parts
of this Act are severable and are not matters of mutual
essential inducements ; and it is the intention of the General
Assembly to confer upon every municipality of the third
class the whole or any part of the powers of this Act pro-
vided ; and if any one or more of these sections , clauses ,
sentences , or parts of this Act , including those authorizing the
payment by the board of taxes , shall be adjudged invalid or
inoperative , such judgment shall not invalidate the remain-
ing provisions of said Act , but shall be confined in its opera-
tion to the specific provision or provisions so held invalid or
inoperative ; and the inapplicability or invalidation of any sec-
tion , clause , sentence or part of this Act in any one or more
instances shall not affect the applicability or validity in any
other instance , or the validity or application of any other
part or provision of the Act .

602 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 213
(S. B. 175)
AN ACT providing for the payment by cities of medical and hospital
bills incurred by any city employee who becomes disabled while
engaged in the performance of duty .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
There may be paid from the general fund of cities all
necessary and reasonable medical and hospital expenses in-
curred by any city employee who becomes totally or partially
disabled physically or mentally as a result of an injury re-
ceived by him or her while engaged in the performance of
duty.
Passed and enrolled March 21 , approved March 23, 1946 .
CHAPTER 214
(S. B. 178 )
AN ACT authorizing the establishment and erection of memorials in
honor of veterans of any one or all wars ; and authorizing cities
and counties to appropriate money therefor ; to act jointly in connec-
tion therewith ; authorizing the voting of bonds by the people for
such purposes ; and authorizing the establishment of a Commission
to manage and control the establishment or erection of same .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
( 1) Every county in this Commonwealth is authorized
to make provision for the establishment or erection of a
memorial or memorial building to commemorate the sacrifices
of its citizens serving or who has served in the armed forces
of the United States during any one or all wars .
(2 ) Every city in this Commonwealth is likewise so
authorized . Such city shall not be required to locate such
memorial within the city .
(3 ) Every city and every county are authorized to
undertake the establishment or erection of such memorial
jointly, and to share the expense thereof in such proportions
as may be agreed upon between the governing bodies of the
city and county .
( 4 ) Cities and counties are authorized to make appro-
priations for such purposes out of the current revenues , sub-
ject to revenue limitations .

CHAPTER 215
(S. B. 180 )
AN ACT to amend subsections ( 1 ) and ( 2 ) of Section 289.200 of the
Kentucky Revised Statutes , pertaining to Building and Loan As-
sociations .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Subsections ( 1 ) and ( 2 ) of section 289.200 of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes are amended to read as follows :
(1 ) Associations may make loans on the direct reduc-
tion plan . The board of directors may or the bylaws of the
association shall prescribe interest rates which may be
variable and may prescribe the duration of the loan , and the
loan shall be payable in equal weekly or monthly installments .
( 2 ) The applicant for such loan shall subscribe for and
shall pledge one or more shares of stock of the association
or the association may require the applicant for such loan to
subscribe for and pledge shares or fractional shares of stock ,
equal , when paid up , to the amount of the loan . Payment of
dues and interest shall be credited upon the loan and shares
in accordance with the direct reduction plan adopted . In con-
sideration of making loans upon such plan no dividend shall
be declared or paid or credited upon amounts credited as
dues or principal upon such loans , but all payments , made
on the loans shall be first applied to interest due to the date
of respective payments and the balance applied as dues on
principal , and interest shall be collected only on the balance .

CHAPTER 216
( S. B. 181 )
AN ACT relating to pawnbrokers .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Sec . 1. Any pawnbroker , as defined in KRS 226.010 ,
may , in loaning money on deposit of personal property ,
charge , contract for or receive interest at a rate not exceed-
ing three and one -half percent per month on the unpaid prin-
cipal balance of the loan, and may , in purchasing personal
property on condition of selling the property back again at a
stipulated price , fix such stipulated resale price at a sum not
exceeding an amount equal to the price at which the property
was purchased plus interest at the rate of three and one- half
percent per month . No pawnbroker shall directly or indi-
rectly charge , receive or contract for any interest or con-
sideration greater than that allowed by this section .
Sec . 2. Every pawnbroker , upon receiving any payment
of money from a borrower , shall give to such person a plain
and complete receipt for such payment , specifying separately
the amount applied to principal and the amount applied to
interest . In a case where the pawnbroker has purchased
personal property under an agreement to sell it back at a
stipulated price , the pawnbroker shall , on receiving any pay-
inent of money from the person from whom the property was
purchased , give such person a receipt stating the original
purchase price , the stipulated resale price , and the amount
received .
Sec . 3. Subsection ( 2 ) of Section 226.050 of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes is amended to read as follows :

CHAPTER 219
( S. B. 186 )
AN ACT authorizing certain persons to condemn land and rights of way
for the purpose of securing a water supply and necessary facilities
pertaining thereto .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
The owner of real estate serving the public , providing
sleeping accommodations and meals , when pure water is not
available in adequate quantities shall have the right to con-
demn on the banks of any navigable stream a tract of land
not more than twenty ( 20 ) feet square and above the high
water mark for a pumping station site and a right -of-way for
a pipe line from said site to the deep water of the navigable
stream and from said pumping station site to the point on the
owner's real estate where the water is desired for use . The
said property owner may condemn a right -of -way for an elec-
tric power line over the most direct course to the pumping
station site .
The provision of the Kentucky Statutes , Sections 381.-
660 , 381.670 , and 381.680 shall be complied with except that
when the pumping station site and pipe line are located on
cliff land and do not cross any tillable land the pumps and
pipe may be located above ground .
The procedure for condemnation shall be that set forth
in KRS 416.010 to 416.080 .
Passed March 7, enrolled March 8 , 1946 , not approved.

608 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 220
(S. B. 187 )
AN ACT authorizing counties and cities to condemn land for flood con-
trol purposes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
1. Whenever in the opinion of the fiscal court of any
county the title to or any interest in real property within the
county is needed for the purposes of flood control and the
fiscal court cannot acquire such property by agreement with
the owners thereof, whether by reason of disagreement as
to the price or by reason of any other cause , the fiscal court
shall adopt a resolution setting forth the foregoing facts and
ordering the condemnation of the required property , giving
a general description thereof, together with the names of
the owners thereof where they are known to the fiscal court ,
and directing the county attorney to institute the proceedings .
The condemnation proceedings may be had in the county
court under the procedure prescribed in KRS 416.110 , or
may be had in the circuit court under the procedure pre-
scribed in KRS 416.120 , the selection as to which procedure
to be followed being made by the fiscal court in the resolu-
tion ordering the condemnation .
§ 2. The legislative body of any city shall have the
same power to condemn real property , lying within or with-
out the corporate limits of the city, for flood control pur-
poses , as is given to the fiscal court of a county by section 1
of this Act . The condemnation proceedings shall be insti-
tuted and prosecuted by the city attorney .
3. This Act is intended to constitute an alternate
method of acquiring property for flood control purposes , in
addition to , and not as a substitute for , any other method
authorized by law .
Passed March 20 , enrolled March 21 , approved March 23 , 1946 .
