612 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 224
( S. B. 203 )
AN ACT relating to the salaries of the deputy commissioners of Circuit
Courts in counties having a population of 200,000 or more .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
That Section 27.080 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
( 2nd . Edition ) be repealed , amended and re -enacted to read
as follows :
" (1) When necessary to aid the commissioner in the
discharge of his duties the court may authorize him to ap-
point , with the approval of the court, deputies to assist him .
If more than one deputy be necessary , the court shall de-
termine how many deputies shall be appointed . The deputies
shall take the same oath taken by the commissioner , may per-
form any of his duties and may be removed at his pleasure .
( 2 ) In counties having a population of seventy -five
thousand or over, the number of deputies allowed to each
commissioner and receiver and the compensation allowed to
each deputy shall be fixed by an order of the circuit court
entered upon the order book , and a certified copy of the
order, and any subsequent changes made therein , shall , as
soon as entered , be forwarded to the Department of Finance .
(3 ) In counties having a population of two hundred
thousand or over , the circuit court, in general term , shall fix
the salary of the chief deputy commissioner at not exceeding
$4,800.00 per annum , and the salary of each other deputy
commissioner at not exceeding $4,200.00 per annum .
(4) The salaries provided for by subsections ( 2 ) and
(3) of this section shall be paid out of the fees of the office . "
Passed March 20 , enrolled March 21 , approved March 23 , 1946 .

CHAPTER 225 613
lishment of local service roads ; the prohibition of certain acts there-
on and provisions for penalties therefor ; and for other purposes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
SECTION 1. Declaration of policy- The legislature
hereby finds , determines , and declares that this act is neces-
sary for the immediate preservation of the public peace ,
health , and safety , and for the promotion of the general
welfare .
SECTION 2. Definition of a limited - access facility-
For the purpose of this act, a limited -access facility is de-
fined as a highway or street especially designed for through
traffic , and over , from or to which owners or occupants of
abutting land or other persons have no right or easement or
only a limited right or easement of access , light , air , or view
by reason of the fact that their property abuts upon such
limited -access facility or for any other reason . Such high-
ways or streets may be parkways , from which trucks , busses ,
and other commercial vehicles shall be excluded ; or they may
be freeways open to use by all customary forms of street and
highway traffic .
SECTIONS 3. Authority to establish limited - access
facilities — The highway authorities of the State , counties ,
cities , towns , and villages , acting alone or in cooperation ,
with each other or with any Federal , State , or local agency
or any other State having authority to participate in the
construction and maintenance of highways , are hereby
authorized to plan , designate , establish , regulate , vacate ,
alter , improve , maintain , and provide limited - access facilities
for public use wherever such authority or authorities are of
the opinion that traffic conditions , present or future , will
justify such special facilities : Provided , That within cities.
and villages such authority shall be subject to such municipal
consent as may be provided by law . Said highway author-
ities of the State , counties , cities , villages , and towns , in ad-
dition to the specific powers granted in this act , shall also
have and may exercise , relative to limited -access facilities ,
any and all additional authority now or hereafter vested in
them relative to highways or streets within their respective
jurisdictions . Said units may regulate , restrict , or prohibit

SECTION 4. Design of limited - access facility -The
highway authorities of the State , county , city , town , and
village are authorized to so design any limited -access facility
and to so regulate , restrict , or prohibit access as to best serve
the traffic for which such facility is intended ; and its de-
termination of such design shall be final. In this connection
such highway authorities are authorized to divide and separ-
ate any limited -access facility into separate roadways by the
contruction of raised curbings , central dividing sections , or
other physical separations , or by designating such separate
roadways by signs , markers , stripes , and the proper lane for
such traffic by appropriate signs , markers , stripes , and other
devices . No person shall have any right of ingress or egress
to , from or across limited - access facilities to or from abutting
lands , except at such designated points at which access may
be permitted , upon such terms and conditions as may be speci-
fied from time to time .
SECTION 5. Acquisition of property and property
rights- For the purposes of this act , the highway authorities
of the State , county , city , town , or village may acquire private
or public property and property rights for limited -access
facilities and service roads , including rights of access , air ,
view, and light , by gift , devise , purchase , or condemnation in
the same manner as such units are now or hereafter may be
authorized by law to acquire such property or property rights
in connection with highways and streets within their respec-
tive jurisdiction . All property rights acquired under the
provisions of this act shall be in fee simple . In connection
with the acquisition of property or property rights for any
limited -access facility or portion thereof, or service road in
connection therewith, the State , county , city , town , or village
highway authority may, in its discretion , acquire an entire
lot , block , or tract of land , if by so doing , the interests of the
public will be best served , even though said entire lot , block ,
or tract is not immediately needed for the right -of-way
proper .
SECTION 6. Preference of condemnation cases -Court
proceedings necessary to acquire property or property rights
for purposes of this act shall take precedence over all other

ing eliminations-The highway authority of the State , county ,

way or other public way shall be opened into or connected

are authorized to enter into agreements with each other , or
with the Federal Government , respecting the financing , plan-
ning , establishment , improvement , maintenance , use , regula-
tion, or vacation of limited -access facilities or other public
ways in their respective jurisdictions , to facilitate the pur-
poses of this act .
SECTION 9. Local service roads-In connection with

616 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
or street , and to exercise jurisdiction over service roads in
the same manner as is authorized over limited -access facili-
ties under the terms of this act , if in their opinion such local
service roads and streets are necessary or desirable . Such
local service roads or streets shall be of appropriate design ,
and shall be separated from the limited - access facility proper
by means of all devices designated as necessary or desirable
by the proper authority .
SECTION 10. Unlawful use of limited- access facilities ;
penalties —It is unlawful for any person ( 1 ) to drive a vehicle
over, upon, or across any curb , central dividing section or
other separation or dividing line on limited -access facilities ;
( 2 ) to make a left turn or a semicircular or U-turn except
through an opening provided for that purpose in the divid-
ing curb section , separation or line ; ( 3 ) to drive any vehicle
except in the proper lane provided for that purpose and in
the proper direction and to the right of the central dividing
curb , separation section , or line ; ( 4 ) to drive any vehicle into
the limited -access facility from a local service road except
through an opening provided for that purpose in the divid-
ing curb, or dividing section or dividing line which separates
such service road from the limited -access facility proper .
Any person who violates any of the provisions of this section
is guilty of a misdemeanor and upon arrest and conviction
therefor , shall be punished by a fine of not less than five
dollars ( $ 5.00 ) nor more than one hundred dollars ( $ 100.00 ) ,
or by imprisonment in the city or county jail for not less than
5 days nor more than 90 days , or by both such fine and im-
prisonment .
SECTION 11. Severability-If any section , provision ,
or clause of this act shall be declared invalid or inapplicable
to any person or circumstance such invalidity or inapplicabil-
ity shall not be construed to affect the portions not so held or
persons or circumstances not so affected . All laws or por-
tions of laws inconsistent with the policy and provisions of
this act are hereby repealed to the extent of such incon-
sistency in its application to limited -access facilities pro-
vided for in this act .
Passed March 20 , enrolled March 21 , approved March 25 , 1946 .

CHAPTER 226 617
CHAPTER 226
(S. B. 206 )
AN ACT to provide for the construction , maintenance and operation of
flood control systems by all the cities of the Commonwealth .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. "Governing authority " and " Board " as
used in this Act shall mean and include the Board of Council-
men, Board of Aldermen , and Board of Commissioners or
other legislative body of a city . " Commission " shall mean
and include any agency or group of individuals appointed
by the governing authority of a city for the execution and
operation of the provisions of this Act .
Section 2. For the purpose of protecting property with-
in and beyond their limits from floods or high water, cities
of all classes are authorized and empowered to establish a
flood control system by constructing , enlarging , extending ,
equipping , maintaining and operating walls or other barriers
with necessary appurtenances and equipment , either within
or beyond the boundaries of the city .
For that purpose every city may acquire all necessary
and convenient land , rights of way and other property there-
for by gift , purchase or condemnation , and cause the walls
or barriers and their appurtenances and equipment to be
erected , maintained and operated as herein provided . The
cost thereof may be met and paid ( a ) by the issuance of bonds
of the city as voted by the citizens thereof , or ( b) by assess-
ments made upon the property benefited by such construction
for the satisfaction of bonds issued therefor , or ( c) by a com-
bination of both plans as the governing authority may de-
termine . The city is authorized to accept upon such terms
as the governing authority may deem necessary or appro-
priate any gift , contribution or allotment of money or prop-
erty by the government of the United States or any of its
agencies or by any individual or corporation .
Section 3. Before any city shall undertake the construc-
tion , acquisition or improvement of any flood control system ,
its governing authority shall enact appropriate ordinances
which shall (a ) set forth a general or brief description of the
system , its location and the plan and method of establishing
and operating it ; (b) designate the particular method or plan.

classification.title: An Act to Amend Section 133.160 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes, 1944 Edition, Relating to Tax Assessments and Appeals
classification.summary: This act details the procedures for taxpayers to petition the Kentucky Tax Commission for a review of tax assessments, rulings, orders, or findings made by the Department of Revenue. It also specifies that appeals from the Department of Revenue or the Kentucky Tax Commission are generally limited to the procedures outlined in KRS 131.110 and 131.120, and that a refusal to refund a tax can only be reviewed under those conditions. Additionally, it amends KRS 133.160 to require the Kentucky Tax Commission to provide notice to county courts, school districts, affected cities, and taxpayers through the county judge when contemplating raising property valuations in a county, and to fix a time and place for a hearing.

AN ACT providing for the issuance of a license to Samuel C. Reid , of
Danville , Kentucky , to practice veterinary medicine , surgery and
dentistry in the Commonwealth of Kentucky , and authorize the said
Samuel C. Reid to practice veterinary medicine , surgery and den-
tistry in this Commonwealth .
WHEREAS , Dr. Samuel C. Reid , of Danville , Ken-
tucky , has had professional training in the practice of veter-
inary medicine , surgery and dentistry in this Commonwealth
for a period of more than one year prior to July 1 , 1916 ,
upon which date the veterinary licensing law was enacted
in this Commonwealth .
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
That Dr. Samuel C. Reid , of Danville , Kentucky , be
and he is hereby authorized to practice veterinary medicine ,
veterinary surgery and dentistry in Kentucky , and the State
Board of Veterinary Examiners is ordered and directed
to issue to the said Samuel C. Reid , upon payment of the
fee prescribed by KRS 321.040 , a license to practice veter-
inary , medicine , surgery or dentistry , which license shall
confer all the privileges that are conferred by licenses is-
sued by the State Board of Veterinary Examiners .
It is the express intent of the General Assembly to
confer the privilege herein conferred upon Dr. Samuel C.
Reid in consideration of public services rendered by him
to the Commonwealth over a long period of years .
Passed and enrolled March 21 , 1946 , not approved .

CHAPTER 239
(S. B. 233 )
AN ACT relating to special local peace officers appointed by the Gover-
nor and amending Section 61.360 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 61.360 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is hereby amended and as amended reads as fol-
lows :
(1 ) The Governor may appoint special local peace of-
ficers , for such time as he deems necessary , to preserve the
peace and protect the property of any person from waste
or destruction . Upon the application of an owner of property
for such services , and upon payment of a fee of five dol-
lars for each officer to be appointed , the Governor shall
immediately appoint the person recommended by the
owner , if the person is eligible . No person shall be eligible
for appointment under this section unless he has established
to the satisfaction of the Governor that he possesses the
qualifications prescribed for nonelective peace officers by
KRS 61.300 . The Governor may remove any officer so ap-
pointed at will or at the request of the owner of the
property .
( 2 ) The duties of the officer shall be confined to the
premises of the property to be protected , except while in
pursuit of a person fleeing from the property after com-
mitting an act of violence or destruction of the property .
In that case , the officer may pursue the person and make
arrest anywhere within this state . He may wear such badges
and insignia as will plainly indicate to the public that he
is a special local peace officer , but he shall not , in any event ,
wear any uniform , or any part thereof , of any public police
officer ; nor shall he in any way impersonate a public police
officer or represent himself to any person or persons as
being a public police officer ; nor shall he perform any of
the duties of a public police officer , except those specifically
herein granted and at the places herein specifically
designated .
(3 ) When application is made for the appointment of
an officer under this section , the Governor shall require

CHAPTER 241
(S. B. 238)
AN ACT Authorizing the Municipal Corporations of the fourth class to
issue interest bearing warrants payable from the income of Electric
Power and Water Plants .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Municipal corporations of the fourth class in the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky which own and operate municipal
electric power plants or water works are authorized to issue
interest bearing warrants in payment for extensions and
improvements to said electric power plants or water works .
Said warrants shall bear interest at not more than 41 % and
be due not more than five ( 5 ) years from date . They shall
be payable only out of the income from the operation of said
electric power plants or water works .
Passed March 20 , enrolled March 21 , approved March 23 , 1946 .

§ 1. As used in this Act , unless the context otherwise
requires :
(1 ) " Absent voter " means any qualified voter who ex-
pects in the course of his business or duties to be absent from
the county in which is situated his voting precinct on the day
of holding any election .

in Section 7 of this Act. If the County court clerk does not
find the applicant to be so registered , and if the application
does not indicate that the applicant is a member of the Armed
Forces , the clerk shall immediately mail a letter to the ap-
plicant , at the address stated in the application , advising him
that his application cannot be honored because of the fact
that he is not registered , and advising him what period of
time , if any , remains in which he may register prior to such
election , and the clerk shall place the application in a file
entitled " Nonregistered Applicants for Absent Voter's Bal-
lots . " If the clerk thereafter , and prior to the election , re-
ceives notice from the applicant advising the clerk that the
applicant has registered , the clerk shall again examine the
registration books , and if he finds that the applicant has
registered he shall remove the application from the non-
registered applicants ' file and forward a ballot as provided
in Section 7 of this Act . Any applications not so removed
from the nonregistered applicants ' file shall be retained in
such file until thirty days after the election , when they shall
be burned by the clerk in the presence of the judge and sheriff
of the county . If the application of a person found not to be
registered indicates that the applicant is a member of the
Armed Forces , the county court clerk , upon being satisfied
that the applicant is entitled to be registered , shall cause the
applicant to be registered in the registration records of the
proper precinct , and shall mark upon such registration rec-
ords the words , " Absentee Registration " . Such registration

§ 5. The county court clerk shall prepare for each pre-
cinct a list of the names of all voters of that precinct to whom
he has sent absent voters ' ballots . When a ballot is received
from a voter, as hereinafter provided in this Act , that fact
shall be indicated opposite the voter's name on the list . Such
lists shall be delivered by the county court clerk to the pre-
cinct election officers of each precinct at the same time other
election paraphernalia is delivered to them on the morning
of the election , and the precinct election officers shall post the
list in a public place at the voting place where it may be
inspected by any voter . Thelist shall be entitled " List of
Applicants for Absentee Ballots " .
6 The county court clerk, after the ballots for an
election have been printed , shall take the book of ballots and
stubs for each precinct in which there are voters who have
applied for absent voter's ballots and , commencing with the
first ballot in the book , shall write the names and adresses
of such absent voters on the primary stubs of the ballots ,
and across such stubs shall write the words , " Absent Voter . "
In the case of primary elections , the clerk shall use the ballot
books in which are contained the ballots of the political party
with which the particular voter is affiliated . After having
made the entries above required , the county court clerk shall
remove the ballots from the book , with the secondary stubs
attached , and shall send the ballots of each voter to the ad-
dress given by him in his application , the ballot sent to each
voter to correspond with the number on the stub on which
such voter's name is written . There shall be enclosed with
the ballot sent to each voter the two official envelopes for re-
turning the ballot hereinafter provided for, and a copy of
the instructions for absent voters . The ballot , envelopes
and instructions shall be sent postage prepaid , and if the
address of the applicant is outside the continental limits of
the United States the same shall be sent Air Mail .
§ 7. The county court clerk of each county shall cause
to be prepared and printed an appropriate number of outer
envelopes for absent voter's ballots , which shall bear the
words " Absent Voter's Ballot " and shall also bear the
address and official title of the county court clerk.

CHAPTER 242 647
absent voters . The Secretary of State shall furnish to each
County Court Clerk , upon application , a sufficient number
of inner envelopes , application forms , and instructions .
§ 10. When the absent voter receives the ballot and the
two envelopes , he shall go before some civil officer authorized
by law to administer an oath and before him make and sub-
scribe to the affidavit on the face of the envelope and shall ,
in the presence of such officer and no one else , mark the ballot
so as to indicate the way he desires to vote , but in such
manner as to prevent the officer from knowing how the
ballot is marked or how he has voted . He shall then , and
while in the presence of the officer , fold the ballot in such
way as to conceal the markings made thereon by him, and
after the ballot has been so folded , he shall enclose it in
the inner envelope ; then seal said envelope and then enclose
it in the outer envelope ; then seal and write his name across
the face of the outer envelope , and at once mail it to the
county court clerk.
Section 11. Before sending an absent voter's ballot to
any applicant prior to any election , the county court clerk
shall provide himself with one or more legal ballot boxes , as
may be required . The boxes shall be locked in the presence
of the County Board of Election Commissioners , and one of
keys shall be delivered to each member of the County Board
of Election Commissioners . The boxes shall remain locked ,
and the members of the County Board of Election Com-
missioners shall retain the keys in their possession , until
the boxes have been delivered to the County Board of Election
Commissioners following the election and opened as pro-
vided in Section 14 of this Act .
Section 12. ( 1 ) When the county court clerk receives an
absent voter's ballot, he shall immediately stamp on the
outer envelope the day and hour he received the same . All
such ballots received by him prior to the hour for closing
the polls on the day of the election shall be deposited in the
ballot box provided for in Section 11 of this Act , without
opening the outer envelope . All ballots received by the clerk
after the hour for closing the polls on the election day shall
be kept by the clerk unopened until thirty days after the
election , when they shall be burned by the clerk in the pres-
ence of the county judge and sheriff , without being opened .

§ 16. The provisions of this Act shall not be construed
to prohibit any absent voter, who has returned to his place
of residence , from voting in person within his precinct at any
election notwithstanding that he may have made application
for an absent voter's ballot and such ballot has been mailed
to him, if such voter has not voted the ballot and mailed it to
the county court clerk . In such case the voter shall return
the ballot not voted to the county court clerk from whom he
received it, and the county court clerk shall mark the ballot
"cancelled " and preserve it for the same period as other ab-
sent voter's documents are to be preserved . The clerk shall
also enter the word " cancelled " upon the ballot stub in the
ballot book , and remove such voter's name from the list pro-
vided for in Section 5 of this Act .
Section 17. When the election paraphernalia is de-
livered to the precinct officers of each precinct , they shall ex-
amine the list of applicants for absentee ballots , and shall
also examine the stubs in the ballot book for the ballots which
have been removed and mailed to the applicants for absentee
ballots , for the purpose of ascertaining the persons to whom
absent voter's ballots have been sent . If any person so in-
dicated as having received an absent voter's ballot appears
and makes application to vote in person , he shall not be is-
sued a ballot by the precinct officers .
Section 18. Any oath or affirmation required by this Act
to be taken before a civil officer may , in the case of a person
serving in or attached to the Armed Forces of the United
States , be taken before any commissioned officer of the Armed
Forces . Any officer before whom the same is taken shall sub-
scribe his name and official designation .
§ 181. The death of any absent voter prior to the day in
which the election is held shall constitute grounds for
challenging his ballot before it is deposited by the election of-
ficers , and shall constitute grounds for not counting such
ballot in any contest proceeding .
19. The cost of postage and envelopes required to be
used by the county court clerk in carrying out the provisions
of this Act , including the cost of printing the outer envelopes ,
shall be paid by the county .

CHAPTER 242 651
§ 20. Jurisdiction of any prosecution for a violation of
any provision of Sections 1 to 19 of this Act , whenever the
violation occurred , whether within or without this state , is
hereby vested in the circuit court of the county of the voter's
residence .
§ 21. ( 1 ) Any person who , for the purpose of voting
under the provisions of Sections 1 to 19 of this Act , swears to
or affirms an application for an absent voter's ballot , or
swears to or affirms the oath on the inner envelope for an
absent voter's ballot , knowing that the same contains any
false statement , shall be confined in the penitentiary for not
less than one nor more than five years .
(2 ) Any county court clerk or election officer who will-
fully refuses or fails to perform any of the duties required
of him by Sections 1 to 19 of this Act , or who in any manner
willfully violates or abuses any trust or duty imposed on him
by those sections , shall be fined not less than one hundred
dollars nor more than one thousand dollars .
( 3 ) Any person who , by threat , bribery , corrupt or
arbitrary measure , offer or payments of reward, or by other
unlawful means , interferes with any qualified voter in the
free exercise of his right to vote under the provisions of Sec-
tions 1 to 19 of this Act , shall be fined not less than one hun-
dred dollars nor more than one thousand dollars .
22. For the purpose of carrying out the duties im-
posed on the Secretary of State by Sections 1 to 19 of this
Act , there is hereby appropriated to the Secretary of State ,
out of the General Expenditure Fund in the State Treasury ,
for each of the fiscal years 1946-1947 and 1947-1948 , the sum
of $2,000 .
§ 23. Section 119.080 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
of 1944 is amended to read as follows :
Candidates for offices to be voted for by the electors of
one county or of a district less than one county , except mem-
bers of Congress , shall file their nomination papers with the
county clerk of the county at least forty -five days before the
primary election . Candidates for offices to be voted for by
the electors of more than one county , and for members of
Congress , shall file their nomination papers with the Secre-
tary of State at least fifty -five days before the primary
election .

CHAPTER 243
( S. B. 243 )
AN ACT to amend Section 338.050 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes ,
relating to unsafe working conditions .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 338.050 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
(1 ) When any employe or his representative complains
that any place of employment , equipment or practice is un-
safe , he or his representative shall file with the commission
a statement setting forth the facts upon which he relies for
his contention , and transmit a copy of the complaint and
statement to his employer . Upon such complaint the com-
missioner or his authorized representative shall immediately
investigate and undertake to have corrected any dangerous
condition he finds . If the commissioner or his representa-
tive is unable to effect an adjustment he shall file the com-
plaint , statements and findings with the board for hearing .
(2 ) The commissioner may , upon his own motion and
without complaint having been filed as provided in sub-
section ( 1 ) of this section , file a complaint with the board
with respect to any unsafe place of employment , equipment
or practice that has come to his attention or to the attention
of a representative of the department and which, after the
commissioner or representative has undertaken to have it ad-
justed , has not been corrected or removed .
( 3) The board shall give at least ten days ' notice to the
employer of the hearing on any complaint filed with the

classification.title: AN ACT to amend Section 338.050 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes, relating to unsafe working conditions.
classification.summary: This act amends Section 338.050 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes concerning unsafe working conditions. It outlines the process for employees or their representatives to file complaints about unsafe employment conditions, equipment, or practices with the commission. The commissioner or their representative is required to investigate and attempt to correct any dangerous conditions found. If an adjustment cannot be made, the complaint, statements, and findings are to be filed with the board for a hearing. The commissioner can also initiate complaints on their own motion if unsafe conditions are not corrected after an attempted adjustment.

CHAPTER 244 657
board , and , in the case of complaints made under subsection
(1 ) of this section , shall give like notice to the employe or
his representative . If , after hearing the testimony and in-
spection of the premises or equipment where it considers
such inspection necessary , the board determines that such
place , equipment or practice is unsafe , it shall issue an order
to that effect and shall post conspicuously on the premises
or equipment complained of a notice to that effect . This
notice shall be removed only by an authorized representative
of the department after the board has determined that the
premises or equipment are no longer unsafe .
(4) After the determination by the board that the
premises or equipment are unsafe , and constitute a serious
menace to the life or safety of the persons about it, the com-
missioner may apply to the circuit court of the county in
which they are located for an injunction restraining their
use or operation until the dangerous condition is corrected .
(5) The application to the circuit court shall be accom-
panied by the certified order of the board and an affidavit
showing the facts upon which the board relied for its de-
termination . Upon such a presentation , the court may issue a
temporary restraining order pending final hearing and ap-
plication for injunction .
(6) No person, after the notice provided for in subsec-
tion ( 3 ) is attached to the premises or equipment , shall use
any such place , equipment or practice before it is made safe
and the required safeguards or safety appliances are pro-
vided . No person shall deface , destroy or remove any such
notice , without the authority from the commissioner or order
of the court .
Passed and enrolled March 21 , approved March 23 , 1946 .

SECTION 5. ( 1 ) Except as otherwise specified in this
section , no adoption shall be permitted except upon the
notarized consent of the living parent or parents of the child ,
or of the mother of an illegitimate child , but , if the court finds

made for the parent or parents over a period of time not less
than six ( 6 ) months from the date of the initial hearing. Con-
sent cannot be given by the next of kin , guardian or person
standing in loco parentis or by the Department , charitable
institution or association unless it is determined by the Court
that such next of kin , guardian , Department, charitable in-
stitution or association or person standing in loco parentis
has the legal right to give such consent . If the court finally
determines that the child has no living parent , next of kin ,
statutory guardian or person standing in loco parentis as de-
fined in this Act, consent must be given by the Department
before the granting of the petition .
(2) If the court determines that the child is of
illegitimate birth notwithstanding that the mother was mar-
ried at the time of its conception or birth , it is not necessary
to obtain the consent of the man to whom said mother was
married at the time of said child's conception or birth nor
make him a party defendant .

(7) The consent of a parent shall not be required if

such judgment shall have been in effect for not less than one
(1 ) year prior to the filing of the petition for adoption and

guardian ad litem for such parent who has been adjudged
incompetent by reason of mental disability and such guardian
ad litem after making a full investigation of the facts per-
taining to the adoption files a written report recommending
that such adoption be made . In the event that such incom-
petent parent shall have a committee , then such committee
shall perform the acts herein before required of the guardian
ad litem and the guardian ad litem shall not be required to
be appointed , but such committee shall be made a party de-
fendant . However , in such cases adoption shall be permitted
only on the consent of the Department .
(8) Where a parent is not made a defendant by the pro-
visions of this Act , a guardian ad litem shall be appointed to
represent the infant to be adopted and said guardian ad litem
shall file a written report with his recommendations .

required consent the Court adjudges that the facts stated in
the petition are established , and that all legal requirements
relating to adoption have been complied with , it shall enter
an order setting forth all jurisdictional facts and adjudging
that from and after the date thereof the child shall be deemed
the child of the petitioners , for the purposes set forth in
Section 9 .
( 3 ) In the judgment the name of the child may be
changed to conform with the prayer of the petitioners .
( 4 ) If for any reason whatsoever a petition for adoption
of a minor child be dismissed or the prayer thereof be denied ,
the following disposition shall be made of the child :
(a ) If the child is , or was prior to the filing of the
petition , in the care , custody and control of the Department
or any charitable institution or association , then the child
shall be returned to the custody of the Department or such
institution or association .
(5 ) Otherwise the Court shall certify the case to the
Juvenile Division of the County Court of the County where
the adoption proceeding is pending for appropriate action
and disposition . Said County Court shall advise the Depart-
ment of the pendency of such action .
SECTION 8. Any party to any adoption proceeding
who is aggrieved by any final order made therein shall have
the same right of appeal to the Court of Appeals of Kentucky
as in other equity action .
The appeal of any minor party to such action may be

CHAPTER 245 665
taken in his behalf by a next friend or by any guardian as
such minor party may have .
SECTION 9. Any child adopted according to this
Chapter shall be considered , for purposes of inheritance and
succession and for all other legal considerations , the natural ,
legitimate child of the parents adopting it . A child adopted
under this Chapter shall be freed from all legal obligations
of maintenance and obedience to its natural parents , except
that where the adopting parent of the child is married to the
natural parent of the child , the relation of the child to that
natural parent shall not in any way be altered by the adop-
tion and the rights and obligations of the natural parent and
the adopting parent to the child shall be the same as if the
child were the natural child of both the natural parent and
the adopting parent . Nothing herein shall be so construed
as to prevent the adopted child from inheriting under the
general law in regard to the descent and distribution from
its natural parents.
SECTION 10. Any person or parent instrumental in
placing, or who assists , aids or abets in placing a child for
adoption in a home other than its own without first notify-
ing the Department in writing and having its advice and con-
sent shall be guilty of contributing to the neglect of such
child as that offense is defined by the Kentucky Revised
Statutes , 1944 Edition , Chapter 199 , and punished as therein
provided .
SECTION 11. ( 1 ) No person , corporation or associa-
tion shall advertise in any manner that it will receive children
for the purpose of adoption nor shall any newspaper pub-
lished in the Commonwealth of Kentucky nor any other pub-
lication which is prepared , sold or distributed in the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky contain an advertisement which
solicits children for adoption or solicits the custody of
children .
( 2) An violation of this Section shall be punishable by
fine of not less than Ten Dollars ( $ 10.00 ) nor more than One
Hundred Dollars ( $ 100.00 ) , or by confinement in the County
Jail for a perod of not less than thirty ( 30 ) days nor more
than twelve ( 12 ) months , or by both such fine and imprison-
ment .

CHAPTER 247 667
Revenue a bond with approved surety in an amount of not less

Passed March 20 , enrolled March 21 , 1946 , not approved .
STATEMENT BY THE GOVERNOR
RE : SENATE BILL NO . 250
I have permitted this Bill to pass without approval or disapproval .
It provides for a refund of taxes paid on motor fuel used in the
operation of aircraft .
There is obvious merit in the principle that taxes on aviation gas
should be devoted to aviation uses . But the amendment to the Con-
stitution requires that all taxes on gasoline and other motor fuels shall
be devoted exclusively to Public Highway uses and shall not be diverted
to any other purpose .
This whole subject of tax refunds is of doubtful validity and will
no doubt be settled in the near future . The Constitution , Section 52 ,
provides that the legislature shall have no power to release , repay or
otherwise return taxes accrued , and Section 180 prevents diversion of
tax money from the purpose for which the tax was levied . The Court
of Appeals has held that taxes lawfully levied and collected cannot be
repaid or diverted to any other use except that for which it was levied .
In view of these considerations , I have concluded to let the Act
take its course so the question can be finally settled .
Simeon Willis
Governor
Frankfort, Kentucky
March 26 , 1946

CHAPTER 248
(S. B. 263 )
AN ACT to amend Section 21.160 of Kentucky Revised Statutes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
That Section 21.160 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
" The Court of Appeals may employ and pay clerical as-
sistants for each Judge and Commissioner of the Court of
Appeals . Their salary shall be paid monthly out of the
treasury upon the warrant of the Department of Finance ,
which shall be issued upon certificate of the Chief Justice .
The assistants shall be subject to removal at the pleasure of
the court and shall not practice law or accept any employ-
ment or render any services as attorney in the Court of
Appeals ."
Passed and enrolled March 21 , approved March 23 , 1946 .

of this Resolution .

tucky may investigate the claim against the Commonwealth
of Kentucky herein and settle said claim before or after suit
is filed , if he deems it advisable . Any amount recovered in
said suit or by settlement with the Attorney - General shall be
paid out of the General Fund upon warrant drawn by the
Commissioner of Finance upon the State Treasurer .
In the event suit is filed and judgment rendered by the
Circuit Court , either party may appeal to the Court of
Appeals of Kentucky .
No statement in this resolution shall be considered by
either the court or the jury as an admission of negligence
upon the part of the Commonwealth and this resolution shall
not be introduced as evidence , nor commented upon by coun-
sel in the argument of the case to the jury .
The Committee recommends that all judgments rendered
in accordance with the foregoing resolution be paid from the
highway fund .
Passed March 7 , enrolled March 8 , approved March 14 , 1946 .

Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Common-

That said Samuel Peters is authorized to sue the Com-

damages resulting from injuries caused by the loss of serv-
ices of his son , Bruce Peters , a minor , and costs of doctor's
bills , nurses ' bills , nursing , medicines and hospitals in at-
tempting to effect a cure of his said son .
Said injuries were caused by the wrongful act of negli-
gence on the part of an employee , agents and officers of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , while said employee ( Bruce
Peters ) was in the line of duty as an employee of this Com-
monwealth .
Said suit shall be for a sum not in excess of Two Thou-
sand Dollars ( $2,000 .) . The Attorney General of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky may investigate the claim ( or
claims ) against the Commonwealth herein and settle said
claim ( or claims ) before or after suit is filed , if he deems it
advisable . Any amount recovered in said suit or by settle-
ment with the Attorney General shall be paid out of the
General Fund upon warrant drawn by the Commissioner of
Finance upon the State Treasurer .
In the event suit is filed and judgment rendered by the
circuit court either party may appeal to the Court of Appeals
of Kentucky . No statement in this resolution shall be con-
sidered by either the Court or jury as an admission of negli-
gence upon the part of the Commonwealth and this resolu-
tion shall not be introduced as evidence , nor commented upon
by counsel in argument to the jury .
Passed March 13 , enrolled March 14 , approved March 23 , 1946 .

688 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Gumm were such as to impose a moral obligation upon the Com-
monwealth for damages for wrongful death ; and
WHEREAS , Mrs. Mary Katherine Gumm , as the widow of the said
L. R. Gumm , suffered loss and damages by reason of such death
of her husband ,
NOW THEREFORE ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
That Mrs. Mary Katherine Gumm is authorized to sue
the Commonwealth of Kentucky in the Lyon County Circuit
Court for damages for the wrongful death of her husband ,
L. R. Gumm , arising out of and in the course of his employ-
ment as an employe of the Commonwealth , for which dam-
ages the Commonwealth is morally responsible . Said suit
shall be for a sum not in excess of $ 5,000 .
The Attorney General may investigate the claim and
may settle said claim before or after suit is filed , if he deems
it advisable . Any amount recovered in said suit or by settle-
ment with the Attorney General shall be paid out of the
General Expenditure Fund in the State Treasury upon war-
rant drawn by the Commissioner of Finance upon the State
Treasurer .
In the event suit is filed and judgment rendered by the
circuit court , either party may appeal to the Court of Appeals
of Kentucky .
The General Assembly hereby expressly recognizes the
moral responsibility of the Commonwealth for damages for
the death of said L. R. Gumm , and in any suit that may be
filed under authority of this resolution the jury shall have
authority only to fix the amount of such damages .
Passed March 7 , enrolled March 8 , 1946 , not approved .

A RESOLUTION authorizing Mrs. Elizabeth McDonald , personal repre-

WHEREAS , on or about the 8th day of December , 1936 , George Mc-
Donald , a resident of Garrison , Lewis County , Kentucky , received
injuries when in a collision of two motor vehicles on a state high-
way (No. 10 ) , near Quincy , Kentucky , from which injuries he then
and there died ; and
WHEREAS , it is claimed that said accident resulting in said injuries
and death was caused by embankments of dirt and stone which had
been thrown up on the highway and which had frozen , it being
contended that said embankments of dirt and stone restricted the
usual known usable width of said highway to such an extent as to
leave insufficient room for passage of the vehicles herein mentioned ,
thereby causing said accident ; and it being further contended that
the condition of the highway was caused and created by the throw-
ing up of these embankments of dirt and stone on the highway and
in the bed of the highway and allowing them to remain there and
freeze , by the agents and employes of the Department of Highways
and/or the Commonwealth of Kentucky , and since there were no
warning signs or other indications of the defective condition of the
highway , which it is contended the Commonwealth of Kentucky
and/or the Department of Highways , by and through its agents
and employes knew , or in the exercise of ordinary care should have
known of the existence of the said defective condition of the high-
way , which is contended to be the proximate cause of the death
of George McDonald ; therefore ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Sec . 1. That Elizabeth McDonald , of Garrison , Lewis
County, Kentucky , personal representative of George Mc-
Donald , deceased , be , and she hereby is , authorized , em-
powered and permitted to file a suit or suits against the
Commonwealth of Kentucky and/or Department of High-
ways of Kentucky , or either of them , for the purpose of

CHAPTER 280
(H. R. 101 )
A JOINT RESOLUTION authorizing the personal representative of the
estate of John Robert Gray to sue the Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
That the personal representative of the estate of John
Robert Gray is authorized to sue the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky in the Fayette Circuit Court for damages resulting
from the death of John Robert Gray , caused by alleged
negligence on the part of the employees or members of the
Kentucky Active Militia of the Commonwealth of Kentucky
while said employees or members were in the line of duty
as employees or members of the Kentucky Active Militia
of this Commonwealth .
The Attorney General of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky may investigate the claim against the Commonwealth
herein and settle said claim before or after suit is filed , if
he deems it advisable . Any amount recovered in said said
suit or by settlement with the Attorney General shall be
paid out of the General Fund upon warrant drawn by the
Commissioner of Finance upon the State Treasurer .
In event suit is filed and judgment rendered by the
Circuit Court , either party may appeal to the Court of
Appeals of Kentucky .
No statement in this resolution shall be considered by
either the Court or jury as an admission of negligence upon
the part of the Commonwealth and this resolution shall not
be introduced as evidence , nor commented upon by counsel
in argument to the jury.
Passed and enrolled March 14 , approved March 23 , 1946 .

paying claims of Ruby Watts , Sheriff of Knott County , for ex-

directing the payment of the amount appropriated to the said
Ruby Watts .
WHEREAS , Ruby Watts , Sheriff of Knott County , incurred expenses in
the amount of $ 250.47 in returning to Knott County , Kentucky ,
certain fugitives from justice , which claims have not been paid
because the fugitives were not convicted after being returned ,
but such expenses are expenses to which the said Ruby Watts is
justly entitled to be reimbursed .
NOW, THEREFORE ,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
THAT there is hereby appropriated out of the General
Expenditure Fund in the State Treasury the sum of $ 250.47
for the purpose of paying the claims of Ruby Watts , Sheriff
of Knott County , for expenses incurred in returning certain
fugitives from justice , and the Commissioner of Finance is
directed to draw his warrant upon the State Treasurer in
favor of the said Ruby Watts for the amount so appropriated .
Passed and enrolled March 21 , 1946 , not approved .

WHEREAS , on May 9 , 1942 , and for a number of years prior thereto , the
State Highway Patrol of the Commonwealth of Kentucky was en-
gaged , in conformity to Kentucky Statutes , in patrolling the high-
ways of the Commonwealth and enforcing the motor vehicle laws ,
and
WHEREAS , the State Highway Patrol was greatly understaffed due to
many of its personnel having entered the various branches of the
armed forces or having taken positions in private industry and
capable replacements being unavailable , and
WHEREAS , from time to time , in case of emergency, members of the
Patrol , in order to effect arrests and to properly enforce the laws ,
have found it necessary to deputize or call upon private citizens
for aid , and
WHEREAS , Walter Griffey , a former inmate of the Kentucky State
Reformatory who had served a long term on a charge of man-
slaughter or murder , on May 9 , 1942 , in Rowan County , Kentucky ,
was speeding and racing his automobile over the highways , in
violation of the Kentucky Statutes , and ran into an automobile
which was parked , and

WHEREAS , said offense was observed by a member of the State High-

Walter Griffey undertook to escape from the scene of the accident
and crime by racing his car eastwardly out of Morehead into Rowan
County , Kentucky , and
WHEREAS , the State Highway Patrolman deemed it necessary that
Griffey be pursued and that he be arrested , and realizing that as-
sistance was necessary in pursuing Griffey and making said arrest ,
he requested Les Ward , a citizen and resident of Morehead , Ken-
tucky , to act as his deputy and to aid him in overtaking and arrest-
ing Griffey, and
WHEREAS , Ward agreed to aid the State Highway Patrolman and to
act as his deputy and entered the State Highway Patrol car and
for more than three miles they pursued Griffey , overtaking his
car at a point on Highway No. 32 in Rowan County near the State
Highway Garage about three miles from the city limits of More-
head , Kentucky , and
WHEREAS , as Les Ward got out of the State Highway Patrol car and
ordered Griffey to leave his car and advised him that he was under
arrest , and simultaneously Griffey drew a gun and shot Ward , im-
mediately killing him , and at the same time turned his gun upon
the State Highway Patrolman shooting him twice through the left
arm and once through the left chest , barely missing the heart , and
the State Highway Patrolman then fired several shots into the body
of Griffey who died shortly thereafter , and
WHEREAS , Les Ward at the time of his death was forty -six years of
age and in good physical condition , earning in excess of $ 100.00 per
month , and left surviving him his widow , Mrs. Roah Ward , and
eight children , all of whom are very young and the oldest under
the age of sixteen years , and
WHEREAS , Mrs. Ward , during the years of her marriage to the de-
ceased , Les Ward , devoted all of her time and activities to man-
aging their household and rearing their children , and consequently
is untrained in any business pursuit and is incapable of engaging in
any type of work that will provide sufficient income for the sup-
port of her eight children and herself , and
WHEREAS , Les Ward was unable to accumulate any savings or estate
during his lifetime and upon his death left nothing other than
sufficient money with which to pay his funeral expenses , and
WHEREAS , a valuable public service was being rendered by Les Ward
in aiding and assisting the Highway Patrolman in the apprehension
of a fugitive from justice , and but for such public service Les Ward
would not have met his death in the manner aforesaid and could
have continued rearing and supporting his children and providing
adequate maintenance for his wife and none of them would now be
destitute , and
WHEREAS , it is deemed necessary that all citizens who are called upon
by State officials to render a public service may know that in case

CHAPTER 292
(S. R. 16 )
A JOINT RESOLUTION authorizing J. H. Webb , of Pikeville , Ky., to
sue the Commonwealth of Kentucky .

wealth of Kentucky :
That J. H. Webb, of Pikeville , Ky . , is authorized to sue
the Commonwealth of Kentucky , in the Pike Circuit Court ,
for damages resulting from personal injuries and medical
expenses , caused by alleged negligence on the part of em-
ployees of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , while said em-
ployees were in the line of duty as agents of the Common-
wealth . Said suit shall be for a sum not in excess of ten
thousand dollars .
The Attorney General of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky , may investigate the claim herein and settle same before
or after suit is filed , if he deems it advisable .
Any amount recovered in said suit or by settlement with
the Attorney General shall be paid out of the Road Fund
upon warrant drawn by the Commissioner of Finance upon
the State Treasurer .
In the event suit is filed and judgment rendered by the
Circuit Court either party may appeal to the Court of Ap-
peals of Kentucky .
No statement in this resolution shall be considered by
either the court or the jury as an admission of negligence
upon the part of the Commonwealth , and his resolution shall
not be introduced as evidence for either party nor commented
upon by counsel in argument to the jury.
Passed March 20 , enrolled March 21 , approved March 23 , 1946 .

CHAPTER 302
(S. R. 30)
A RESOLUTION appropriating from the General Fund of the Common-
wealth the sum of $ 2,000.00 for the purpose of restoring the Daniel
Boone Monument in the Frankfort Cemetetry (Incorporated ) at
Frankfort, Kentucky .
Whereas , Pursuant to an act of the Legislature of Kentucky , the remains
of Daniel Boone and his wife , Rebecca , were on September 13 ,
1845 , re -interred in the cemetery of the Frankfort Cemetery ,
(Incorporated ) at Frankfort, Kentucky , in a lot consisting of about
one -quarter of an acre which had prior thereto been dedicated by
said corporation to the Commonwealth of the Kentucky for said
purpose, and of which the Commonwealth of Kentucky is now owner ,
and
Whereas the Commonwealth of Kentucky did in 1860 erect a monument
upon said said lot depicting various phases of the life of Daniel
Boone , on four Italian marble panels set in Kentucky River marble ,
and
Whereas in 1906 , due to defacement by vandals of said marble panels ,
same were restored under an act of the Legislature of Kentucky , and
Whereas on June 14 , 1945 , during a violent wind storm , a large tree
was blown down and upon said monument , causing it to be practi-
cally destroyed , with the exception of said marble panels which
were salvage intact and without injury , and
Whereas it is desired that said monument be restored to its original
condition , or as nearly thereto as is possible, through the utilization
of said panels together with such other materials as will accomplish
same, and
Whereas the Frankfort Cemetery (Incorporated ) has expresed its willing-
ness to procure proposals for, and supervise the restoration of said
monument , subject to the advice and approval of the representa-
tive , or representatives of the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
( 1 ) There is hereby appropriated from the General

Circuit Court , either party may appeal to the Court of
Appeals of Kentucky .

be introduced as evidence nor commented upon by counsel
in argument to the jury .

720 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 309
(S. R. 43 )
A JOINT RESOLUTION repudiating , retracting and withdrawing House
Resolution No. 79 of the Regular Session of the 1944 General
Assembly .
WHEREAS , by House Resolution No. 79 of the Regular Session of the
1944 General Assembly , application was made to the Congress of the
United States to call a convention for the purpose of proposing an
amendment to the Constitution of the United States relating to taxes
on income, inheritance and gifts ; and
WHEREAS , such resolution was adopted by the General Assembly
under a misapprehension as to its true meaning , intent and purpose ,
and without a full consideration of the results that might obtain
from such action , and
WHEREAS , the amendment proposed in such resolution establishes a
policy with regard to taxation that is contrary to the established
public policy of Kentucky , and will impose the burden of taxation
upon those least able to bear it ,
NOW, THEREFORE
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
THAT House Resolution No. 79 of the Regular Session
of the 1944 General Assembly of Kentucky is hereby re-
pudiated and retracted , and the General Assembly hereby
withdraws the same .
The Secretary of State is directed to send a duly cer-
tified copy of this resolution to the Senate of the United
States and to the House of Representatives in Congress of
the United States .
Passed and enrolled March 21 , 1946 , not approved .

WHEREAS , W. Henry Grimes was duly elected Tax Commissioner of
Warren County for the years 1930 to 1934 inclusive , and
WHEREAS , W. Henry Grimes was duly elected Tax Commissioner of

a period of four years or until December 31 , 1934 , and
WHEREAS , during the said years of 1930 to 1934 inclusive , the said W.
Henry Grimes as Tax Commissioner of Warren County , did not re-
ceive the minimum net salary of $4000.00 per annum for rendering
the services required of him under the law , and
WHEREAS , the Attorney General of the Commonwealth of Kentucky
ruled that said Tax Commissioner of Warren County together with
other tax commissioners , could not receive full salary on his com-
mission as provided by the statutes and laws then in full force and
effect, and
WHEREAS , by reason of said opinion there was deducted from the com-
mission due from the Commonwealth of Kentucky to said Henry W.
Grimes the sum of $300.00 per annum , and
WHEREAS , the Attorney General of the Commonwealth of Kentucky
thereafter ruled that said tax commissioners , as such officers , were
entitled to a minimum of $ 4000.00 per annum , and
WHEREAS , thereafter the Commissioner of Revenue of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky in agreement with the interpretation of the
Statute as contained in the opinion of the Attorney General of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky in the year 1940 , paid to the said tax
commissioners the sums provided by statute , and
WHEREAS , in order to pay some of the tax commissioners for former
years a resolution was passed by the General Assembly at its 1942
session , being Chapter 212 of the Printed Acts of said session and
Senate Resolution No. 13 , authorizing the payment of the sums due
them for the years 1936 to 1940 inclusive , and
WHEREAS , there was not included in said resolution tax commissioners
who served in former years , and

CHAPTER 314

A JOINT RESOLUTION making an appropriation to the committee for
Lizzie Jenkins .
WHEREAS , by mistake and oversight , an inquest was not held at the
January, 1944 term of the Magoffin Circuit Court , so to as to entitle
the committee of Lizzie Jenkins , a pauper idiot , to receive compensa-
tion for the care of said pauper idiot , she having been so provided
for at public expense for a number of years, and
WHEREAS , aforesaid mistake and oversight was not discovered until
the January , 1946 term of circuit court , so late as to deprive the com-
mittee from the regular annual allowance for the years 1944 and
1945 , and
WHEREAS , the Commonwealth of Kentucky has a definite moral obliga-
tion to reimburse said committee for care of this pauper idiot ,
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
THAT Lizzie Jenkins , a pauper idiot , and Arminta
Jenkins , as committee for said Lizzie Jenkins , be paid the
sum of $ 75.00 , as the state's portion for the care of said
pauper idiot for the years 1944 and 1945 under KRS 203.250
to 203.280 , the provisions of KRS 203.260 notwithstanding .
Passed and enrolled March 21 , 1946 , not approved .
